vehicle tax notification or
registration document
from the last 12 months
A valid
photo driving licence
showing your address
A
tenancy agreement
for the current year
A letter or document from a government or financial institution issued within the last 12 months
Any other government or financial institution-issued document
Good to know:
If you live with family or friends and don't have a document in your name, you can provide a document in their name along with a government-issued document confirming your relationship (like a birth certificate).
For students, a
university acceptance letter
confirming your on-campus address, issued within the last 3 months.
Troubleshooting verification
Sometimes, we might have trouble verifying your documents. Here are some common issues and how to solve them.
What if my document is rejected?
We'll always email you to let you know why a document was rejected. It's usually for one of these reasons:
The photo is blurry or unclear.
Make sure you're in a well-lit room and that all four corners of the document are visible. Don't cover any part of the document.
The document is not supported.
We can only accept the document types listed above.
The details don't match.
Please ensure the name and date of birth on your document match your Wise account details.
The document has expired.
Please check that your document is still valid before you upload it.
I'm having technical trouble uploading my document
If you're having issues with the upload, try these steps:
Use a different browser (Google Chrome often works best).
Clear your browser's cache and cookies.
If you're using the website and it asks you to use your phone, click
I don't have a phone
to use your computer's webcam instead.
Find more troubleshooting steps here
Common questions
Question
Answer
How long does verification take?
Once we receive your documents, we'll review them and get back to you by email. This usually takes 1–2 working days.
Why do I need to get verified?
When you open a bank account, you usually need to take some ID into the bank branch, and Wise is no different. We’re a financial institution, so we need to know who’s using our service. Depending on where you’re sending money from, and how much you send, we’ll ask for you to verify your identity.
What if my document isn't from the country I live in?
That's usually fine. We can accept identity documents (like a passport or national ID card) issued by a country different from your country of residence. In some cases, we might also need to see a separate document that proves you have the right to live in your current country, like a visa or residence permit.
Learn about how we verify documents from the country you reside in
.
What is a selfie with ID verification?
For some transfers, we may ask you to take a picture of your face alongside your ID document. This is a security step to make sure that it's really you who is making the transfer, and helps us prevent fraud.
Find out more about selfie verification
.
Why are you asking me to get verified again?
To keep your account secure, we need to make sure we have up-to-date information about you. We may ask you to re-verify from time to time to comply with financial regulations. If we need you to do this, we'll send you an email with instructions.

title: How does Wise verify my identity?
content:
Like all financial institutions, we verify your identity. This is a legal requirement that helps us combat financial crime and keep everyone's money safe.
We take your privacy and data security seriously. The documents and information we collect are only used for identity verification and fraud prevention, as required by global financial regulators. We don’t sell your personal data.
Read about our Privacy Policy
.
What documents do you accept?
We typically accept one of the following documents to verify your identity. The document must be a clear photo of the original.
Document Type
General Requirements
Exceptions
Passport
Photo page only
National ID Card
Plastic card format, showing both sides
US Military IDs
aren’t accepted.
Hungary:
Only the card ID format is accepted (not the old booklet ID).
Photo Driver's Licence
Photo licence (Electronic Driving Licences aren’t accepted)
EU/EEA:
We can’t accept a driving licence if you live in Europe.
Exceptions for specific countries or currencies
You may need to provide additional information or different document types depending on your scenario. Refer to the table for more information.
Scenario
Document Requirement
Brazilian-issued ID Card
It must have been issued within the past 15 years. We need to see the front and back.
Japan
We will need a My Number Card, or another Japanese ID and a My Number document.
Learn more
Sending JPY from outside of Japan
We will need a valid ID and a
proof of address document
Sending NZD (New Zealand)
If you live in New Zealand and send from NZD, we need to verify your identity and address. We don't accept Kiwi Access Card as an identity document.
Address in Spain
If you are a Spanish citizen we can only accept national ID card. For EEA citizens we accept passport and national ID. For non-EEA citizens we accept passport and residence permit (TIE card).
Refugees and Displaced Persons
We accept documents specifically issued for identity verification if you are a refugee or displaced person.
You can learn more about the acceptable documents here.
Russian-issued ID Card
We can’t accept identity documents issued by the Russian government for new customers, or to re-verify an existing profile. If you have an address outside of Russia, you can provide other documents to get verified.
Learn more about acceptable documents to verify your identity here.
Other documents and verification scenarios
We have special procedures for name and date of birth verification. If you have a name mismatch or issue with your Date of Birth on your documents, please
contact us
for help.
What makes an identity document acceptable?
To prevent rejections and delays, ensure your document meets all of these basic quality requirements:
Full Name:
Your full first name and at least one full family name must be visible.
If your ID (e.g. Indonesian, Malaysian) only shows a full name without separate first/last fields, we can accept it as long as it has at least 2 words.
Photo:
The photo of the ID holder must be clear, not blurry, and not manipulated.
Date of Birth:
Your date of birth must be visible.
Validity:
The document must not be expired.
Clarity:
All information must be visible and legible—nothing can be hidden or redacted.
Unique Numbers:
The unique document/identifier number and the MRZ code (Machine-Readable Zone, found on passports and newer ID cards) must be visible.
Full Document:
For most plastic cards (National ID, Driving Licence), we need to see
both the front and back
of the document.
When will Wise ask me for verification?
When and how we verify you depends on several factors.
Learn more about how verification works
.
How do I check my verification status?
You can check your status directly in your Wise account. We use two main banner types to let you know what's happening.
Neutral status (light grey banner)
Critical status (red banner)
What it means:
Verification is pending, or we're reviewing your documents.
Action:
No action is required from you at this time. We'll let you know once the review is complete.
What it means:
Verification has failed, and immediate action is required to restore full account functionality.
Action:
Click Update information on the banner to see the specific reason for the rejection and upload new documents.
What if I think my document was rejected by mistake?
If you've reviewed the requirements above and believe your document meets all the criteria, please
contact our support team
.

title: How do I verify my address?
content:
As a financial institution, we need to know who is using our services. It helps us combat money laundering and keep everyone’s money safe.
This means we sometimes need to ask for documents that prove your address. We’ll ask you to upload a high-quality photo or scan of a document. It must clearly show:
Your full name:
This must match the name on your Wise account.
Your full home address:
This must also match the address on your Wise account.
Date of issue:
Utility bills, bank statements, and council tax bills can't be older than 3 months.
A logo or clear name of the sender:
For example, your bank or your utility provider.
What documents can I use to verify my address?
Documents we accept
A utility bill (gas, electric, or landline phone)
A bank or credit card statement
A council tax bill
A vehicle registration or tax document
A photo driving licence with your address and expiry date
A rental agreement — it must display the address, along with your name and your landlord's name, and include both signatures, as well as the start and end dates of the tenancy
For students, a
university acceptance letter
confirming your on-campus address, issued within the last 3 months.
Other documents issued by a government or financial institution (N26, Monzo, Bunq, Monese)
If you live with family or friends and don't have a document in your name, you can provide a document in their name along with a government-issued document confirming your relationship (like a birth certificate).
Documents we don't accept
Note that we can't accept the following as proof of address:
Documents from Moneygram, WesternUnion or Revolut
Mobile phone bills
PO box addresses
Address proofs that have been issued to a business — the address proof must be issued to your
personal address
Driving licenses as a proof of address if it was already used as a proof of identity
Wise statements or Wise transfer receipts
Country-specific documents
If you live in
China:
Learn more about getting verified when sending from CNY
If you live in
New Zealand:
If you live in New Zealand and send from NZD we need to verify your identity and address. We don’t accept Kiwi Access Card as identity documents
Important to note
If we've requested an ID document and an address document, these must be separate documents
If you live in a country that recognises both registered addresses and residential addresses, please use your residential address on your Wise account
You can also submit a document from a Local Authority or FCA authorised institution which was issued within the last 12 months. For example, a yearly council tax bill or a letter confirming the opening of a bank account
How long does address verification take?
After you upload your documents, we'll review them and send you an email within 2 working days.
You can check your verification status at any time by going to the
Verification
tab in your account settings.
Once we've successfully verified you, we'll automatically resume any transfers you have in progress.
How can I change my address?
You typically can edit your address from your Wise account. But, if the address field is greyed out — you can’t change it yourself. This is usually because of regional regulations.
Get in touch
and we’ll help change it.
Learn more about editing your personal details
Learn more about changing your address on a business account
What if my document wasn't accepted?
If we didn't accept your document, here's a few things it could be.
It may have some of the information covered. We need to see the full document photo page.
It may be expired. Please check that your document is still valid before you upload it.
Your details may not match. Please check that you have entered your name and date of birth correctly on the website.
We may also need the back of the document. If there is information on both sides, please make sure you upload both for us.
It may be a document type that we can not accept.
It may be in a format we can not accept, for example ID documents are not accepted in PDF format. However, if this is the case, you can make a screenshot of the document, and upload that screenshot, as this will be in JPG format, which we can accept.
We do not accept photos taken of screens.
What does Wise do with this information?
Protecting your information is very important to us. All data, financial details, or documents you provide us are stored securely, encrypted, and kept private as per our
privacy policy
.

title: How do I verify my country of residence?
content:
To meet financial regulations, we sometimes need to check that you have a legal right to live in the country your Wise account is registered in.
This check is different from
verifying your address
(which uses documents like a utility bill). For the right to reside, we need to see a valid, government-issued document.
When do you ask for this?
We’ll ask you to provide a document if the ID you used to verify your identity (like a passport) wasn't issued by the same country as the address on your Wise account.
For example, if you live in Germany but verified your identity with a passport from another country, we'll need to see your German-issued document proving your right to live there.
Until this check is complete, we may have to limit access to some features, like ordering a card, adding a currency or sending a transfer.
How do I upload my document?
If we need this document from you, we'll ask you with a
banner on your account's homepage
.
Log in to your Wise account on the website or app.
Look for a banner that says "We need more information to verify your account"
Click the banner and follow the instructions to upload your document.
Important:
You must upload the document through this banner or during account creation if we ask for it. We can't accept documents sent to us via email.
If you don't see a banner but think you need to provide a document, please
contact us
.
What documents can I use?
The document must be issued by the country your account is registered in.
We can accept the following:
Document Type
Requirements
Passport
Must be valid and unexpired.
National ID Card
Must be valid and unexpired.
Residence Permit
Must be a valid and unexpired card. We can't accept paper residence permits.
Visa
Must be a long-term visa valid for more than 90 days. For a UK eVisa, you must also provide your immigration share code.
Refugee Document
We can accept some refugee identity documents.
Asylee Document
We can accept some asylee documents.
This list isn't exhaustive — other types of government-issued documents, like diplomatic IDs, may also be accepted. If your document isn't on this list and you're not sure if it's acceptable, please
contact us
.
What documents can't we accept?
We can't accept the following documents for this check:
Document Type
Examples
Short-term Visas
Tourist visas, transit visas, or any visa valid for 90 days or less.
Proof of Address
Utility bills, bank statements, or rental agreements. (These are used for
address verification
, which is a different check).
Driving License
Digital-only IDs
We can't accept most virtual or electronic documents, like the Estonian e-Residency. The only exception is the UK eVisa with a share code.
Applications
Application forms for a visa, permanent residency, or refugee status.
Other Documents
Asylum seeker documents, tax bills, census documents, or business documents.
What happens if my document is rejected?
If your document is rejected (for example, if it was blurry or expired), you can upload it again.
Log in to your account.
Click the same banner you used the first time.
Follow the steps to re-upload your document.
When taking your photo, make sure the document is clear, in good light, and not blurry. All information on it must be easy to read. If you are not able to provide us a right to reside document, we might need to close your account.
Are there any exceptions?
Yes, there are a few rules to be aware of:
EEA Countries:
If your registered address is in an EEA country, we can accept a right-to-reside document issued by any other EEA country.
Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC):
If your registered address is in a GCC country, we can accept a right-to-reside document issued by any other member country.
Bilateral Agreements:
Some countries have agreements that let their citizens live in either country without extra documents. For example, citizens of Australia and New Zealand can reside in either country.
What if I need to change my country of residence?
If your primary residence has permanently moved to a new country, you'll need to update the country on your profile.
If the country field in your profile is greyed out, it means you'll need our help to change it. Please
contact us
with a document issued by your new country, and we'll help you update it.

title: How to take a selfie with your ID for verification
content:
To keep you and your money safe, we sometimes need to confirm it’s really you. One way we do this is by asking for a photo of you holding your ID — this is known as a ‘selfie with ID’.
We’ll guide you through the process, but here are some tips to help you get it right on the first try.
Find the best light.
Face a window to use natural daylight and turn off any overhead lights that might cause glare. Avoid having a strong light source behind you.
Show your whole face.
Look straight at the camera and make sure nothing is covering your face. It's best to remove hats or sunglasses.
Hold your ID correctly.
Hold the document next to your face. Make sure your fingers aren't covering any information or the edges of the ID.
We might also ask you to hold a piece of paper with a specific code or the current date written on it. Please make sure this is also clearly readable in the photo.
Check for clarity.
Before you submit, check that every single word on your ID is sharp and readable. All four corners of the document should be visible in the frame.  If your photo isn't of high enough quality (sufficiently sharp and not blurry), we'll request a better copy.
What if I’m having trouble?
If your photo keeps getting rejected, this is almost always due to photo quality. The most common issues are glare on the ID, blurry text, or a finger covering information. Please double-check the tips above and try again in a different spot.
We understand this can be tricky. If you're struggling because of a physical disability or you're having technical issues, please
get in touch with our Support team
. We're here to help.

title: How do I confirm my identity with a photo of my ID and a live photo?
content:
Every customer needs to be verified at some stage when using Wise. This is how we make sure we're safe and secure for all our customers, and stay compliant with anti-money laundering rules.
Sometimes, this means we'll ask to compare a live photo of your face with a picture of your official ID.
This is different from taking a photo while holding your ID next to your face. For this check, you’ll take a photo of your ID, and then a separate live photo of your face. You
can't upload
photos you've already taken.
Read more on our biometric data processing
Which ID can I use?
Before you start, make sure you have a valid, unexpired ID that we accept for this type of verification.
In most countries, we can accept:
Passport, National ID card
In the European Economic Area (EEA), we can't accept driver's licenses
for this verification. You must use a Passport or National ID card
To see the full list of documents we accept for identity verification by country, please
check our guide to getting verified
. Using a document that isn't supported for this process will cause the verification to fail.
How do I complete the verification?
You can usually complete this process using your smartphone (via the Wise app or a secure link) or your computer's webcam.
Using your phone is highly recommended as the camera is usually better.
Starting on the Web:
When prompted to verify your identity, we'll suggest using your phone. You'll see options like:
Scan QR Code:
Use your phone's camera to scan the code shown on your computer screen.
Get link by email:
We'll email you a secure link to open on your phone.
Follow the link or QR code on your phone to continue the process in the Wise app or your phone's browser.
If you can't use a phone, select
I don't have a phone
or
Use webcam
to use your computer's camera.
Completing on your phone or computer:
Choose your ID:
Select the country that issued your ID and the document type.
Take a photo of your ID:
Position your ID clearly within the frame on screen.
Make sure the photo is clear, without glare or blurriness. See tips below.
You might need to photograph both the front and back.
Review the photo. If it's unclear, select
Retake photo
. If it looks good, confirm it.
Take a live photo (selfie):
Position your face within the oval frame.
Don’t hold your ID card in this step.
Follow the on-screen instructions (like moving closer or holding still).
The photo will be taken automatically.
Submit:
Once both photos are captured, submit them for verification.
Tips for taking good photos
Find good lighting:
Use a well-lit room, but avoid direct light that causes glare on your ID or shadows on your face.
Keep it steady:
Place your ID on a flat surface to take its picture. When taking your live photo, hold your phone or sit still. Blurry photos will be rejected.
Fill the frame:
Make sure your entire ID is inside the frame – don't cover any part with your fingers. For your live photo, ensure your whole face is visible inside the oval.
Check the text:
All text on your ID must be sharp and readable. Double-check your name, date of birth, and the expiry date.
What if my verification fails or I have problems?
Bad photo quality:
If your photos are rejected, you'll usually be prompted to try again. Use the tips above for the best results.
Technical issues (app freeze, webcam error, QR code not working):
Restart the Wise app or your browser.
If you're using a webcam, check that you've given your browser permission to use it and try a different browser if it still fails.
Look for a
recovery link
on any paused transfer or in an email from us.
Red banner on Home screen:
If your verification failed, you might see a red banner on your Wise account Home screen. Clicking this may let you retry.
No recovery link or banner:
As a last resort, try setting up a
new
, small transfer. You don't need to pay for it, but this can trigger the verification flow again.
"We have all we need" message:
If you see this, it means your photos are already with our team for a manual review. You can't upload more until they make a decision. Please wait for our email.
Still stuck?
If you've tried these steps and can't complete the verification, please
contact our support team
.
I still need extra help
We understand taking photos can be difficult. If you continue to struggle after multiple attempts:
Ask someone you trust to help you.
If that's not possible,
contact our support team
and explain your situation.
If you see a message that says 'No thanks, do it manually', then you might still be able to manually upload a photo, but bear in mind that this will take longer. If you don't see this option, then you can't upload manually.

title: How do I prove the source of income for my personal account?
content:
This article is for
personal customers
. If you have a Wise Business account,
learn about verifying your business here
.
We're a regulated financial institution, and to keep your money safe, we sometimes need to ask for a document that shows where your income comes from. We know this is an extra step, but it helps us make sure your money is secure.
We’ll only ask for this document after you’ve set up and paid for a transfer.
Quick tips before you upload
To make sure we can accept your document the first time, please check that:
All pages are included and clearly visible.
Don't crop, edit, or hide any information. For bank statements, this means uploading the full statement.
The format is correct.
We can accept PDF, JPEG, and PNG files up to 10MB per file. We can't accept password-protected files.
You upload the correct document.
Check the table below to see which document matches your income type.
What details does my document need to show?
No matter the type, your document must clearly show:
Your full name
The date the document was issued
The amount(s) you received
The name of the person or company who paid you
What document should I send for my income type?
Find your main source of income in the table below and upload one of the corresponding documents. You can upload up to 10 files if you need to show multiple documents.
Salary
One
of the following:
Your most recent payslip (from the last 3 months)
A signed employment contract
A recent tax return validated by your official authority
Full bank statements from the last 3 consecutive months showing all transactions — it must be downloaded or sent from your bank — this shouldn’t be a screenshot of your online banking
Freelance work or Independent Contractor
One
of the following:
A recent tax return validated by your official authority showing your income
The most recent statement of earnings from a freelancing platform
Service agreement contract signed within the last 12 months and showing the pay period and total amount — we can’t accept agreements that only show an hourly wage
Pension
One
of the following:
A pension statement from your provider (from the last 12 months)
Full bank statements from the last 3 consecutive months showing your pension payments
Rent
One
of the following:
The most recent tax return validated by your official authority your rent income
Signed rental agreement
Full bank statements from the last 3 consecutive months showing all transactions — it must be downloaded or sent from your bank — this shouldn’t be a screenshot of your online banking
Sale of property
The signed completion statement or sale agreement for the property.
A gift, inheritance or family support
Both
of the following:
A signed letter or deed from the person who gave you the money.
A document showing their source of income. This could be their payslip or a property sale agreement.
Why do you need this?
It's a regulatory requirement to understand the origin of the funds. If the giver is retired, you can provide their pension statement or a bank statement showing their income.
Investments
One
of the following showing net proceeds or value of the investment portfolio:
Employee stock plan exercise confirmation
Investment/portfolio account statement
Dividend voucher
Share sale confirmation
Crypto wallet statement — Must come from a regulated crypto wallet provider
Bank statement confirming income from investments
A loan
Both
of the following:
The signed loan agreement
Supporting bank statement showing the amount being received
Savings
Both
of the following:
A savings account statement from the last 6 months.
A supporting document that shows how you built up these savings (like payslips or a property sale agreement).
Other sources (e.g. divorce settlement, scholarships, child support, lottery)
An official document from a third party (like a legal firm or institution) that clearly shows where the money came from. This could be
one
of the following:
A divorce settlement document
A scholarship letter
Child support document
Lottery winnings confirmation document
Along with supporting documentation, such as bank statement confirming income
What happens after I upload my document?
Once we’ve reviewed and approved your document, we’ll automatically resume your transfer and let you know. If there are any issues, we’ll contact you by email.
If you cancel your transfer while we're checking, we'll stop the review. We may need to ask for the documents again if you set up another transfer in the future.

title: I don't have the documents you asked for
content:
We know that it’s not always easy to get certain forms of ID. So we try our best to be flexible.
In this article, we’ll let you know what kinds of ID we accept around the world, as well as what to do if you have refugee status.
Verifying your identity
You can verify your identity with a good quality copy of your ID. We accept:
the photo page from your passport for international travel. We can’t accept internal passports, like those in Russia, with an exception for Ukrainian internal passports
driver’s licence — unless you’re a New Zealand resident sending from NZD
national ID card (we only accept the most recent version of the Nigerian national ID card)
Tip:
We can’t accept bank cards as ID.
For some currencies, there are different rules.
Residing in EU/EEA:
If you live in Europe, we can’t accept a driving licence to confirm your identity.
Sending from NZD
If you live in New Zealand and send from NZD we need to verify your identity and address. We don’t accept driver’s licence (including foreign and NZ driver’s licence) or Kiwi Access Card as identity documents.
Sending from JPY
Your ID must be issued by Japan and we’ll also ask for your My Number document.
Sending from BRL
Your ID must be issued by the Brazilian government. You can also verify your identity with a national identity (RG) card.
If you’re a refugee, you may be able to use Wise
We know that it’s hard for people with refugee status to open a bank account. But if you originate from, or have settled in any of the countries below, we can verify you with the following documents:
The US
Passport photo page
State identification card or driver’s license
Permanent resident card (also called a green card)
Tip:
We can’t accept green cards or work permits if your address is in Nevada, Hawaii, the Mariana Islands, or the Virgin Islands.
Canada
Passport
Canadian refugee travel document
Permanent resident card
A country in the EU or EEA
Alien’s passport photo page
Travel document
ID from an EEA member country, like a passport or ID card, that states your refugee status
Tip:
If you’re a stateless person, the NY Convention of 28th September 1954 gives you the legal right to an alien’s passport. And under the Geneva Convention of 28th July, 1951, you have the right to a travel document.
What if my ID isn't listed in this article?
If you don’t have any of the documents above, we won’t be able to verify you. That means we might need to limit the services we offer you as well.
We can sometimes make exceptions, depending on the country you’re in. For more details, please
get in touch with our team
.

title: My document wasn't good enough quality
content:
Part of the verification process involves our team reviewing what’s printed on the documents. If we can’t read what’s on a document, we have to reject it.
When making a copy or taking a picture of a document, here’s what to check:
Lighting:
If it’s too bright, glare might make part of your document unreadable. This can be a problem with laminated passport photo pages or driving licences.
Your fingers:
If you’re holding up a document for a photo, it’s easy to cover up some details with your fingers. Try taking the photo with the document on a flat surface.
The camera frame:
We need to see the whole document, as this helps us confirm its authenticity. So try to fit the whole document in the shot.
Blur and camera grain:
If you’re on the move, your camera might not be able to focus on the details printed on your document. Try taking the photo with the document on a flat surface.
Tip:
Be careful of zoom mode, or bad lighting. It can result in photos that are too grainy for us to read properly.
We can’t accept copies of photocopies. We need to be sure that you have the document you’re verifying your identity with, so it’s best to take a new photo when you’re going through verification.
Using a camera is better than a scanner
Many scanners aren’t great at scanning IDs and passports because they’re too thick and stop the lid from closing. So the resulting scans are close to unreadable. Again, it’s best to take a photo if you can.
Are you sending us the right documents?
If you need to verify your identity, we’ll ask for a copy of your driver’s licence, national ID card, or passport photo page. There are some exceptions, depending on where you’re sending from.
Tip:
We can’t use selfies or bank cards to verify your identity, but sometimes we’ll ask you to take a photo of yourself holding your ID next to your face.
Have you sent us a new document?
Usually, we need 1–2 working days to review new documents. But if you had to upload a lot of documents, it may take us a little longer. Keep an eye on your inbox, as we’ll confirm that you’re verified by email.
When you’re verified, we’ll automatically resume any transfers you have going through. You don’t have to do anything.

title: My document didn't have enough information on it
content:
When we review documents, we look for certain pieces of information on them. Most times, this helps us confirm that the document you’ve sent us is authentic and valid. We’ll also make sure that the details printed on them match with your Wise account.
Here’s what we check for:
Expiry date and date of birth
When we verify your identity, we need to see when your driver’s licence, national ID card, or passport expires. And we use your date of birth to confirm that the ID really belongs to you. So if we can’t see these details, we’ll have to reject your document.
Tip:
Often ID cards have some key bits of info printed on the back. So always make sure you send us both sides.
The spelling of your name
Because we’re a safe and regulated way to send money, we need to know your full legal name. And if your name on Wise and your document don’t match, we’ll need to ask you for more information.
Tip:
Made a typo or signed up with a nickname? You can fix that by going to your
Personal Details
in the app, or
View personal profile
on desktop.
The Machine Readable Zone
On documents like passport and ID cards, there’s a unique barcode called a Machine Readable Zone. We look for them because they confirm the document you’re uploading is authentic.
If this code is cut off, we’ll need to ask you for a new copy of your ID.
If you upload a driver’s licence or national ID card, we need to see what’s on the back
Most driver’s licences or ID cards have important information on the back. So we need to see that too.
If you haven’t uploaded the back of your ID, we’ll ask you to upload a copy of it.
Have you sent us a new document?
Usually, we need 1–2 working days to review new documents. But if you had to upload a lot of documents, it may take us a little longer. Keep an eye on your inbox, as we’ll confirm that you’re verified by email.
When you’re verified, we’ll automatically resume any transfers you have going through. You don’t have to do anything.

title: Can I have more than one Wise account?
content:
Here’s a simple guide to how personal and business accounts work at Wise:
You can only have one personal Wise account.
It's against our
Customer Agreement
to create more than one.
You can have a separate business account for each of your different businesses
, and manage them all from a single login.
Personal accounts
You can only have one personal Wise account. This helps us keep your account secure and comply with financial regulations.
If you’ve forgotten your login details, it’s best to follow the steps to
reset your password
or
change your email address
instead of creating a new account.
Business accounts
You can add multiple Wise Business accounts for each of your
separate legal entities
(e.g. a Limited Company, Partnership, or LLC).
How can I add a new business account?
On the web
Click on your name in the top right-hand corner
Select
Open a business account
from the dropdown menu
Follow the on-screen instructions to set up your new business profile
On the app
Tap on your profile picture or initials in the top-left corner
Select
Open a business account
Follow the on-screen instructions to set up your new business profile
What if I'm a sole trader or freelancer?
If you're registered as a sole trader or freelancer, you’re considered the same legal entity as your business. This means you can only have one Wise Business account.
Good to know:
Each of your businesses must be a separate legal entity. This means you can't open different business accounts for multiple trading names under the same company.
You can easily switch between your accounts by clicking your name (on the web) or profile picture (on the app).
How to add a personal account if I signed up for Business first?
If you only have a business account, you can easily add a personal account to your existing login.
On the web
Click on your business name in the top right-hand corner
Select
Open another account
Choose
Personal account
and follow the on-screen instructions
On the app
Tap on your business initials or photo in the top-left corner
Tap on
Open an account
Choose
Personal account
and follow the on-screen instructions
What if I accidentally created a duplicate account?
If you've accidentally created a second personal account, we'll need to close one of them. This keeps your account secure and avoids any issues with your transfers.
Please
get in touch with our support team
and let us know:
The email address on the account you want to
keep
The email address on the account you want to
close
Our team will then help you move any remaining money and close the duplicate account safely.
Learn more about what to do if you create a duplicate account
.

title: What happens if I have a duplicate account?
content:
You can only have one personal Wise account, and one business profile for each of your businesses. If you've accidentally created a duplicate, we'll need to close it to keep your money safe and comply with regulations.
Don't worry — we'll help you get access to the right account and make sure you don't lose any money in the process.
I already have a duplicate account. What should I do?
The best thing to do is
contact Wise Support
and let us handle the closure. This helps avoid any issues with your money or information.
When you contact us, please let our team know the email addresses for both accounts.
How can I avoid creating a duplicate account?
Most duplicate accounts are created by accident when you can't log in to your original account.
If you forgot your password,
reset your password
.
If you no longer use the email address connected to your account, see how you can
change your email address
.
How do I add a business account to my personal account?
You can have multiple business accounts, but they must all be linked to your single personal Wise account.
To add a business account:
Log in to your personal account on the web or app
Click on your name in the top right corner (web) or your profile picture in the top left corner (app)
Select
Open a business account
and follow the instructions.
Good to know:
Your money is safe.
If you have money in a duplicate account that we close, we'll automatically send it to your other Wise account, or refund it to the bank account it came from.
Transaction history can't be merged.
We can't move your transaction history from the duplicate account to your main one. If you need these records, make sure to
download your statements
before we close the account.
If you’ve given us your CPF number
, you can’t deactivate the other account to make a new one. Instead, try resetting your password or changing your email to get access to your account.

title: Getting verified in Brazil
content:
To send money from Brazilian Real (BRL), we need to verify your information. This is to comply with local regulations from the Brazilian Central Bank and keep your account safe.
This verification is only required for customers who are
Brazilian tax residents
. If you aren't a tax resident in Brazil, you won't be able to send money from BRL.
It usually takes 1-4 working days for us to review your documents after you upload them.
What are the verification steps?
We verify you in a few different stages.
Identity and Address:
When you set up your first transfer from BRL.
Proof of income:
Once per fiscal year, we'll ask you to confirm your income and assets.
Source of funds:
We may ask for documents if you send amounts over your yearly limit.
Verifying your identity
When you set up your first transfer from BRL, we'll ask for a high-quality photo or scan of one of the following documents:
Your passport
Your Brazilian identity card (RG) — it must have been issued in the last 15 years
Your Brazilian driver's license (CNH) — we can't accept the electronic version
Your Carteira de Registro Nacional Migratório (CRNM), also known as RNE
Note that we're not able to accept provisional refugee IDs (Documento Provisório de Registro Nacional Migratório).
If your ID document has a plastic cover, please remove it before taking the photo — it'll helps us see your information better.
Read more about verifying your ID
Verifying your address
You'll need a Brazilian address to send from BRL. We'll ask for a high-quality photo or scan of a document. It needs to show:
Your full name
Your full Brazilian address
The date of issue
The name or logo of the issuer
Purpose of the document — we can’t accept an image of the envelope
Acceptable documents include:
Utility bills (like electricity, water, or gas) issued within the last 3 months
A mobile phone bill (must be from a Brazilian provider) issued within the last 3 months
A Boleto (both the invoice and receipt parts) issued within the last 3 months
Urban Building and Property Tax (IPTU) issued within the last 12 months
A condominium bill issued within the last 3 months — we need to see a breakdown of what you're paying for
Proof of income
Due to regulations, we must collect information about your income and assets once per Brazilian fiscal year (which runs from April 1st to March 31st).
We’ll need to see all of your Brazilian tax return along with its filing receipt. You can download these from the
Receita Federal website
.
Keep in mind that your income should support the amount of BRL you plan to send for the year.
Common questions
Question
Answer
Why do I need to add my CPF?
Your CPF (Cadastro de Pessoas Físicas) is your Brazilian taxpayer ID number. We're required by local regulations to report all BRL transfers using a CPF.
How do I prove my income if I live abroad?
We can only accept proof of income earned during the current tax year in Brazil. You can download your tax return with the filing receipt directly from
Brazil’s Internal Revenue Office website
. To download the full declaration, you might need to use the IRPF app.
What if my family supports me and I don't receive income in Brazil?
You can upload your family member, or partners, tax declaration and its filing receipt as long as you’re listed as a dependent. They need to have enough income to show they can support you and any other dependents over 18. Although you’re a dependent of someone, when you pay for your transfers the money must come from a bank account in
your name
.

title: How we can help you when someone with a Wise account dies
content:
We understand that handling the finances of a loved one who’s passed away can be very difficult.
We do our best to make the process of closing an account, and if needed, withdrawing money, as simple as possible.
How to report a death to Wise and what information we can disclose
Our process is flexible, and we’ll need to understand some things about both you and our deceased customer to help you in the best way possible.
Per our privacy policy, we’re only able to disclose account information to those legally entitled to it.
If you want to receive an account or receive activity statement, select the following based on your relationship to the customer.
If you're:
an executor appointed in our customer’s will
or the legal next of kin
Learn more about how to contact us, and our deceased customer report guidelines — Testate and Intestate, and how to contact us
If you're:
reaching out on the behalf of an Executor of the estate
or a next of kin as a solicitor, attorney, or notary
or a public servant acting under official appointment by relevant authorities
Learn more about how to contact us, and about our deceased customer report guidelines — Legal representatives and public servant
Who can we share information with?
If you aren’t any of the above, we aren’t able to share any account information with you, even if you’re helping someone. But you can still help them navigate our process.
Follow the relevant link above and include an email address for the executor or next of kin in your communications.
If you’re not legally representing our customer, but request to engage in our process on behalf of the executor or next of kin, your request may be rejected and our ability to communicate further may be terminated. This can be at any time for a variety of reasons and it’s in line with our regulatory responsibilities.
If you’re a business co-owner
If you’re the new director of a business and our deceased customer held ownership of the business account, contact our support team and provide the following documentation to initiate the change of ownership:
The death certificate
A copy of your ID. If you have a Wise account, you can
upload your ID here
Legal document showing you're a shareholder or director of the business
Our team won’t be able to disclose any information about the business, but we’ll help transfer ownership of the business to the new director, as soon as possible.
We ask that you have the representative(s) of the estate visit this page and follow our process at their earliest convenience.

title: Can I set up a beneficiary for my account?
content:
Unfortunately, we don’t currently offer the option to set a beneficiary for your account.
If you were to pass, the funds held on your account would be handled per your will or local succession law.
We recommend that you
:
have a will in place
keep your account information up to date – especially your registered address updated to make sure your funds are addressed properly.
This helps make sure your funds are addressed properly, efficiently, and as humanely as possible.
What should my family or executor do?
Your legal next of kin or appointed executor will need to contact us. We’ll ask them for the appropriate legal documents showing they have a right to the funds held — these depend on your region and other factors.
We sincerely hope  that you and your loved ones don’t have to go through our bereavements process anytime soon.
Learn more about what Wise can do if someone with a Wise account dies

title: What should I do if I get an 'invalid address' error?
content:
When you're signing up, changing your details, or adding a recipient, we need to check your address. As a financially regulated institution, we have to make sure your address is valid and exists.
If our system can't find your address, you'll see an error message. Below you can see how to fix it.
How to fix an invalid address error
Most of the time, this error can be fixed by double-checking the details you've entered:
Check your postcode and city.
Make sure they’re in the correct, separate fields.
Enter your full street address.
In the
Home address
field (sometimes called
Address line 1
), enter your complete street name, building number, and apartment or unit number.
Don't mix details.
Make sure you haven't entered your postcode or city in the
Home address
field.
Check the country.
Make sure you've selected the correct country. This option might be on the same page or the previous one.
Good to know:
If your address doesn't have a house number, you can use a house name or another official local identifier.
It can help to look up your address on Google Maps to see how it's officially formatted.
What if my address is correct?
In some countries, if we can't find your address, you'll see a button that says
Proceed anyway
.
If you're sure that all the details you've entered are correct, you can click this button to continue. Please note that if you do this, we may need to ask you for a
proof of address
document later on.
What if I'm changing my country?
If you're changing your country and see this error, the same troubleshooting steps apply. Just make sure you've selected the new country correctly on the form.
Learn more about editing your personal details
What if I'm still stuck?
If you've tried all the steps above and are still getting an error, please
contact us
. It’ll help speed things up if you have a proof of address document ready.

title: How do I verify my email address?
content:
When you add or change the email on your Wise account, we’ll ask you to verify it. Just click the link in the verification email we send you to complete the process.
Verifying your email confirms you own the address and helps us keep your account secure. It allows us to:
Help you recover your account if you get locked out
Notify you of important changes to your account
Make sure you have full access to all of Wise's features
What should I do if I didn't get the verification email?
If the email isn't in your inbox after a few minutes, here's what to do.
1. Double-check your spam or junk folder
Sometimes, email providers can accidentally filter our messages. Make sure to look in your spam or junk folder first.
2. Make sure the email is spelled correctly
Go back to the Wise app or website and check for any typos in the email address you entered.
3. Resend the verification email
If it's still not there, select the
Resend email
option in the app or on the website where you are being asked to verify.
Good to know:
For your security, the verification link expires. If your link has expired, you’ll need to resend the email.
If you’ve tried all these steps and the resent email still doesn’t arrive, please
get in touch with us
so we can help you.
What if I've lost access to my registered email?
If you can't get into your email inbox to verify it, you won't be able to receive our verification link. You’ll need to change the email address on your Wise account first.
Learn how to change your email if you've lost access
Learn how to change your email if you've lost access and are logged out of your Wise account
What if I think my account is compromised?
If you see an email address on your account that you don't recognise, or believe an incorrect email was verified without your permission, please
get in touch with us
immediately.

title: How do I change my email address?
content:
The email address listed in your Wise account is how we keep in touch. You can change it from your settings.
I’m logged in
This is the fastest way to change your email. You'll need access to your old email address to confirm the change.
On the web or app
Go to your profile name or picture in the top corner
Go to
Personal details
and select
Email address
Select
Change email address
Complete the steps to update the email address
Add a new email address
Approve the 2-step verification
Go to your old email inbox and confirm the new email
Once you’ve verified the new email, you’ll be able to log in using it
I’m logged out and can't access my registered email
Use this option if you forgot your Wise password and can't access your old email to reset it.
Go to the
Change your email page
Enter the phone number associated with the account which email you want to change
If you have a business account, and there's more than one account with the same phone number, the self-service won't work
, and you’ll need to
call us
Confirm the phone number via the 2 step-verification SMS sent
Enter the new email address and confirm it via the email sent to the new email’s inbox
Set a new password
Upload a selfie with ID
A confirmation or rejection email will be sent to you to let you know if the change is completed:
If yes, your email will have been changed and you can now log in using the new email and password combination
If no, it's likely you’ve uploaded a poor quality image and will need to re-upload the selfie with ID
It usually takes about 10 minutes for us to review your documents and update your email.
For Wise Business accounts
If you need to change the email address for your Wise Business account, you'll need to
give us a call
. For security reasons, this change can't be made using the self-service steps.
I can't access my email or my phone number
For security, you'll need to
give us a call
so we can help you regain access to your account.
I didn't get the confirmation email
Check your spam or junk folder. The link is only valid for a limited time, so if it has expired, please start the process again.
I'm using a social login
Changing your email will disconnect your account from Facebook, Google, or Apple. You'll need to log in with your new email and password from now on.
See
I want to disconnect my social account
.

title: How can I change or reset my password?
content:
This article covers how you can change your password and what to do if you've forgotten it.
If you're logged out
If you can't remember your password, you'll need to reset it to get back into your account.
For your account security, we'll need to confirm it's really you. Before you start, make sure you have access to your:
Registered email address
2-step verification method
Once you're ready:
Click here to go to our password reset page
Enter your email address and follow the steps to set a new password
I can't access my registered email or want to change my email address
Can't access your registered email?
If you've lost access to your email and can't receive the reset link, you won't be able to reset your password yourself. For your security, please
give us a call
so our team can help you regain access to your account.
Trying to change your email address?
If your goal was to change your email, once you've successfully reset your password and logged in, you can now see
how to change your email address.
If you didn't get the password reset email
If you didn't receive our email, check:
that you're using the email address that you used to sign up with Wise
for any typos in your email address
your spam folder
all inboxes in your email. For example, for Gmail you may have different tabs set up for Primary, Social and Updates.
You can use your email's search bar to look for emails from "Wise" which will find the email and it'll show previous emails from us. If there aren't any previous emails, you're likely looking in the wrong email account.
AppleID users
If your email is hidden and you can't remember which one is connected to your Wise account, you can try to log in via social login. If you can't, you'd need to unhide your email address in your iCloud settings first, then go through our Trouble logging in? flow to get a password reset link.
If you're logged in
Web
Go to your profile name in the top corner
Select
Security and privacy
under
Settings
Select
password
, under
Security
Enter your current and new passwords and confirm
App
Go to your profile name in the top corner
Select
Security and privacy
under
Settings
Select
password
, under
Security
Enter your current and new passwords and confirm

title: How do I edit my personal details?
content:
You can edit most of your personal details right in your Wise account.
For your security, some details — like your name or date of birth after a transfer — can only be changed by us. If a field is greyed out, it means you'll need to
contact us
to update it.
How to edit your details
On the app
Tap on your profile picture or initials in the top-left corner.
Select
Personal details
.
Choose the detail you want to edit
On the web
Click on your name in the top-right corner.
Select
Personal details
.
Click on
Edit details
.
Can't log in?
If you've changed your phone number and can't access your account, you'll need to
use another way to log in
first.
Changing your address
You can usually
change your address yourself in your settings
. We may ask you to verify your new address.
If the address fields are greyed out, you can’t change it yourself. This often happens for security reasons, like if you've moved to a different country.
Contact us
and we’ll help you.
Changing your name
For security, you can't change your legal name yourself, but we can do it for you. Please
contact us
and send a clear photo or scan of:
L
egal document showing your name change (like a marriage certificate, name change document)
Your new ID showing your updated name
We need both documents to verify the change. If your ID hasn't been verified yet, we will only need the new ID.
Learn more about what IDs we can accept
What's a preferred name?
Your preferred name is what we use when we talk to you. It also shows up in your Settings. You can change this yourself in your
Personal details
after we’ve verified your identity.
Your
legal name
is different. We use it for verification, your Wise card, tax invoices, and payments.
If you live in Japan
Due to local regulations, you first need to update your name on your Japanese ID document. Once that's done,
send us
a photo of the front and back of the ID document, and we'll update your name in Wise. For the My Number card, we’ll only require the front side of the card.
Learn about getting verified in Japan

Personal details

If you have already completed a transfer with Wise, you'll need to
contact us
to change it.

title: How do I update my address?
content:
The process can vary depending on where you're moving to and from, as different countries have different rules. Sometimes we'll need to ask you for a document to verify your new address.
How to change your personal address
On the web/app:
Go to your name/initials/profile picture in the top corner of your account
Go to
Personal details
Select
Personal information
Scroll down and enter your physical address
Select
Save
Once you save the changes, we’ll let you know if we need to verify it.
Learn more about documents we accept as proof of address
Why can't you edit your address?
Your address details might be greyed out if you've moved to a new country or if we need to verify your address for another reason. When this happens, you’ll need to provide a document so we can change it for you.
Depending on where you're moving to we might also need you to confirm your tax details.
Learn more about confirming your tax details here
Moving to or from the US
If you move to or from the US, we will cancel your existing Wise cards. You can order a new card once your new address is verified.
When do you need to provide a proof of address document?
We may ask for a document if:
Your address has been verified before
You’re moving to or from the US, Canada, Japan, Brazil, Singapore, The Philippines, Indonesia, Hong Kong, India, or Türkiye
You've changed your address more than 5 times in the last 30 days
If we ask you for a document, we’ll send you an email with instructions and a secure link to upload it.
How to change your business address
We can only change a business address to one in the same country where the business is registered. If your business has moved to a new country, you'll need to open a new business account.
Learn about business addresses and acceptable documents
On the web/app:
Go to your name/initials in the top corner of your account
Go to
Business details
Click on
Edit
Enter your address
Select
Save
If you can't edit the address yourself, it's because we need a document to verify it first. We’ll need an official document that shows your new registered business address.
Once you have the document, please
contact us
so we can help you with the change.

y or

title: How do I manage my notifications?
content:
You can manage the way we get in touch in your
Settings
on web or app by going to
Notifications
.
Notification settings
You can review and change your notifications on the website or on the app.
There’s some things we’ll always need to tell you about, like changes to our T&Cs or your transfers. That means you can’t turn off notifications about the really important stuff.
Types of notifications
Your transfers and currencies:
Notifications about where your money is.
Your Wise card:
Notifications about your card transactions.
Currencies and features:
News about our latest work. Plus tips on using Wise.
Interviews, reviews, and surveys:
Invites to test new products and share your thoughts.
Our campaigns:
Updates about causes we care about, and information on how you can get involved.
How you get notifications
Email:
We’ll send your notifications to the email address that you’ve registered with.
Push:
We’ll send your notifications to any device that you have installed the app on.
Inbox message:
You’ll see an alert on the homepage when you log in to your account.

title: What is 2-step verification?
content:
2-step verification is a necessary layer of security on your account that helps keep your account protected, on top of your password. We use 2-step verification when we need to confirm it's really you.
You'll be prompted for it in most cases where you need to enter your password, but sometimes you'll need to use only your password without 2-step, and sometimes you'll need to use 2-step without a password. We do this to keep your account and money safe.
Why we use 2-step verification
Even if someone finds your password, 2-step verification helps stop them getting into your account.
It’s best to use the passkeys or Wise app for 2-step verification because they're more secure. They only need an internet connection, so they’ll work even if you’re abroad, or changed your SIM.
Options for your 2-step verification
You can choose one of these ways to complete 2-step verification.
Use passkeys on your device
—
Passkeys
are a secure authentication method we offer. They allow you to use your face, fingerprint, a device PIN or a hardware security key without ever needing to type a password. You can use Passkeys across multiple devices.
Receive a notification in the Wise app on a trusted device
— we’ll send a notification to the Wise app on your trusted device, and you’ll need to confirm on your device to continue.
Enter a code from a separate authenticator app
— you’ll need to pair the authenticator app with your Wise account first, then we’ll ask for the code from your authenticator app
Receive a code by text message or phone call
— we’ll send you a code by text message or phone call, and you’ll need to enter the code before you can continue
It’s best to use the Wise app or an authenticator app for 2-step verification because they're more secure. They only need an internet connection, so they’ll work even if you’re abroad, or changed your SIM.
Read here if you didn't receive a notification
Learn how to change your 2-step verification settings
What if I don't want 2-step verification?
We understand that it's an extra step, especially when you want to use Wise quickly. But we always make sure your account is protected, so this is a necessary step in your security. You can always choose how we do it from these options above.

title: How can I use a passkey?
content:
What is a passkey?
Passkeys are a secure authentication method we offer. They allow you to login and approve
2-step verification
with your face, fingerprint, a device PIN or a hardware security key without ever needing to type a password.
Passkeys are:
Faster: Use face and fingerprint recognition to verify it’s you in seconds.
More secure: Passkeys are phishing-resistant. They can’t be guessed or reused.
Useable across multiple devices: Log in with your face or fingerprint on any of your synced devices, through a passkey that’s stored securely in the cloud.
Why are passkeys safer?
A passkey consists of two parts: a public key that is stored by Wise and a corresponding private key on your device. When you log in, Wise checks to see if the two keys match. To do that, you are simply asked to use your face or fingerprint similar to unlocking the device you are using.
Where can passkeys be stored?
Passkeys can be stored in your credential manager such as Apple iCloud, Google Password Manager or 1Password. These credential managers typically support syncing your Passkey to multiple devices. This means that the Passkey can be used on any device with access to your credential manager.
Passkeys can also be stored outside of your credential manager directly on your device or browser. In this case, you will need one passkey per device.
Setting up a passkey
To set up your passkey:
Go to your name or initials in the top corner
Select
Security and privacy
under
Settings
Select
2-step verification
Select
Passkeys
Confirm it’s you using your existing 2-step verification method
Go through the browser or device flow to finish setting up. You'll need a compatible browser and device (listed below).
Once you've set it up,
Passkeys
will be your default 2-step verification method.
You'll be able to use your passkey for any action that needs 2-step verification.
Log in with a passkey
To log in with a passkey after you've set it up:
At log-in, select
Log in with passkey
at the bottom of the page
Go through the browser or device flow using your face, fingerprint, or device PIN to finish logging in
To log in on new devices, you'll need to complete additional authentication challenges. If you can’t access your passkey, you can still use your email and password on the
login page
.
Manage your passkeys
To manage your passkeys:
Go on your name or initials in the top corner
Select
Security and privacy
under
Settings
Select
2-step verification
Select
Passkeys
You can:
Add an additional passkey — just select
Add
Rename an existing passkey — just select
Edit
next to the passkey you want to rename
,
select
Rename
and
Save
Remove a passkey
To remove a passkey you've set up:
Go on your name or initials in the top corner
Select
Security and privacy
under
Settings
Select
2-step verification
Select
Passkeys
Select
Edit
next to the passkey you want to remove
Select
Delete
and confirm it’s you using your existing 2-step verification method
If you've removed a passkey from your Wise account, it won't work anymore. Even if you still see it as a login option, you won't be able to use it to log in and might get an error.
To remove the passkey from showing as an login option, you'll need to delete it from your credential manager.
Learn how to remove passkeys in Chrome
Learn how to remove passkeys on iPhone
What if Passkeys isn’t working?
Make sure that you're using a supported browser and device (listed below).
If your passkey still isn't working — log in using your email, password, and 2-step verification.
If you've forgotten your password, reset your password
Supported browsers
Chrome (version 108 and higher)
Firefox (version 122 and higher)
Safari (version 18.4 and higher)
Microsoft Edge (version 108 and higher)
Supported devices
iOS (version 16 and higher)
macOS (version 13 and higher)
Windows (version 10 and higher)
Android (version 9 and higher)

title: I didn't receive the 2-step verification code or notification
content:
If you’re not receiving the 2-step verification code via text or an app notification from us, here’s what you can do.
2-step verification with the Wise app
If you’re not getting the app notification for 2-step verification, check that:
wifi or mobile/cellular data is turned on
you’re checking the app on a device you set as
trusted
you don’t have power saver mode on
you’ve turned on notifications from Wise
you have the most recent version of the app
If it’s still not working, select
I haven’t received an approval request
and choose one of the other available methods. We recommend that you set up passkeys as alternative 2-step verification method.
Read more about passkeys
.
2-step verification by text message
If you’re not getting the text, check that:
your device can receive texts
you’re checking the right phone number. It should be the one registered to your Wise account
you have phone signal
If it’s still not working:
turn mobile/cellular data off and on
turn your phone off and on
try a local carrier if you’re on international roaming
You can also select
Try another way
and choose one of the alternative methods available.
We recommend that you set up alternative 2-step verification methods (e.g Passkeys or Wise app notifications) to avoid future issues with text messages.
Read more about how to add 2-step verification methods
.
2-step verification by WhatsApp
If you’re not getting the app notification, or the text message, you can choose to receive the code by WhatsApp. Just make sure your WhatsApp number matches the primary number on your Wise account. WhatsApp might not always be available, so select one of the other methods if you don't see this option.
2-step verification by Authenticator app
If your code is not working, check that:
you're on the correct Authenticator app
your account was correctly paired
What if none of these work?
If you’re eligible, you can recover your account with a selfie. For that:
Add
email and password in the login page
Choose
Try another way
In the 2-step verification page, choose
I don’t have any of these
Click
Continue
to confirm it’s you with a selfie
Select to check automatically, and continue to the selfie step
Follow instructions in the screen until the selfie is approved
Choose if you want to keep the same number or change
Still having issues logging in?
Troubleshoot logging in here
or
contact us
.

title: Logging in from a new device
content:
When logging in from a new device, we’ll need to verify that you're the device owner. To do this, we’ll send an email to your registered email address asking you to confirm the login
, or if you’re eligible, you can approve it with a selfie.
Will I need to do this every time I log in?
No. You'll only need to approve your device once if you haven't used it before.
Some privacy focused browsers may make your device look new every time. To avoid this, you can switch to a different browser such as Chrome or Edge.
Note: If you log in to the Wise website from your phone browser, it'll be considered a different device to logging into the Wise app from the same phone.
What if I can't use my old device?
If you have an old device attached to your 2-step verification and can't log in, you can remove it and connect 2-step verification on your new device.
When you see the message
Check your other device
on screen, select
I've got a new device
This will give you options to approve another way. You can select one of the listed options, like SMS, to log in with your existing number on a new device.
But if you also can't use your number, select
I don’t have any of this
and if you’re eligible, you can confirm it’s you with a selfie. If not, you can change your phone number from there or contact us.
What if I don't have access to my email?
If you don’t have access to your email, and you're eligible, you can use a selfie to approve your new device. Once you're logged in, you’ll need to update it.
If you're not eligible, you’ll need to update it before logging in.
Learn how to change your email address.
I haven’t received an email yet
The email can take a few minutes to arrive. If your email hasn’t arrived after 5 minutes, click the resend email button.
If that doesn’t help, please check your spam or junk folder and email filters.
I’ve got an email I wasn’t expecting, what do I do?
If you weren’t expecting the email, this could mean someone is trying to access your account.
Reset your password
immediately and
get in touch with us
.

title: I'm seeing the error message "We are unable to send an SMS to your number"
content:
Sometimes, we can't send out an SMS to phone numbers for security reasons.
This can happen if you requested 2-step verification too many times, or because of our internal verification checks for your number.
What if I didn't get an SMS?
If we couldn't send an SMS to your number, you can use another method:
Select
Try another way.
Then complete your 2-step verification using WhatsApp, Voice, or other methods available to you.
After you’re able to login, we recommend that you set up alternative 2-step verification methods (e.g. Passkeys or Wise app notifications) to avoid future issues with SMS.
Read more about how to add 2-step verification methods
.
If you're still having issues
read more about 2 step-verification troubleshooting
, or just
get in touch with us
and our team will take a look into your case.
We understand that this cause some inconveniences, but we're committed to keeping your account safe and to help you through this process as much as we can.

title: Why am I being logged out?
content:
To keep your account safe and to avoid anyone accessing your sensitive data, we automatically log out our customers after 5 minutes of inactivity. This means that if you log into your account, and leave it idle for 5 minutes, you'll be logged out.
This happens on both
web
and
app
.
On the
web
, you'll need to log in again with your email and password.
On the
app
, you'll need to use your biometrics or passcode to unlock the app. Note that your passcode is different from your password.
What if I forgot my passcode or can’t use biometrics?
If you forgot your passcode or can’t use biometrics:
Choose the option to log out of your account
Use your email and password to log in
After logging in, you'll be prompted to set up the passcode or biometrics again
Find out here how to set up biometric authentication

PT Wise Payments Indonesia is licensed by Bank Indonesia as a Fund Transfer Operator under licence number 21/304/DKI/B/14-001.

Malaysia
Wise Payments Malaysia Sdn. Bhd. is regulated under the laws of Malaysia by the Bank Negara Malaysia as a licensed
remittance, money-changing and e-money issuance
business.

, and supervised by regulatory authorities in each of those states.
