title: Wise card restrictions for Russian and Belarusian customers
content:
The European Union has recently adopted a new sanctions package. As a result, we’ve had to temporarily block Wise cards for our Russian and Belarusian customers. In order for us to unblock these cards, we need to verify  citizenship or residency in the European Economic Area (EEA) or Switzerland.
We had to permanently block
EU-issued digital or physical cards for our
Russian and Belarusian customers who
have since moved. If they
live in an eligible country
, they can to reorder the card for free.
How can I use my card again?
We informed affected customers about this change. If you received an email, please reply to this email by 30 January 2026 attaching one of the following valid documents:
a passport or national ID card confirming your European nationality or citizenship, or
a residence permit or visa document confirming your temporary or permanent residency status in the EEA or Switzerland
If you have questions or need help, please reply to the email we sent you.
I have provided a document. How long will it take to use my card again?
It may take up to 5 business days for us to review your documents.
How will I know that my card works again?
We’ll email you as soon as we have reviewed your submitted documents or if we require more information.
I can’t provide any of these documents
This means that you unfortunately can no longer use our card. But nothing else about your account is changing. You can still send, hold, receive, and convert money.
Does this impact physical and digital cards?
Yes, this impacts both physical and digital cards issued by Wise.
I have submitted a document, but my card still doesn’t work. Why?
We can’t unblock your Wise card until we’ve successfully reviewed the requested documents.
Until then, you won’t be able to:
use your physical and digital cards for online or in-store purchases,
withdraw money from ATMs, or
pay for recurring subscriptions using your card details.
Which sanctions package do you refer to?
This change relates to the 19th sanctions package adopted by the European Union. The new regulation specifically prohibits providing, directly or indirectly, payment services to Russian/Belarussian nationals or natural persons residing in Russia, or to legal persons, entities or bodies established in Russia.

title: Wise Travel Card in India
content:
The Wise Travel Card is available for Indian residents who are planning to travel.
We'll verify your identity using your Aadhaar number, PAN, and
video verification
.
Note,
the name for your 'main account' can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
How long will my card take to arrive?
Your physical card will take between 5-7 days to arrive depending on where in India we're shipping to. Cards typically arrive faster in Tier 1 cities.
How do I activate my card?
Enter the 6 digit code that comes along with your card in the physical envelope on the Wise app to activate it.
If you lose your code, you can use you card for a chip and PIN transaction to activate when abroad. You can also activate the card via any ATM by checking the balance and entering the PIN. This 4 digit PIN can be viewed on the app/website, under the
Cards
tab.
Read more on
How do I activate my Wise card?
.
Where can I use my card?
You can use your card when travelling abroad. You can't use your card in India, Nepal or Bhutan. We also don't allow transactions in sanctioned jurisdictions.
How can I verify my bank account?
When sending money from INR, we need to make sure that money is coming from your own account. If we detect a mismatch against your PAN name or account number, you'll need to provide a bank statement or a bank profile PDF.
A bank statement can be provided if:
The name on your bank statement matches exactly with the name on your PAN card
The account number on your statement fully matches with the account number in the linked bank transaction
The transaction to Wise is visible in the statement
A bank PDF needs to be provided if:
The name on your bank account is slightly different than the name on your PAN card
A bank PDF needs to be a print PDF version from your Netbanking page, where your profile details, like PAN number (obfuscated), your account name and your account number are clearly visible. Footnote of the PDF must mention the bank’s official website link. We can't accept mobile screenshots
What documents need to be provided for proof of travel?
We require the following documents:
Your passport
Your flight
Your visa, if applicable
You don’t need to provide proof of travel documents again in the case of re-loading money to your card during the same trip.
How to unload money from the card?
You can initiate unload (withdraw) on any unutilised funds in foreign currencies from the Wise app. You’ll be asked to provide proof of bank account ownership if you’re sending it to a different bank account than the initial one that you have paid in.
Note: As per guidelines, you can only withdraw the balance (unload the card) from the card after 10 days of the last card transaction. This rule doesn't apply if there are no card transactions done on your account.
Fees & Pricing
What are the ATM withdrawal charges from the card?
All withdrawals are free up to and including a cumulative of 200 USD per month. If you withdraw more than that, the fee is 1.40 USD
+ 3.25% of the amount over 200 USD.
What's Tax Collected at Source (TCS)?
TCS is a tax regulation for remittances under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) in India. We’ll use your PAN number to check if TCS applies to you. If it does, we’ll charge you an additional fee for your transaction and Wise’s banking partner will remit the TCS collected to the tax authorities in India. You’ll see the applicable TCS amount on the
Review
step of the transaction.
If you transact less than 10,00,000 INR in a financial year, no TCS will be charged on payments for travel, vacations, or visits to family and close relatives
If you transact over 10,00,000 INR in a financial year, you’ll be charged 20% of the amount for travel or vacation purposes
You'll need to declare if you've remitted less than 10,00,000 INR in the current financial year. If you've remitted more, you’ll be charged TCS on the full amount of the transaction
The financial year starts from 1 April and ends on 31 March the following year.
What's the Goods and Services Tax (GST)?
GST will be calculated based on the transaction amount and fees according to the relevant provisions of the Indian GST law. We’ll show you the applicable GST amount on the Review step of the transaction, and you’ll be able to download a tax invoice for the GST paid.
Common questions
Question
Answer
Are there any restrictions on my card?
You can't use your card for transactions in India, Nepal or Bhutan. We also don't allow you to use your Wise card for non-travel related expenses such as investments, topping up e-wallets, cryptocurrency and FX trading.
How can I top-up or load money to the card?
You can add money from your personal bank account within India via a bank transfer through IMPS, NEFT and RTGS payment networks.The name on your bank account has to match exactly with the name on your PAN card.
Is there a limit on how much I can load?
You can load money up to 250,000 USD (approximately 20,800,000 INR) per financial year (April to March). You can load up to 60,000 USD per transaction.
What currencies can I hold in my account?
You can hold in 40+ currencies, except INR and sanctioned currencies. You can also convert from one foreign currency to another, except INR and sanctioned currencies, at any time.
Can I add my card to Apple or Google Pay?
No, we don't support Apple or Google Pay at the moment.
Can I use contactless on my card?
Yes, contactless is enabled by default when you activate your card. You can tap to pay with your Wise card if the merchant accepts contactless.
How much does it cost to convert from INR?
You can find more information about the fees you have to pay on our
pricing page
. Read more on
Fees for sending money
.
How long does it usually take for the money to be converted from INR to a foreign currency?
It usually takes 1 hour for your money to reach Wise. Once we receive it, we convert your money, which can take up to 1 working day. We’ll always give you an estimate for how long your transaction will take when you set it up.

content:
You may be able to view or change your PIN depending on what country your card was issued from.
Check the table below to see what you can do with your Wise card.

If you have a digital card, you can’t change your PIN. If you feel that your digital card PIN may be compromised, you can instantly
replace it.
Viewing your PIN
If you forget your PIN, you can check it on app or web. To do this:
Go to the
Card
tab
Select
Show PIN
.
You may need to enter your Wise password or use fingerprint or facial recognition to check your PIN.
If you forgot your PIN and can't view it,
you'll need to order a new card
.
Changing your PIN
You can't change your PIN in your Wise account, but you might be able to at an ATM. The ATM will need to support PIN changes for cards other than those with the ATM's issuer. If you can’t find a supported ATM, but you need a new PIN, you can
order a new card
.
*You can't change your PIN at ATMs in Austria, Belgium, Finland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, and Spain.
**If your card was issued in Australia, Brazil, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, or Singapore you can only change your PIN at a supported ATM in the EEA/UK while travelling. You won't be able to change it at local ATMs in your own country.
What should I do if my PIN is blocked?
If you enter the wrong PIN 3 times, we’ll block it and you won’t be able to use your card until you unblock it. Here's how you can unblock it:
Go to the
Card
tab
Select
Unblock PIN
You’ll now be able to use your card online. But to use contactless again with your card, you’ll need to make a payment using your chip and PIN first.
We can never change or view your PIN for you and we'll never ask you to share your PIN with anyone.

title: How you can use your Wise card at an ATM
content:
You can use your Wise card like any other bank card to withdraw money, check your account balance and change your PIN at ATMs around the world that support it.
Check the table below to see what you can do at ATMs.

*If your card was issued in Australia, Brazil, Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, or Singapore you can only change your PIN at a supported ATM in the EEA/UK while travelling. You won't be able to change it at local ATMs in your own country.
**Local withdrawals for in Brazil are typically only supported at Rede Banco 24 Horas ATMs, though it's not guaranteed. Choose “international cards” if prompted on the ATM.
***Local withdrawals in Japan are typically only supported at AEON, E-net, and Daily Yamazaki ATMs, though it's not guaranteed. Choose “international cards” if prompted on the ATM.
If you don’t know of any ATM near you, you can use the
Mastercard ATM locator
or
Visa ATM locator
to find one.
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Withdrawing Cash
You can make 2 free withdrawals each month. After that, you’ll pay a fixed fee per withdrawal.
There’s also a variable fee on anything you take out over a certain amount.
The exact fees you pay depend on where your card was issued — it’s usually the registered address on your Wise account.
Learn more about how much it costs to withdraw cash
Changing your PIN
Non-US cardholders can change your PIN from supported ATMs.
The ATM must support PIN changes for cards other than those with the ATM's issuer. This is more common in the EEA and UK.
Learn more about changing or viewing your PIN
Checking your account balance
The best way to check your account balance is on the Wise app or website.
You can check your account balance at ATMs as well, but they don't always give you accurate information. You can't check your balance at ATMs if your card was issued in the US.
Troubleshooting
Sometimes you'll run into issues when trying to use your Wise card at an ATM so here's what you can do:
What if the ATM asks for a 5 or 6- digit PIN?
If you’re asked to enter a 5 or 6-digit PIN at an ATM, all you need to do is add extra zeros to the end of your PIN.
First try with your regular 4-digit PIN
If that doesn’t work, then add an additional “0” or “00” to the end of your PIN
For example, if your PIN is "4321" you would enter "432100".
Note
: in Malaysia you need to add the extra zeros at the beginning.
What if the ATM asks if my Wise card is debit or credit?
The Wise card is a debit card, so for most ATMs just select the
debit
option.
For ATMs in Singapore, Australia or New Zealand, debit might not be an option so you can select
credit
.
An ATM didn't give you your cash for an ATM withdrawal
Contact the ATM provider as they should be able to help with an error on their machine.
Learn more info here
You got charged the wrong rate
If you were charged the wrong rate when using your Wise card, it's likely because the ATM converted your money at a marked up exchange rate. To avoid that, you should let us handle the conversion.
Learn more info here

title: How much does it cost to withdraw cash with my Wise card?
content:
You can use your Wise card like any other bank card to withdraw money at ATMs around the world.
The exact fees you pay depend on where your card was issued — it’s usually the registered address on your Wise account.
How can I check what my withdrawal fees are?
Here’s how to check what your fees are, how much you can still withdraw for free, and when your free allowance limit resets on your account.
On the app
Go to the
Card
tab
Under the
Manage card
section, select
Your spending limits
View your account limits:
If you’re a personal customer:
In the
Account limits
section, you'll see your daily and monthly ATM withdrawal limits and when they'll refresh
If you’re a business customer:
Select
View account limits
in the
Account limits
section. You'll see your daily and monthly ATM withdrawal limits and when they'll refresh
4. Select
View ATM fees
to see your free allowance, and when it resets
On web
Go to the
Card
tab
Select
Your spending limits
View your account limits:
If you’re a personal customer:
Under
Account limits
, you'll see your daily and monthly ATM withdrawal limits and when they'll refresh.
If you’re a business customer:
Select
View account limits
in the
Account limits
section. You’ll be able to see your daily and monthly ATM withdrawal limits and when they'll refresh.
4. Select
View ATM fees
to see your free allowance, and when it resets
You can also view and edit your spending limits on your profile, under
Limits.
You can also check on our
pricing page
next to
Withdrawing money from an ATM
. Make sure to set the correct location from the top right corner of the page to see fees for your region.
Tips to save money when withdrawing cash
Limit the number of withdrawals you make — you can make 2 free withdrawals each month. After that, you’ll pay a fixed fee per withdrawal.
Limit the amount you withdraw — if you withdraw over a certain amount, you’ll be charged a variable fee which changes based on how much you’re withdrawing.
If the ATM asks to convert the money for you, we recommend saying no because they often charge an
unfair exchange rate
.
If the ATM asks to debit in your home currency or local currency, select local. For example, if you’re travelling in the US, select USD.
If you withdraw cash in a currency you're not currently holding, we'll charge a conversion fee to convert it for you.
Check our fees here
.
How do withdrawal fees work?
There are two types of fees we charge for ATM withdrawals, which are based on
how many times
you withdraw, and
how much
you withdraw.
How many times you withdraw
You get 2 free
withdrawals or can withdraw up to 200 EUR (or equivalent in local currency) per
calendar month. After your second withdrawal, we’ll charge a small fixed fee per withdrawal.
For example, if your card has a fixed fee of 0.50 EUR and you withdraw 10 EUR or 100 EUR the fixed fee would be 0.50 EUR either way.
We charge a fixed fee from the third withdrawal onwards — regardless of how much money you've withdrawn that month.
How much you withdraw
Each calendar month you have a free withdrawal allowance — you can withdraw money up to that amount without any variable fees.
A variable fee changes based on how much you withdraw. For example, if your card was issued in the EEA and has a 1.75% variable fee and you withdraw 100 EUR, then the variable fee would be 1.75 EUR.
We charge the variable fee for every withdrawal on your Wise card over your monthly allowance, regardless of how many times you made a withdrawal that month.
What counts towards my free withdrawal allowance?
If you have multiple cards on your account, the fees apply per account.
Wise withdrawal fees and third-party ATM fees are counted towards the withdrawal limits.
Unsuccessful withdrawal attempts may count towards your limits and free allowance.
If you've used up your free amount in one withdraw, the next one will be charged.
Why was I charged more fees by the ATM?
Some ATMs may charge additional fees over what we charge. We have no way of predicting those charges, but they’re usually visible on the ATM before you agree to the withdrawal.
Do my fees and limits change if I move to a new country?
If you change your registered address after moving to another country and order a new card, we’ll automatically update the limits for all your cards. They’ll reflect the limits that apply to your new country of residence.

title: An ATM didn't give me my cash
content:
In most cases, the ATM provider will know that there was an error, and the money will automatically be returned to your Wise account within a few minutes.
But, here’s what you can do if you tried to withdraw cash from an ATM, and it didn’t give you your money.
Collect as much information as possible
Before leaving the ATM, get receipts, take pictures, and note the transaction date, time, amount ATM code, and the operation number so that you can provide this info when it’s needed.
Contact the ATM provider
Every ATM has a contact number. Call them and tell them what happened along with providing the transaction and ATM details.
Check your Wise account activity
You can check on app or web to see if the withdrawal is marked as
pending
or
completed
.
If it’s
pending
,the money should automatically return to your account in 7 days.
If it’s completed and the ATM provider wasn't able to help,
submit a dispute here
so we can look into it for you.
Learn more about using your Wise card at ATMs

title: An ATM asked me to convert currencies when using my Wise card
content:
When you use an ATM or a card terminal, such as a card reader at a shop, it may ask if you want it to convert your money for you.
We recommend saying no, because most ATMs and card terminals will use an unfair exchange rate when converting for you.
When you convert between currencies with Wise, we’ll use the
mid-market exchange rate
so you always know exactly what you’re getting.
What should I choose if the ATM or card terminal asks to convert for me?
If the card terminal asks you to convert currencies, we suggest selecting "no".
If asked to debit in your home currency, or the local currency, choose the local currency. For example, if you’re travelling in Italy but you have a US Wise Multi-Currency Card, then you should select to spend in the local currency, EUR.
Here’s some examples of what you might see when using an ATM, and what we recommend choosing.
What the card terminal might ask
Our recommendation
"Continue with conversion” or “Continue without conversion”
“Continue without conversion”
Debit in your home currency or debit in the local currency
Debit in the local currency  (e.g. JPY in Japan or GBP in the UK)
"Let us convert the money for you — yes or no”
“No”
Confirm the exchange rate — yes or no”
“No”
Why should I let Wise convert for me instead of the ATM or card terminal?
Card machines often charge marked-up exchange rates.
We ran some research
and found that you’ll usually be charged 6%-13% more than if you withdraw without conversion.
If you let Wise do the conversion for you we’ll convert from the currency you have that has the lowest conversion fee.
Check our fees here
If you’ve already got that currency in your Wise account, you won’t be charged anything to convert.
Brazil cardholders
:
smart conversion
doesn't work when Brazilian Real (BRL) currency is involved.

title: Why was my Wise card declined?
content:
The fastest way to see why your card payment was declined is to check the transaction in your activity feed.
On the app or website:
Go to your
Home
page to see your transaction activity
Find and select the declined payment — you'll be able to see the reason it failed
Common reasons you might see in your account
Your card isn't activated
You need to activate your card before you can use it.
Learn how to activate your Wise card
.
There wasn't enough money in your account
Check your account to make sure you have enough money to cover the payment.
Your card is frozen
If your card is frozen, you'll need to
unfreeze it
before you can use it. You can do this in your Wise account.
You've reached my spending limits
There are limits to how much you can spend and withdraw. You can
check or change your limits
at any time in your account.
Chip and PIN payments aren't enabled
For some online or in-person payments, you need to enable Chip and PIN payments in your account.
Learn how to enable Chip and PIN payments
.
You entered the wrong PIN too many times
For security, if you enter the wrong PIN 3 times, we’ll block it and you won’t be able to use your card until you unblock it. Here's how you can unblock it:
Go to the
Card
tab
Select
Unblock PIN
You’ll now be able to use your card online. But to use contactless again with your card, you’ll need to make a payment using your chip and PIN first.
The ATM isn't compatible with your card
Some ATMs, particularly in the US, may only support magstripe payments.
Learn how to enable magstripe withdrawals
.
See
How you can use your Wise card at an ATM
.
Your contactless payment isn't working
If a contactless payment is declined, try using Chip and PIN for that transaction. This can reactivate contactless payments if they were blocked for security reasons.
You entered the wrong card details
The decline reason might say something like 'Incorrect CVV' or 'Invalid expiry date'. If it does, double-check your card details and try the payment again.
Your online payment was declined for a name or address mismatch (AVS)
For some online payments, the name and billing address you enter must exactly match the details on your Wise account. Make sure there are no typos or differences.
The payment was blocked for security
We sometimes block a payment to protect your account. If this happens and you recognise the transaction, please try again. If you don't recognise it,
freeze your card immediately and report the issue to us
.
Other possible issues
Your online payment failed the security check (3DS)
For some online payments, you'll be asked to approve the payment from your Wise app or with a code sent by SMS or a call. This is called a
3D Secure (3DS) payment
. If you don't approve it, the payment will fail.
Some websites only accept locally issued cards, so if your online payment is declined, this might be the reason.
You've recently moved to a new country
If you've updated your address to a new country, your old card may not work for certain payments. You might need to order a new card.
Check if cards are available in your country
.
You're spending in an unsupported country
There are a few countries where your Wise card won't work. If you're in one of these locations, you'll need to use a different card.
See the list of unsupported countries
.
The merchant doesn't accept your card
Occasionally, a merchant's payment processor may decline a payment even if you have enough money. If this happens, you'll need to use a different card.
Your card is physically damaged or won't read in terminals
If your card's chip is damaged, it may not work for in-person payments even if it works online. If this happens, you'll need to
order a replacement card
.
The declined transaction isn't showing in your activity
If a declined payment doesn't appear in your transaction feed, it means the request never reached us. This is usually due to a connection issue at the payment terminal. Please try the payment again or contact the merchant.
Not sure why it was declined?
Sometimes we have cases which lack in data on why transactions are being declined. But, we are always working to reduce these situations.
Help us by sharing details about your declined transactions
.

title: I have a pre-authorised transaction
content:
Businesses like hotels, gas stations, or car rental companies may charge a pre-authorisation when you make a purchase with your Wise card. This acts like a reservation that will change to the correct amount once the business knows how much you’re actually going to spend.
Here’s an example:
You go to a gas station to fill your car with gas. So you use your Wise card to pay. You see a charge on your account for 50 USD, but it only costs you 30 USD to fill your gas tank. After a few days, the 50 USD charge changes to 30 USD and the transaction’s status changes from pre-authorised to spent.
How long should I wait to be charged the correct amount?
Businesses will usually reverse a pre-authorised transaction and charge the correct amount within one day — but it can sometimes take 30 days.
If it’s been longer than that, get in touch with us — fill in
our dispute form
and send us the receipt showing the correct amount.

title: My Wise card transaction is still pending
content:
When you make a payment using your Wise card, the transaction might appear as pending at first. This is because transferring money from your account to the merchant goes through several steps and isn't always instant.
What’s a pending transaction?
A pending payment means that the merchant has reserved the money from your account, but hasn’t claimed it yet. For most payments, the money will be released back into your account within 7 days. When they do, the transaction will change from pending to completed.
Businesses like hotels, gas stations, or car rental companies may use
pre-authorisations
. This acts like a reservation that will change to the correct amount once the business knows how much you’re actually going to spend.
Services like food deliveries and car rental companies may also reserve a payment, let you tip after the service is completed, and then reverse your original pending payment and take the new amount.
Payment was completed but still shows as pending
Sometimes it takes longer for the merchant to claim the money from your account.
There’s nothing you need to do right now. Once the merchant claims the funds, the transaction will be completed.
If the merchant doesn't claim the funds, the money will be released back into your account within 7 days, or within 30 days for pre-authorisations.
Payment was cancelled but still shows as pending
Sometimes we can’t tell if a payment was cancelled. This might happen because of problems with the payment terminal or an internet connection. If this happens, the payment will stay as pending in your account.
It usually takes up to 7 days for the merchant to claim the money from your account, but for pre-authorisation, it could take up to 30 days. This might happen if your payment was for a booking deposit, car rental services or a hotel reservation.
Can you stop a pending transaction?
If a transaction is pending, neither you nor Wise can stop the claiming of the money. If you want to cancel the transaction, you’ll need to contact the merchant directly.
If they won’t let you cancel the transaction, or you think their behaviour could be fraudulent, you can
submit a dispute
.

title: How do I dispute a Wise card transaction?
content:
If you have a problem with a Wise card transaction, you can submit a dispute form. Here’s how:
1. Check the transaction status: Pending or Completed?
First, find the transaction in your Wise account activity list and check its status.
Pending:
The transaction is still being processed. We
can't
start a dispute while it's pending. Please wait until the status changes to
completed
. Most pending transactions complete (or automatically reverse) within
3–7 days
, but some can take longer
Completed:
The payment has been processed and left your account. You
can
dispute a completed transaction
2. Follow the steps for your situation:
Once the transaction is
completed
, decide which situation below matches your problem and follow the detailed steps.
I don't recognise this transaction at all
This means you're certain you (or someone you authorised) didn't make this payment.
Follow the steps:
Freeze your card immediately.
Go to the
Card
tab in the Wise app or web and freeze the card used. This stops any further unauthorised payments. We strongly recommend doing this
Double-check the merchant name.
Sometimes, the name shown (e.g. "Stripe" or a generic processor name) might be different from the shop or service you actually used. Think about recent online purchases or subscriptions
If you still don't recognise it, start the dispute process on app or web:
Go to your
Activity
list
Select the
completed transaction
you want to dispute
Tap the
?
icon (question mark symbol) located in the
top right corner
of the transaction details screen
Choose
I don't recognise this
Select
Report this transaction
to open the dispute form
Carefully follow the prompts in the form, providing all relevant details about why you don't recognise the transaction
Submit the completed form.
I made the transaction, but there's a problem
This applies if you recognise the merchant, but something went wrong. Common examples include:
You didn't receive the goods or services you paid for
You were charged for a subscription you had already cancelled
You were charged twice for the same thing
The amount charged was incorrect
An ATM didn't dispense the correct amount of cash, or any cash at all
You returned an item, but haven't received the promised refund
Follow the steps:
Contact the merchant first.
You
must
try to resolve the issue directly with them before disputing. Ask for a refund, correction, or other solution
Gather evidence.
Keep records of all communication with the merchant (emails, chat logs), receipts, invoices, proof of return tracking, photos of faulty items, or any relevant documents.
If the merchant doesn't respond, refuses to help, or doesn't resolve the issue satisfactorily after you've contacted them, start the dispute process on app or web:
Go to your
Activity
list
Select the
completed transaction
you want to dispute
Tap the
?
icon (question mark symbol) located in the
top right corner
of the transaction details screen
Choose
I don't recognise this
Select
Report this transaction
to open the dispute form
Carefully follow the prompts in the form, providing all relevant details about your specific issue with the merchant
Make sure to
upload the evidence
you gathered when prompted. This is crucial for merchant dispute.
Submit the completed form
Should I freeze my card?
For merchant disputes (when you made the transaction, but there's a problem), you usually
don't
need to freeze or cancel your card unless you suspect your card details have been compromised or the merchant might try to charge you again without authorisation.
3. What happens next?
Our disputes team will review your submission. We might contact you via email if we need more information or clarification.
Disputes involve
coordination
with card networks (Mastercard or Visa) and the merchant's bank, so they take time. The timeframe generally starts from
the date you submit the dispute form
.
Timeline for unrecognised transactions
:
Our investigation usually takes 1–2 business days, but can take up to 10 days
Timeline for issues with merchants
(when you made the transaction, but there's a problem):
Our initial investigation usually takes up to 30 business days
If the dispute is valid, the merchant has 30–45 days to respond
If the merchant rejects the refund, we may be able to escalate it further with MasterCard or Visa. This process can take around 2 months for Visa and 6 months (or more) for MasterCard to complete
We’ll email you as soon as there's an update on your case.
If your dispute is invalid, we won't be able to reverse the charge.
Learn more about invalid or declined disputes
Can I dispute only part of a transaction amount?
Yes. If you were overcharged or only need to dispute a portion of the total amount, explain this clearly in the details section when you fill out the dispute form.
What if the merchant already gave me a partial refund?
If the merchant issued a partial refund but you’re still owed more, you can dispute the remaining amount. When you fill out the dispute form, explain the situation and provide evidence of the partial refund you already received (like a bank statement showing the credit or a confirmation email from the merchant).

title: Why was my card dispute declined or invalid?
content:
If you've disputed a card transaction and it was declined or invalid, it’s likely because it doesn’t meet Visa or Mastercard’s chargeback criteria. In these cases, we won’t be able to reverse the charge.
This article covers what that means and what steps you can take next.
Learn more about disputing Wise card transactions
Common reasons why disputes get declined
Here are some of the most frequent reasons why a dispute may not be valid.
If any of these reasons apply, we won’t be able to dispute the transaction for you.
1.  You missed the deadline to dispute
Visa and Mastercard set time limits for raising disputes — usually 120 days from the transaction date.
2. You didn’t provide the required documentation
Some disputes require supporting documents, such as a copy of communication with a merchant, receipts, or proof of cancellation.
3. You changed your mind, but the merchant has a no-refund policy
If the product or service was delivered as described and you agreed to the merchant’s no-refund policy at checkout, then the transaction can’t be disputed.
4. You didn’t cancel a subscription before it renewed
Many merchants automatically renew subscriptions based on their terms. If you forgot to cancel it, this isn’t considered a valid dispute. However, you can try contacting the merchant to request a refund.
5. You claimed that an item wasn’t delivered, but it was shown as received
If the goods or services were delivered as agreed, but you claim they weren’t, the dispute may be considered invalid. Tracking details, delivery confirmations, or service logs from the merchant can be used to prove the transaction was completed.
6. You paid, but didn’t use the service
Chargebacks aren’t valid if the merchant provided the service as agreed, even if you chose not to use it. For example, if you booked a flight or hotel, but didn’t go, the charge still applies, unless the merchant’s policy allows refunds.
7. A temporary hold was placed on your card
Certain merchants, like fuel stations, hotels, and rental companies, pre-authorise a charge before finalising the transaction. If the final amount is correct, this isn’t a valid reason for a dispute.
Visa and Mastercard allow merchants to place these holds to confirm available funds, and they also permit adjustments to the final amount based on the actual cost of the service or purchase. For example, a hotel may place a hold for incidentals and later adjust the charge to reflect your final bill.
What can I do if my dispute was declined?
If your dispute wasn’t accepted because it was invalid, you might still be able to:
Reach out to the merchant again
– before opening a dispute, always try resolving the issue directly with the merchant. If your dispute was already declined, you can still try to reach another solution with them.
Contact a local consumer protection agency –
if you believe the merchant acted unfairly or violated consumer rights, your local consumer protection agency may be able to advise you on your rights and potential next steps. Some agencies can mediate disputes or take action against businesses engaging in deceptive practices.
For more information on invalid card disputes, read our blog article
Your Wise Card Transactions: Understanding Invalid Disputes
.
Still have questions?
If you're unsure whether your dispute qualifies,
get in touch with us through the Wise app or website
.

title: Contactless payments aren't working for my Wise card
content:
Contactless is a safe and easy way to pay. But if it’s not working, here are the most common ways to fix it.
If contactless suddenly stops working
For security, we occasionally ask you to use Chip and PIN to make sure it’s still you. This can happen after you’ve made several contactless payments in a row.
1. Make a Chip and PIN payment.
Insert your card into the payment terminal and enter your 4-digit PIN. This reactivates contactless payments.
2. Check your contactless setting is on.
If a Chip and PIN payment doesn’t help, check that contactless payments are enabled for your card in the app:
Go to the
Card
tab
Tap
Manage payment methods
Make sure
Contactless
is turned on
3. Check your spending limits.
If your payment is declined, you may have reached your daily or monthly limit for that payment type:
Go to the
Card
tab
Tap
Limits
to see your remaining limits
You can increase your limits here if you need to
Learn more about your card spending limits
If your transaction isn't showing in your activity
If a payment is declined but you don’t see the transaction in your
Wise activity
, it usually means there was a connection issue between the shop's payment terminal and Wise.
This isn’t a problem with your card or account. It’s best to try paying again or use a different payment terminal.
If both contactless and Chip & PIN payments are failing
If you can’t make any payments with your physical card, the card’s chip might be damaged.
To check if it's damaged,
try using your card at an ATM.
If it still doesn’t work at an ATM, your card is likely damaged and you’ll need to replace it.
Learn how to replace your Wise card
If you’re using Apple Pay or Google Pay
If contactless payments aren't working with your mobile wallet:
Make sure you have the latest version of the Wise app.
Try removing your card from your
Apple Pay
or
Google Pay
wallet and adding it again.
If you're using a new card for the first time
Before you can use contactless, you’ll need to activate your new card.
For cards issued in most regions:
Your first transaction must be a Chip and PIN payment.
For cards issued in the US, Japan, or the Philippines:
You must first activate your card using the 6-digit code that came with it. After that, you'll need to make one Chip and PIN payment to enable contactless.
Learn more about activating your Wise card
Good to know:
Cards issued in the US before December 16, 2019, don't have a contactless feature. You can still use them for contactless payments by adding them to a mobile wallet.
Still having trouble?
If you’ve tried all these steps and your card still isn’t working, please
get in touch with us
. We'll be happy to look into it for you.

title: My Wise card transaction isn't showing in my activity list
content:
There could be a number of reasons why your Wise card transaction isn’t showing up in your activities.
Here are some possible reasons, and what you can do.
Country incompatibility
The merchant (the service or company billing you) might not accept cards issued in a different country from where they're based. This is often the case in the US, where many businesses only accept US cards. If that's the case, you need to use a local card.
Online payments
If it was an online payment, there might have been a technical issue with the merchant. Try deleting your card details from their website,
troubleshooting your browser,
and making the payment again.
Authorisation fail
The shop or business you bought from never asked Wise to authorise the payment. Try the purchase again, or contact the merchant directly to sort out the situation. Wise won't be able to influence this transaction.
Offline terminal payments
If your payment was accepted, the terminal where you used your card might have been offline. This usually happens on an aeroplane or underground.
If you spend more money than you have on your account before the merchant captures this transaction, your account might go negative. When it happens, you need to top up your account to
keep using Wise
.

title: I've been charged multiple times for the same transaction
content:
Seeing multiple charges for a Wise card transaction can be worrying — but it doesn’t necessarily mean you’ll be charged multiple times.
Merchants might authorise multiple charges in error, or because of a technical problem.
In most cases, the money for duplicate transactions is sent back to your account within 7 days, so it may be worth waiting.
What you can do if you’ve been charged multiple times
First, check the status of your transaction:
From the Wise app or web, go to Home to see your transaction activity
Select the duplicate transactions to check their status
If all the transactions are pending
The money for the other pending transactions usually goes back to your account within 7 working days. There’s nothing you need to do, but if it’s been more than 7 days, please contact the merchant and ask them to cancel the duplicate transactions.
If one of the transactions is completed, and the others are pending
This means that the money for the completed transaction has reached the merchant. The money for all the pending transactions should be automatically returned to your account within 7 days.  There’s nothing you need to do, but if it’s been more than 7 days, please contact the merchant and ask them to cancel the duplicate transactions.
Learn more about pending transactions
If there are multiple completed charges for the same transaction
The money for all these transactions has been sent to the merchant that you paid so here’s what you can do:
Get in touch with them to ask for a refund. Most of the time, it was an error on their end, so they’ll be able to just send you your money back.
If they’re unwilling or unable to refund you, then you can ask for a refund through our
dispute form
.
If possible, send us screenshots and details of your communications with them. It will help us build a stronger case for you.
Learn more about disputing Wise card transactions

title: How long does a refund to my Wise card take?
content:
We know it’s important to get your money back quickly. When you're expecting a refund, the time it takes can depend on whether the original card payment was
completed
or is still
pending
.
It’s always a good idea to check the merchant’s refund policy first, as they control when the money is sent back to you.
If the payment was completed
Once the merchant sends the refund, it usually takes
5–10 working days
for the money to appear in your Wise account.
We can't see or track the refund until it arrives, but we’ll automatically add the money to your account as soon as we receive it.
Good to know:
You can also receive
partial refunds
. The process is the same.
Refunds can still be sent to a
cancelled or expired card
. The money will be credited to your Wise account as normal.
If you paid using a
mobile wallet
(like Apple Pay or Google Pay), the refund will also return to your Wise account automatically.
If you’ve been waiting longer than 10 working days, you should always
contact the merchant first
to confirm they've sent the refund. When you contact them, it’s a good idea to ask for a
proof of refund document
.
If the payment is still pending
When a payment is pending, the money is on hold but hasn't left your account. The merchant needs to release this hold, which is called a
reversal
.
The merchant can do one of two things to return the money:
Cancel the pending payment:
They can release the hold, and the money will be available in your account almost instantly.
Do nothing:
If they don't act, the money will be automatically returned to your account. This usually takes
7 days
, but can take up to
30 days
for payments like a security deposit, car rental booking, or an
ATM withdrawal
.
If you're waiting for a pending payment to be returned, you don't need to do anything. The money will come back to you automatically within the timeframes above.

title: How do I order a Wise card?
content:
It's very simple to order your Wise card. Let's start with some basic information:
Can I order the card?
Before ordering the card, make sure you live in a place where you can order it.
Here is a
list of eligible countries
. We can’t issue cards to people who don’t live in these countries. We’re working hard to expand and have our card available globally.
If you live in one of the eligible countries, here's what you need to do to order it:
1. Get a Wise account
Sign up
and open your first currency — you’ll be able to add more currencies afterwards.
Note: reviewing your application can take us a few days due to
verification
.
2. Order your Wise physical card
Go to the
Cards
tab and you'll see an option to order the card. We may ask you to pay a small fee for the card or add money to your account.
For personal customers in the EEA, Switzerland and UK you’ll have an option to order
an eco-friendly card
.
We'll check your personal details, like your name and address. We also need to
verify your account
. Once everything looks right, we’ll ship the new card to you.
Note: standard delivery is done via local post so there isn't a tracking number available unless you
speed up your card order
.
What we check is slightly different in the US and Brazil —
learn more about getting the Wise Multi-Currency Card in the US
and
in Brazil
Australian customers — you can read our
PDS/FSG
to learn more
For some regions you can
check your card details
before your card is delivered. Your card details are ready to be used for online purchases, and mobile wallet purchases
3. Delivery
Typically your card will arrive between 4 working days to 3 weeks, depending on where you live. For eligible countries you can pay an additional fee to speed up your card order and get a tracking number — also known as expedited delivery.
Expedited delivery is done by DHL or UPS. Once the card is produced, they’ll deliver it to you in person within 4 working days.
Country / Region
Standard Delivery
Expedited Delivery

7-14 working days
Up to 4 working days
Brazil
5-7 working days
Not available

Up to 14 working days
Up to 4 working days
EEA
Up to 14 working days
Up to 4 working days

7-10 working days
Up to 4 working days

Up to 5 working days
Up to 4 working days
New Zealand
7-10 working days
Up to 4 working days

5-7 working days
Up to 4 working days

3 working days
Not available

2-6 working days
Up to 4 working days
US

Up to 4 working days
Learn how to speed up your card order
Note that standard delivery is done via local post so there isn't a tracking number available unless you speed up your card order.
4. Activate your Wise card
For most regions, you can activate it simply by using it for a Chip and PIN payment. If you live in some of the regions where Chip and PIN activation is not available, you'll get an activation code.
Once you activate your card you can use it almost anywhere in the world.
Learn more about how to activate the Wise card
What if I want an additional card?
We only offer one physical card per user, and it must be issued in the name of the account holder, so if someone else in your family would like to have a card, they have to create their own account and go through the steps above.
You can order a digital card instead — it’s great for a more secure spending experience, you can get up to 3 at any one time, and you can replace them any time you want to get new card details. Check whether cards are currently available
in your region
.
Learn how to get a Wise digital card

title: How do I activate my Wise card?
content:
For most customers, there's no separate activation step for your Wise card —
you just need to make your first payment using Chip and PIN.
If your card was issued in
Australia, Brazil, Japan, New Zealand, Philippines, Singapore, or the US
you can also activate it by using the 6-digit PIN that we provide you.
How do I activate my card with the 6-digit PIN?
Go to the
Cards
tab
If you have other Wise cards, you may need to swipe to the right to select your new card.
Choose
Activate your card
Enter the 6-digit code that’s on the paper letter that your card came attached to.
What if my Chip and PIN payment gets declined?
If your Chip and PIN payment gets declined, you can activate your card at an ATM by doing a balance inquiry or cash withdrawal.
You can use the
Mastercard ATM locator
or
Visa ATM locator
to find an ATM near you.
For Canada or Singapore cardholders
, if you're trying to activate your card at an ATM, you won't be able to make a cash withdrawal. Try a Chip and PIN payment or choose a balance inquiry in order to activate your card.
For Brazil cardholders
, try a Chip and PIN payment or cash withdrawal at an ATM to activate your card.
I ordered my card before September 2024 but didn't get a 6-digit PIN
If you ordered your card from Australia, Brazil, New Zealand or Singapore you won't have a 6-digit activation PIN. You can only activate your card using Chip and PIN or at an ATM.

title: How can I change my Wise card delivery address?
content:
Here’s how to change the address your Wise card is delivered to.
If you've already ordered your card
When the delivery estimate has passed
From the
Card
tab, select
Order a card
Select
Physical card
Follow the instructions
Your original card will be cancelled and you won’t be able to activate it.
When the delivery estimate hasn't passed yet
From the
Card
tab, select your card order
Select
More info
>
Edit
delivery details
Select
Order a new card
and enter the new delivery address
Follow the instructions to complete the order (there may be a small fee to change the delivery address).
Your original card will be cancelled and you won’t be able to activate it.
If you didn't finish your card order
You might have started your card order, but didn't finish it. If this is the case:
From the
Card
tab, select your card order
Select
Edit address
Edit
your delivery address and follow the instructions
This will complete the order, and your card will be sent to you.

title: Can I get my new card delivered abroad?
content:
Due to the Canada Post strike standard card deliveries to Canada are temporarily unavailable.
Due to recent changes in the U.S. regulations concerning international delivery, the United States is temporarily unavailable as a delivery address for cards from the EU, UK, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, and the Philippines.
You may need to order a card to an address you don't live in for a number of reasons. If you're travelling and lose your card, for example, international delivery might be an option for you. It's available for most regions where we provide the Wise card.
Brazil
International delivery isn't available at the moment.
Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Japan and Singapore
Personal and business cards have international delivery as an option for the first and replacement orders.
Europe and UK
For the first order, personal cards can be delivered in Europe only. Replacement cards can be delivered internationally only if the customer had a previously activated EU/UK card.
Business cards have international delivery as an option for the first and replacement orders.
Malaysia and Philippines
Personal cards have international delivery as an option for the first and replacement orders.
US
Delivery is available only within the US for personal and business customers. Due to recent changes in US regulations concerning international delivery, the United States is temporarily unavailable as a delivery address for cards from the EU, UK, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, and the Philippines.

title: Can I speed up my card order?
content:
For eligible countries you can pay an additional fee to speed up your card order and get a tracking number — also known as expedited delivery.
How do I speed up my card order?
To receive your card faster you have to request the expedited delivery method while ordering  the card.
Typically your card will arrive between 4 working days to 3 weeks, depending on where you live. You'll need to be available to accept the delivery in person.
Check how long delivery usually takes
We can't expedite delivery:
after the order is placed — you can only expedite while ordering your card
to PO boxes
to unsupported countries
What countries are eligible?
You can speed up your order of your first-time or replacement card(s) if you're in
Australia, Canada, EEA (excluding Iceland), Japan, Malaysia, New Zealand, Philippines, UK
or the
US
If you ordered your card in
Singapore
you can speed up the card order if the delivery address is outside Singapore.
Brazil
is not currently eligible for expedited delivery.
How does expedited delivery work?
Expedited delivery is done by DHL or UPS. Once the card is produced, they’ll deliver it to you in person within 4 working days.
If you’re not at the delivery address to collect your card, they’ll try again one more time.
If you’re not around to collect your card on their second try, DHL will send the card back to us and we’ll destroy it.
We can’t offer a refund for the cost of delivery in this case.
How much does it cost?
It depends where your card is being sent to. We’ll show you the additional fee when you’re ordering.
Will I get a tracking number?
Yes, you’ll get a tracking number by email or SMS if you choose to speed up your card order. And you can track your card on DHL or UPS’s website.
Remember to add your contact information on DHL’s website to be notified about the delivery.
Can I change my delivery address or schedule it?
You can change your delivery address,
read more about it
.

title: What if my Wise card hasn't arrived?
content:
If you're waiting for your physical card, the first thing to do is check the delivery estimate and address in your Wise account.
You can still use your digital card for online spending and with mobile wallets while you wait for the physical card to arrive.
How to check your delivery status and address
Go to the
Cards
tab in your account (on the app or website)
Select the card you're waiting for
You'll see the estimated delivery date and the delivery address listed
About delivery tracking
We use local post for most standard deliveries, so we can't provide a tracking number.
If you paid for expedited delivery, or if your card is being delivered to
Brazil, Malaysia, or the Philippines
, you'll find a tracking number in the app.
What if your address is wrong?
If your card hasn't been shipped yet, you may be able to edit your address. Go to your card order in the
Cards
tab and select
Edit address.
If your card has already been shipped, you can change the delivery address by following the same steps for a small fee. This is because we'll cancel the old card and order a new one, so there’s a card production fee to cover the costs.
Exception:
If you chose expedited delivery to the US or Canada with UPS, you may be able to change the address yourself by using
UPS MyChoice
.
What should you do if the delivery date has passed?
Postal services can sometimes have small delays. Please wait
5 extra working days
after the estimated delivery date for your card to arrive.
If your card still hasn't arrived after 5 extra working days, you should order a new card.
How to order a new card
Go to the
Cards
tab in your account
Select the card you were waiting for
Select
Card not arrived? Order a new one
on the web, or
I didn’t get my card
on the app
Follow the on-screen steps to confirm your delivery address. This automatically cancels your original card to keep your money safe
If your card was lost in the post, your first replacement is free.
Normal delivery times
Delivery times are estimates in working days. Working days are
Monday to Friday,
and don't include weekends or public holidays.

title: How do I replace or cancel my Wise card?
content:
You can replace a lost, stolen, or damaged Wise card, or cancel a card you no longer need, directly from the Wise app or website.
How to replace your card
You can order a new card for a small replacement fee.
Go to the
Card
tab on the website or app
Select the card you want to replace
Choose the
Replace card
option
Follow the on-screen instructions
You’ll be asked to choose a reason for the replacement. Please select one of the following options:
I lost my card
: Freeze your card to protect your account. This is temporary. If you find your card, you can unfreeze it
My card was stolen
: We’ll permanently cancel your card to prevent anyone from using it. You won't be able to reactivate it
My card isn't working
: Your current physical card will continue to work for online payments and mobile wallets (like
Apple Pay
or
Google Pay
). Keep in mind that replacing your physical card won't affect your digital one. You can still keep using it until you activate the new card
Once you confirm your address, your new card will be on its way.
What's the fee for replacing your card?
We charge a small fee to replace your card. You can check the exact fee for your region on our
pricing page
.
There's no fee if:
Your card is expiring within 6 months
Your card was damaged when it arrived
Your card never arrived
The card has been stolen and you have a police report to send over
Delivery times
Delivery times depend on your region and are estimates in working days. Working days are
Monday to Friday
, and don't include weekends or public holidays.
Country / Region
Delivery Times
Australia
7-14 days
Brazil
within 5 days
Canada
Within 14 days
EEA
Within 14 days
Japan
7-10 days
Malaysia
3-5 days
New Zealand
7-10 days
Philippines
5-7 days
Singapore
3 days
UK
2-6 days

14-21 days
Check what to do if the delivery date has passed
What happens when your card expires?
You’ll need to order a new card from your Wise account, under the
Cards
tab. We don’t automatically send a new one to you.
How to cancel your Wise card
To cancel your physical Wise card, you'll need to
get in touch with us
.
Learn more on how to delete your digital card(s)

title: How do I replace or delete my Wise digital cards?
content:
You can get new card details up to 3 times a day. When you generate a new set of card details, your old ones will stop working:
To replace your digital card(s):
Go to
Cards
Select the digital card you want to replace
Select
Replace card
You can delete your digital card(s) at any time.
To delete your digital card(s):
Go to
Cards
Select the digital card you want to delete
Select
Delete card
Can I still get refunds to my digital card if I replace or delete it?
Even if your card details were replaced or deleted, you can still receive a refund to your account.
If the merchant asks for the last four digits of the card, you can find this on the app. We don't currently have it on the web.
From
Home
, select the transaction you want the refund for
Find the last 4 digits next to “Card ending in”
Once the merchant sends your refund, it typically takes 5–10 working days for the money to arrive in your account.

title: How can I change my name on my Wise card?
content:
Before you complete your card order
Personal cards
When you order your card, we'll show you a list of suggestions based on the name on your Wise account that you can choose from.
Please note:
Your card needs to have the first name and last name that closely match your Wise account
If your name is longer than 22 characters (including spaces), parts of it will be abbreviated with initials, or removed
If there are any characters that we can’t print on the card, we’ll automatically convert them to their Latin equivalent
Business cards
The same name rules from personal cards apply to business cards. But the card will have a different design than the personal card and will have
business
written on it. If the name is longer than 22 characters (including spaces), it won’t be printed on the card.
After you complete your order
Once your card order is completed, you won’t be able to change the name on your card.

title: What do I need to do to get my Wise card in Brazil?
content:
You can get a Wise card in Brazil any time — here are the steps you'll need to go through to receive your card.
Read more on Wise cards in Brazil
1. Verify your ID
Like your bank, Wise is a regulated financial institution. To comply with local regulations, the Central Bank, and to honour our banking partner agreements, we need to verify a valid Brazilian identification document from you.
So before any customers order a debit card in Brazil, you'll need to send us either one of these:
a national ID issued less than 15 years ago
Brazilian passport
national driving license
RNE
2. Add BRL to your account
We'll ask you to add the BRL currency and add some money to it
. Ordering your Wise card is free and the amount that you add is yours to spend at any time.
Check our pricing
3. Set up your PIN
You’ll get to set your own 4-digit PIN when you order your card.
Read more on activating your card
4. Track your order
You’ll get an email from us once you’ve completed the steps above and your order is ready. You’ll also get an email with a delivery estimate from
Loggi
or
Mobi
, so you can track your card.
Please bear in mind the card will only be delivered if someone is there to receive it. After you finish the order, you can already start using your card details online or with your mobile wallet, such as
Apple Pay
or
Google Pay
, before you get your physical card.
You can also
check your card details
on the app or web any time.

title: How do I use my Wise card with Google Pay?
content:
This article covers where you can use the Wise card with Google Pay, how to set up, and how to use it. For general information about how to use or manage your cards on Google Pay,
visit the Google Pay Help website
.
Can I add my Wise card to Google Pay?
At the moment, you can add your card to Google Pay if the card was issued in Austria, Australia, Belgium, Brazil, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Guernsey, Hungary, Ireland, Isle of Man, Italy, Jersey, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, the UK, and the US.
It’s not currently available if your card was issued in Japan.
How to add your card to Google Pay
You can do it on either the Wise app or from the Google Wallet.
Wise app
Go to the
Cards
tab
Select the card you'd like to add
Select
Add to Google Pay
Google Wallet
Open your Google Wallet
Select
Add to Wallet
Select
Payment card
and then
New credit or debit card
Scan your card or enter the details manually
Follow the steps to verify your card
Once you’ve added your Wise card to Google Pay, you can make payments wherever you see these symbols.
After you add your card to Google Pay, you might see a small charge on your account — this is just to check that your card works. It's usually 0.00, but if they charged anything, they’ll refund it soon.
Withdrawing from ATMs
You can withdraw with your Google Pay from any ATM where there's a contactless symbol.
More info here
Transaction limits
You’ll have the same limits as normal.
View your Wise card limits here
.
If your card was issued in the US, transactions made with Google Pay count towards your contactless limit.

title: How do I use my Wise card with Apple Pay?
content:
This article covers where you can use the Wise card with Apple Pay, how to set up and how to use it. For general information on Apple Pay,
visit Apple Pay Support
.
Can I add my Wise card to Apple Pay?
Yes, if your card was issued in Australia, Brazil, Canada, an EEA country where it’s supported, Malaysia, New Zealand, Singapore, the US or the UK, you can add your card to Apple Pay.
At the moment, Wise cards issued in Japan don’t work with Apple Pay. We’re working to change that.
How to add your card to Apple Pay
You can do it on either the Wise app or from the Apple Wallet.
Wise app
Go to the
Cards
tab
Select the card you'd like to add
Select
Add to Apple Wallet
Apple Wallet
Open your Apple Wallet
Select the plus sign
Select the Wise card from the Wise app
Verify the card with an SMS code
After you add your card to Apple Pay, you might see a small charge on your account — this is just to check that your card works. It's usually 0.00, but if they charged anything, they’ll refund it soon.
Read here if you run into any issues
Withdrawing from ATMs with Apple Pay
You can withdraw with your Apple Pay from any ATM where there's a contactless symbol.
More info here
Transaction limits
You’ll have the same limits as normal.
View your Wise card limits here
.
If your card was issued in the US, transactions made with Apple Pay count towards your contactless limit.

title: How do I use my Wise card with Samsung Pay?
content:
This article covers where you can use the Wise card with Samsung Pay, how to set up and how to use it. For general information about how to use Samsung Pay,
visit the Samsung Support website
.
Can I add my Wise card to Samsung Pay?
You can add your Wise card to Samsung Pay, unless it was issued in Japan, Philippines or New Zealand. If you have a Mastercard issued in Japan, you can use it in the UK.
How to add your card to Samsung Pay
Open the Samsung Pay app
Select the
+
icon in the top right corner
Select
Add payment card
Scan your card or enter the details manually
Verify your card with an SMS code or via the Wise app
After you add your card to Samsung Pay, you might see a small charge on your account — this is just to check that your card works. It's usually 0.00, but if they charged anything, they’ll refund it soon.
Read here if you're having trouble adding your card
Transaction limits
You’ll have the same limits as normal.
View your Wise card limits here
.
Some merchants might also apply the contactless limit — in the UK, this is 30 GBP.

title: How do I use my Wise card with Garmin Pay or Fitbit Pay?
content:
This article covers where you can use the Wise card with Garmin Pay and Fitbit Pay and how to set up and how to use them. For general information visit
the Garmin Support Center
or
Fitbit Help
.
Can I add my Wise card to Garmin Pay or Fitbit Pay?
You can add your Wise card to Garmin Pay or Fitbit Pay if your card was issued in in Australia, the EEA, the UK, New Zealand, Singapore, or Switzerland.
After you add your card to either Garmin or Fitbit Pay, you might see a small charge on your account — this is just to check that your card works. It's usually 0.00, but if they charged anything, they’ll refund it soon.
Garmin Pay
Open the Garmin Connect app
Select
Garmin Pay
and then the 3 dots
Select
Add card
Follow the on-screen instructions to finish setting up your card
Once you’ve added your card, choose it on your watch to make a payment with it.
Fitbit Pay
With your watch nearby, open the Fitbit app
Select the
Account
icon, and then your device image
Select the
Wallet
Follow the on-screen instructions to add a payment card
If you’re setting up Fitbit Pay for the first time, you’ll have to set up a 4-digit PIN for your watch, and have Touch ID or a PIN enabled for your phone.
Read here if you're having trouble adding your card
Transaction limits
You’ll have the same limits as normal.
View your Wise card limits here
.
Some merchants might also apply the contactless limit — in the UK, this is 30 GBP.

title: I’m having trouble adding my Wise card to a mobile wallet
content:
If you’re having trouble adding your Wise card to a mobile wallet like Apple Pay or Google Pay, the problem is usually related to eligibility or a missed step in the process.
This guide will help you check if you're eligible and show you how to add your card correctly.
Step 1 — Check if your card is eligible
To add your card to a mobile wallet, you need to have:
A Wise card issued in a supported country
An
active
Wise card (it can't be frozen, blocked, or cancelled)
The latest version of the Wise app installed
A device that supports the mobile wallet you want to use
Supported Wallets and Countries
