Step 2 — How to add your card to a mobile wallet
The easiest way to add your card is from the Wise app. Don't try to add it by typing the details into your mobile wallet app directly.
Open the Wise app
Go to the
Card
tab and select the card you want to add
Tap on
Add to Apple Wallet
or
Add to Google Pay
Follow the steps as per links on the table to complete the process. We'll send a push notification to verify you.
Have you replaced your card?
If you've ordered a replacement card, remember to remove your old, cancelled card from your mobile wallet first. The details for the new card won't update automatically.
Step 3 — Still having trouble? Try these solutions
If you've confirmed you're eligible but are still running into issues, here are some common fixes.
Why do I see a "Card not added" or "Contact your card issuer" error?
This usually means there's an issue with your address, personal details, or the way you're adding the card.
Check your billing address:
Make sure the address on your Wise account matches the billing address in your Google Pay or Apple ID account.
Did you add the card from the Wise app?
Errors like this, or a message asking you to "Call Wise" for verification, can happen if you try to add the card manually from your phone's wallet. Please make sure you follow the instructions in
Step 2
.
What if my physical card was just issued?
If you've just ordered a physical card, you may need to
activate it
first before you can add it to a mobile wallet.
What if I'm not receiving the verification code?
We verify your card by sending a push notification to the Wise app or an SMS code. If you're not receiving it:
Make sure you have
notifications enabled
for the Wise app in your phone's settings.
Check that your phone number is correct in your Wise profile.
If you're a US customer without a US phone number, choose the option to receive the code by
email
instead.
If you've tried everything and still can't add your card, please
get in touch with us
for more help.

title: My Wise card was lost or stolen
content:
We know how worrying it can be if your Wise card is lost or stolen, but here's what you can do if that happens.
You can temporarily freeze your card to prevent anyone from using it, or you can replace it to get a brand new card.
If you never received your card in the post,
check here for more info
.
Freezing your card
While your card is frozen, you won’t be able to use it to make any transactions. You can instantly freeze or unfreeze your card from the
Cards
tab in your account.
Check here for more info on how to freeze your card
If you had any pending transactions before your froze your card, they would still go through.
We recommend freezing your card if:
you lost your card, but you’ll think you’ll find it again soon e.g. you lost it in your home
there’s
a transaction you don’t immediately recognise
, but you want to investigate it more e.g. a pending charge from a petrol station or subscription service.
you plan on just not using your card for a while e.g. you only use it while travelling
Replacing your card
If you replace your card, your old card will be permanently blocked. There will be no way to unblock it. You can replace your card from the
Cards
tab in your account.
Check here for more info on how to replace your card
We recommend replacing your card if:
your card was stolen, damaged or lost permanently
you left it in a public place where someone could have copied your card details, but you recovered it later e.g. you left your card at a bar, but then got it back the next day.
Replacement cards take 2-21 days to be delivered. The timeframe depends on your region.
More info here
What should I do if I notice an unauthorised transaction?
You must let us know immediately if you don't recognise a transaction through our
reporting form here
.
If you can’t login,
contact us
right away to report the unauthorised transaction.

title: How do I freeze or unfreeze my Wise card?
content:
If you’ve misplaced your card, noticed a transaction you don't recognise, or just want to pause spending, you can freeze your card any time to keep your money safe.
Freezing your card is a temporary measure. While it's frozen, you can't use it for payments, but your account and currencies will still work as normal.
How to freeze your card
On the web:
Go to the
Cards
tab
Select
Freeze card
On the app:
Go to the
Card
tab
Select
Freeze card
How to unfreeze your card
To unfreeze your card, simply follow the same steps above and select
Unfreeze card
.
What should I do after freezing my card?
If you suspect fraud or see an unrecognised transaction,
you should also report the transaction and then cancel your card
Learn how to report a fraudulent payment
If your card was lost or stolen,
you should replace it. This will cancel your old card and allow you to order a new one
Learn how to replace a lost or stolen card
What happens when my card is frozen?
When your card is frozen:
You
can't
make any new payments (in-store, online, or with a mobile wallet)
You
can't
withdraw money from an ATM
Any recurring payments or subscriptions you have set up
will be declined
Keep in mind that
pending
and
offline
transactions
will still be processed. For example, if you freeze your card after the payment was initiated/authorised, it will go through.
But, you can still:
Receive money into your Wise account
Send money from your currencies to others
Why can't I unfreeze my card?
If you can't see the option to
Unfreeze card
, it's because we've had to
restrict
it for security reasons. This can happen if we detect suspicious activity.
When your card is restricted, it's also frozen, and you won't be able to unfreeze it yourself. Please
contact us
so we can help you.

title: I didn't get what I paid for with my Wise card
content:
Here’s what you can do if you paid for goods or services with your Wise card, but had to wait longer than expected or received something different:
Contact the seller
As a first step it's best to contact the seller directly and try to resolve the issue. Sometimes if there are delays or something went wrong with the received goods, they’ll try to make it right for you. This is usually the easiest and fastest way to work it out.
Collect information
If they aren't cooperative or cannot help you, we’ll need the following information:
Receipt, email order, invoice or confirmation for the purchase
Detailed description of what the purchase was for and how the goods or service wasn't delivered as expected.
If it was different, let us know how it was different than you expected
If it was late, let us know when it was supposed to be delivered and when you actually received it.
Detailed description of what you’ve done so far, such as
screenshots of your attempts to contact the seller and their replies
if you returned the goods or service, provide a confirmation return with the date you returned it.
Screenshots and photos are always helpful for the investigation.
File a dispute
Lastly, once you have all the information
file a dispute with us
so we can look into it for you. For non-deliveries please wait at least 15 days from the date you made your order before filing a dispute.
If you’ve been scammed, you can file your dispute immediately.
Learn more about reporting fraudulent payments
.
Next steps
Once you filed the dispute, we’ll reply to you within 30-45 days. This timeframe is due to Visa and Mastercard policies and procedures. We can’t speed up that time, but we promise to send you any updates as soon as we receive them.

title: How do I report a fraudulent Wise card payment?
content:
If you see a Wise card payment in your account that you didn't make, it's important to act quickly. Here are the steps to take:
1. Freeze your card first
This is the most important first step to stop any further payments from being taken.
How do I freeze or unfreeze my Wise card?
2. Check if the payment is definitely fraudulent
Sometimes, payments can look suspicious but are actually legitimate. Before reporting a payment as fraud, quickly check if it might be one of these common situations:
Is it still pending?
Has the money actually left your account, or is the transaction status
pending
?
Pending means the merchant has reserved the funds, but hasn't claimed the final amount yet.
Wise can't stop a pending payment or release the held funds faster
— only the merchant can do this. Learn more about
pending payments
.
This often happens with
petrol stations, hotels, or car rentals
, which pre-authorise (hold) an estimated amount temporarily.
The correct amount is usually charged within a few days (sometimes longer depending on the merchant), and any extra held amount is automatically released.
Do you recognise the merchant's name?
Sometimes the registered business name is different from the shop name you know. Try searching online for the name listed on the transaction. Check your email receipts for clues too.
Could it be a subscription?
Check if it's a recurring payment for a service you signed up for, even if you forgot about it. If you want to cancel,
contacting the merchant directly is usually the fastest way
to stop future payments.
Was it an offline payment?
Payments made during a flight, at underground ticket machines, or at food trucks might be processed with a delay and appear in your activity later than expected.
Did someone you know use your card?
Could a friend, partner, or family member have used your card details, perhaps for a shared service?
If the payment isn't one of the above and you're sure you didn't authorise it, then it might be fraudulent. Please proceed to the next step.
3. Report the payment to Wise
If you've checked and are sure the payment is fraudulent, report it directly in the Wise app or on the web:
Go to
Home
to see your activity list
Find and select the transaction you want to report
Select
Get help with this transaction
Choose the option
I think this payment was fraudulent
Follow the on-screen instructions to submit your report. Provide as much detail as possible.
4. Cancel the affected card and order a replacement
After reporting the transaction, you
must cancel the card
that was used fraudulently to keep your account secure. You can then order a new card right away.
See
How do I replace or cancel my Wise card?
What happens after I report the payment?
Investigation:
Our specialist team will carefully investigate your case. This might involve checking technical information about the payment and potentially contacting the merchant
Updates:
We'll keep you informed about the progress via email
Timeline:
We aim to provide an outcome within
10 working days
for most card fraud cases. However, complex situations involving merchant responses might take longer in specific regulated cases
Outcome:
We'll email you the final result of our investigation. If we confirm the payment was fraudulent and unauthorised, we’ll process a refund back to your account according to card scheme rules (like Visa or Mastercard).
Learn more about
what happens when you report a Wise card payment
.
Important:
This article is for reporting fraudulent
Wise card
payments only. If you suspect fraud related to your Wise account or Wise transfers (not card payments), check
I may be the victim of fraud or a scam
instead.
If you'd like to file a complaint in the US, you can find state contact information on
Wise's licensing page
.

title: I don't recognise a subscription on my Wise card
content:
If you spot an unwanted subscription on your Wise card, it’s best to get in touch directly with the merchant who charged you so they can cancel the subscription or refund you.
I was charged for a subscription that I already cancelled
First get in touch with the merchant and ask for a refund. If they can't help, you can
cancel your subscription
from your Wise account.
Tips to avoid unwanted subscription charges
Since it takes time to investigate, you can usually cancel your subscription faster if you contact the merchant directly.
Always pay attention to the
Terms of Use
you’re accepting. If they say that you’ll be signed up for a subscription and you tick the “I agree” box, you might be charged.
If you sign up for a free trial, set a reminder on your phone for a few days before it ends. That way you can cancel in time before you’re charged for a full subscription.
I don’t recognise a subscription
Check if you subscribed to any services recently. You’ll usually have an email confirmation from the merchant. Sometimes the merchant name will be slightly different or even under an entirely different name, such as with discreet billing.
It can help to Google search the merchant name so you can see who it belongs to. These merchants usually have a website, which you can go to to cancel the subscription.
Check if you signed up for any free trials recently. Free trials can automatically become a paid subscription after a set amount of time — especially if you gave them your card number when signing up.
Here are a few common subscription services:
Apple.com
—
Apple.com/bill
appears on your billing statement when you buy an app, music, movie, or other content from Apple. It also appears when a subscription renews, or when a family member buys apps or content from Apple.
Find out more about this on Apple's support page
Amazon Prime
– if you see a charge on your account from Amazon Prime/UK Prime, this means you’ve signed up for an Amazon Prime subscription. Contact Amazon customer support to manage this subscription or
click here
to find out more about cancelling it.
eDreams Prime
— eDreams Prime is a service offered by eDreams. It often starts as a free trial that renews automatically.
You can get in touch with their support
to end the subscription and request a refund.
Discreet billing
—if you see a subscription charge from a merchant you don’t recognise, it could be an adult content or online dating subscription. Often they’ll use a different name which will show up on your account. This is called discreet billing.

title: How do I keep my Wise card safe?
content:
At Wise, protecting your money is a top priority. Sometimes we may decline a transaction or ask additional questions before allowing a transaction. Here’s why that happens, how fraud works, and what you can do to stay safe.
Most common frauds and how to stay protected
Online payment fraud
What it means:
Somebody uses your card details to make online purchases.
How it happens:
A merchant you used in the past may have had a data breach, or their website may be infected with malware.
How to protect yourself:
Research new merchants before giving them your card number (a quick online search is often enough to check if they’re reputable).
Enable push notifications
in the Wise app so you notice unusual activity right away.
Use a digital card
for online payments.
Disable payment methods you don’t intend to use in
Card controls
.
Scams
What it means:
You’re tricked into sending money under false pretenses.
Common examples:
Impersonations of your bank, police, or the IRS; fake investment opportunities, romance scams, urgent payment requests.
Learn more about common signs of scam
How to recognize a potential scam:
It offers a deal that’s too good to be true and creates fear of missing out (e.g. investments with guaranteed returns, online jobs with low effort and high pay).
It creates a sense of urgency (claiming your money is at risk or that the police/IRS are after you).
It appears as a social media advertisement claiming celebrity endorsement (e.g. Taylor Swift, Martin Lewis).
You’re contacted via WhatsApp or Telegram and asked to “confirm your card details,” often with references to recent reservations or deliveries.
You’re asked not to talk about it with friends or family.
Account takeover
What it means:
A fraudster gains access to your Wise account.
How it happens:
You fall victim to
phishing
and unknowingly give someone else access to your Wise account.
How to protect yourself:
Avoid clicking on links received via email or SMS, even if they look official.
Always check the sender to make sure they’re legitimate.
If in doubt, contact the sender directly to verify that they actually reached out to you.
Physical card fraud
What it means:
Your card is stolen or cloned.
How it happens:
Someone looks over your shoulder while you’re entering your PIN and then steals your card.
A replica of your card is created when you insert or swipe it in a terminal with malicious hardware (a “skimmer”) installed.
How to protect yourself:
Never keep your PIN with your card, and make sure nobody sees you entering it.
Avoid using terminals that look bulkier than usual, have loose parts, or have unusually stiff PIN pads.
Always keep your card under your control. Don’t hand it to waiters or store clerks — tap or insert it yourself instead.
Commonly asked questions
Why didn’t Wise ask me for additional confirmation when the payment was made (also known as 3D Secure)?
The request for additional confirmation can only be initiated by the merchant, not by Wise. If you’d like to decline transactions that don’t use 3D Secure, you can configure this in
Card controls.
Somebody else used my card details — did Wise have a data leak?
No. Most card detail leaks happen at merchants that don’t have sufficient safeguards in place or whose systems are infected with malware.
My card was compromised. Is my Wise account also vulnerable?
In cases of simple card compromise, your Wise account remains safe. However, if you see bank transfers you didn’t set up, clicked suspicious links, or accidentally approved login requests, go to
Security and privacy
settings and
secure your account immediately
.
What should I do if I see card transactions I didn’t make?
First, ask yourself:
Is this a subscription I forgot I signed up for?
Did I legitimately use this merchant, but don’t recognize the most recent charge?
If
yes
— contact the merchant to request a refund or clarification on the charge.
If
no
—
report the transaction as fraudulent.

title: Setting transaction controls for your Wise card
content:
You can manage the types of transactions allowed on your card by setting card controls. By using these, you can customise your card’s security and usage to fit your needs. You can set different controls for different cards.
To manage your card controls, go to the
Cards
tab and select
Card controls
.
List of controls
Card Control
Details
Online payments
Turning off this control will block all payments made over the internet, including online purchases and subscriptions.
Magnetic stripe
Turning off this control will block all payments made using the magnetic stripe on the back of your physical card. This includes in-store purchases where you swipe your card and ATM withdrawals where the terminal reads the magnetic stripe instead of the chip.
Contactless
Turning off this control will block all payments made by tapping your physical card. This includes in-store purchases and withdrawing money from a contactless ATM.
Chip transactions
Turning off this control will block all payments where you insert your card into a terminal and it reads the chip to authorise the payment. This includes in-store purchases and ATM withdrawals.
Mobile wallet
Turning off this control will block all payments made by tapping your mobile phone or other smart devices on a terminal. This includes in-store purchases and withdrawing money from a contactless ATM.
Cash withdrawals
Turning off this control will block all cash withdrawals made using your physical card or mobile wallet at an ATM.
Non-3D secure Payments
Turning off this control will only allow online payments that require an extra layer of security, which you approve through the Wise app or via your mobile wallet.
Overseas Payments
Turning off this control will restrict the use of your physical card or mobile wallet to the country where you ordered the card. However, you can still shop online with this control disabled.

title: What happens when you report a Wise card payment
content:
If you have an issue with a payment on your Wise card,
report it to us
and we’ll do our best to help.
Issues we can help with are:
Payments you believe to be fraudulent.
Payments you didn’t make or don’t recognise.
Refunds, when you haven’t been able to get in touch, or get help from the seller.
When you report an issue with a payment, it raises a dispute that we review before taking it to Mastercard or Visa. We can only do this once for each payment, so it's important we make sure we have all the details they'll ask for.
Once you've reported a payment, you can view the dispute status any time by navigating to the transaction you disputed.
What to expect and how long it takes
1.
We review the information
If you suspect fraud, we’ll assess all the evidence to confirm this. If it’s an issue with a seller, we’ll check that the details you’ve given us meet the rules for getting a refund.
Once we’ve confirmed what type of dispute it is, we’re able to begin the process of recovering your money.
Our initial review can take
up to 7 days
, but we try to get back to you earlier than this.
2.
If it’s fraud, we block your card
If you report suspected fraud to us, we’ll freeze your card to stop it working while we investigate. Once we're sure it's fraud, we’ll block your card permanently. Don't worry, we'll let you know when and how to replace it for free.
If it’s not fraud, but an issue with a seller, we recommend you keep your card frozen during the investigation.
You can
create a digital card
to use while your card is frozen, or before your replacement arrives.
3.
We’ll email you with an update
Once we’ve decided whether we’re able to accept your dispute, we’ll let you know by email. We may also ask for more information, so keep an eye on your inbox.
If we can't accept your dispute, we’ll also email you to explain why.
4.
We attempt to recover your money
Next, we get in touch with Mastercard or Visa, and follow their process to try and get your money back. The exact steps are different depending on the type of issue you’ve had, so how long it takes can vary.
We resolve most disputes within 30 days, but it can take as long as 12 weeks for complex cases. As it's a formal process, we can't speed it up or give you any more information while it's ongoing. But we'll do our best to update you as much as we can.
If the dispute is successful, we’ll refund your money to your Wise account. If it’s not, we’ll email you to explain why.

title: Someone may have access to my Wise account
content:
If you've noticed anything unusual, such as transfers that you didn't make, logins from unknown devices, you can’t access your account — or if your phone or device is lost or stolen, it's important to act quickly.
What are the next steps?
Step 1 — Secure your account, if you can still log in
Log out of all devices: Select your profile icon or initials →
Settings
→
Security and privacy
→ Select
Log out everywhere
.
When logging back in:
Change your password to a strong and unique one
Confirm two-step verification (2FA) is enabled and updated
Step 2 —
Contact us
immediately
Use the link above to
call or start a chat right away
. We’ll suspend your account and cards immediately and escalate your case to our specialists.
Can't call or chat?
Contact us from the email connected to your Wise account. Include as much detail as possible.
Don't have access to your Wise-connected email?
You can still contact us using any email address. Please include:
Your full name and date of birth
The email address and phone number linked to your account
A photo of yourself holding your ID and a paper showing “Wise + today’s date” next to your face (use the ‘upload files’ field).
If your device was lost or stolen
Follow this guide
here
to secure it, then notify your mobile provider.
Learn how to protect yourself from phishing
Is the website you’re logging into really Wise
Watch out for phishing
I have issues with my Wise card payment only
For issues with card payments that don't involve your account security, such as a lost or stolen card, or unrecognised transactions — and you don’t suspect that someone accessed your account —
report it here
.
Include a receipt, and as much information as you can. Screenshots are always helpful.
I need to report a fraudulent card payment
I don't recognise a subscription on my Wise card
I didn't get what I paid for with my Wise card
I made the payment myself, but it’s a scam
If you think you’ve made a transfer to a scammer, report it
here
. Make sure to be logged in before clicking the link. Check
this page
for more details.

title: How do I report fraud or a scam?
content:
This article is for what to do if you think you
authorised
a payment to a scammer, such as a fraudulent purchase or fake investment.
If you received a service or product but aren't satisfied with it, this isn't considered a scam — it's a dispute.
Learn how to dispute a Wise card transaction
.
If a payment was made with your
Wise card
without
your permission,
please report a fraudulent card transaction.
If a payment was made from your
Wise account without
your permission (you don't recognise it and you didn't authorise it),
contact us immediately
.
What does it mean if Wise warns me my transfer might be a scam?
Sometimes, we may show you a warning in the app if your transfer has attributes that look like a scam.
This is to help you protect yourself. We can't tell you if your recipient is definitely a scammer, but we ask you to stop and think.
If you're at all unsure about the person or business you're sending money to, it's safest to
cancel the transfer
. You can always come back and send it later once you feel more confident.
How to report fraud or a scam
Step 1: Report to Wise
If you've already sent a transfer to someone you believe is a scammer, report it to us immediately.
Report using our web form
Log in to your Wise account
Submit your scam report using our web form
. This is a short guided form (5 minutes)
Report in the Wise app
Open your account and go to your
Transactions list
Select the transfer you want to report
Tap the
?
icon (Get help) at the top of the screen
Choose
How do I report a fraudulent payment?
then
Start Report
Provide as much detail as you can
If you can’t use the form, or have any difficulties with it,
get in touch with us.
Step 2: File a police report
File a report with your local police or authorities. Wise always cooperates with law enforcement when they contact us as part of an investigation.
Region
How to report
Australia
Reporting fraud in Australia
Brazil
Reporting fraud in Brazil
Canada
Reporting fraud in Canada
EU
Reporting fraud in the EU
Hong Kong
Reporting fraud in Hong Kong
Japan
Reporting fraud in Japan
Malaysia
Reporting fraud in Malaysia
New Zealand
Reporting fraud in New Zealand
Singapore
Reporting fraud in Singapore
UAE
Reporting fraud in the UAE
UK
Reporting fraud in the UK
US
Reporting fraud in the US
What happens after I report it?
Our team will investigate your case and let you know the outcome via email. We may try to issue a recall for your money, but recalls aren't guaranteed and can be a long process. If you haven't heard from us about a successful recall after 40 days, it unfortunately means it wasn't successful.
We can't guarantee a refund.
Keep in mind that when you authorise a payment to be sent, Wise isn't responsible for the loss if the transfer is completed. This is because you're responsible for making sure the recipient is legitimate before you make a transfer. However, we'll try our best to recover the funds, and we'll always take action against any Wise account found to be receiving funds fraudulently.

title: How Young Explorer works
content:
Young Explorer is a card for 6-17-year-olds, managed by parents. It's currently available for
Wise account holders living in Australia, Switzerland, and the UK
.
Your child will get a card which they can use to spend directly from money you set aside for them in your account.
They won’t have their own account details and it won’t be possible for them to top up their card, so any money will need to be added by you for now.
If you’re a parent setting up Young Explorer
Here’s how it works:
1. Order a card
They can use their card globally — in store, online and at ATMs. If they’re 13 or over, they can add it to Apple Pay or Google Pay, too.
2. Add money
Top up their card instantly, in the currency they need.
3. Set limits
Choose how much they can spend in a day and over a month. Their spending will count towards your overall account limits.
Learn more about overall monthly spending limits, depending where you live
.
4. Monitor their spending
Keep an eye on their card usage, with all transactions available to view through your app.
5. Let them spend globally
They’ll get the same benefits as you when using their Wise card overseas — no exchange rate markups or high transaction fees.
If you’re in the UK or Switzerland
1. Give your child access to their own app
They’ll then have their own child-friendly view of the Wise app. They can see what they have to spend and gain confidence in managing their money independently.
Learn more about app access
.
2. Add another adult
They can top up when you’re busy, keep an eye on your child’s spending, and generally share the load.
Learn more about adding another adult
.
If you’re in any other country
Right now, Young Explorer is still a beta — which means it won’t have all the features you might expect yet, but we’re working on it.
If you order a Young Explorer card, you’ll be the first to know when new features are available. You’ll also have a chance to tell us what you’d like us to work on next.
If you’re an adult that’s been added to a Young Explorer Account
You can add money for your child and see their transactions. 

You can’t remove money from the account or manage its members.
To remove a Young Explorer card or close the account
The parent who set up the account can close it — this will also cancel the card. From your Young Explorer account screen, select the 3 dots icon in the top right corner, then ‘Close group’.
How much does the Young Explorer card cost?
Young Explorer comes with no monthly subscription — you’ll just pay a 10 AUD, 8 CHF or 7 GBP one-off fee for the physical card.
When your child spends on their card, they’ll get the same benefits as you.
How many Young Explorer cards can I have?
You can order up to 5 Young Explorer cards for 6-17 year olds.
They must be for different children (you can’t have multiple cards for the same child), and you’ll need to be their parent or legal guardian.
Ready to order a card?
You’ll need to be the child’s parent or legal guardian, and already a Wise customer, to join our Young Explorer.
On the web or Android:
Log in to your account
Select
New
—
you might have to scroll to the far right
Choose
Get a card for a child
Follow the steps on the screen to apply for a Young Explorer card
On iOS:
Log in to your account
Select the
+
icon
—
you might have to scroll to the far right
Choose
Get a card for a child
Follow the steps on the screen to apply for a Young Explorer card

title: How Young Explorer app access works
content:
Young Explorer app access is currently only for Wise account holders living in UK and Switzerland.
With Young Explorer, your child can see a limited view of the money you set aside for them through the Wise app.
You’ll need to help them get access by generating a unique QR code within your app and sharing it with them.
It’s really important they don’t try to register for the app without your QR code.
Once they have access, they’ll be able to:
Log in using facial recognition or a passcode —
We’ll prompt them to set this up the first time they access the app
See the money you set aside for them —
They won’t get access to any other part of your account
You can
check the full list of what your child can and can’t do
— it also shows you what permissions you and any additional adults have, too.
Ready to share app access with your child?
Log in to your app
Go to your Young Explorer account
Tap
Give your child app access
We’ll talk you through the next steps
It’s best if you're with your child when you give them access, so they can easily scan the QR code you generate from within your app.

title: How do I top up the money on my child’s card?
content:
Your child will spend directly from money you set aside for them in your account. Nobody else can access this, and only you can add or remove money for them.
Log in to your account and tap or click on your shared group with your child
You’ll see an option to
Add
or
Withdraw
money — choose
Add
If you have currencies in your account
, you’ll be asked whether you want to top up their card with one of your currencies or from a different bank or card
If you don’t have currencies in your account
, you’ll just see the option to add from a different bank or card
Once you’ve made your choice, the money will be added and your child can start spending straight away
If they use their card overseas, we’ll automatically convert to the local currency when they spend.

title: Can I stop my child buying things not suitable for under 18s?
content:
The money your child spends is still legally yours, so you’re responsible for how they use their card, and what they buy.
We have controls in place to block payments to certain merchants not suitable for under 18s, which will automatically be applied to your child’s card. While we try our best to make sure these payments are blocked, there may be times when a merchant isn't blocked by mistake.
We can’t stop your child from buying inappropriate things from a merchant that isn’t blocked, for example if they attempted to buy alcohol in a supermarket or corner shop (which are approved merchants).
If you’re worried about your child’s spending, you can set limits for them, monitor their spend and control whether they can use their card online, in store and at ATMs — all from within your account.

title: Is my child’s money safe?
content:
We’ll protect the money on your child’s Young Explorer card in the same way we protect yours.
We’re regulated by local authorities across the world, in more than 65 countries. And we have 24/7 customer support in place — on the phone, by email or through chat, whenever you need us.
If something goes wrong and your child’s card is lost or stolen, you’ll need to notify us straight away. The money is legally still yours, so you’re responsible for what happens to your child’s card.
Learn more about how we keep your money safe

title: How is my child’s data collected, stored and protected?
content:
We’ll ask for two bits of information when you apply for a Young Explorer card:
Your child’s name
— this will be printed on their card. We’ll also include your child’s first name in the app where you manage their card, and in emails and push notifications where we’ll share updates with you about their spending
Your child’s age
— this is to verify they’re eligible for a Young Explorer card, and how long they should have it (it’ll stop working once they’re 18 and 3 months)
We won’t create a profile for your child, so the information will only be associated with your account.
Our
Young Explorer Privacy Notice
explains more about how we store and protect your child’s data.

title: What happens when my child turns 18?
content:
As soon as they turn 18, they’re eligible to apply for a Wise card. You can even
refer them and earn a reward
.
Your child’s Young Explorer card will keep working for 3 months after they turn 18. After that, their card will be cancelled and any remaining currencies will be moved out of their dedicated space in your account, back to your money.

title: How to add Young Explorer to Apple Pay or Google Pay
content:
If your child is 13 or over, they can add their Young Explorer card to Apple Pay or Google Pay on their device.
You can also add their card to your own mobile wallet.
Cards for children under 13 can't be added to a mobile wallet.
How to add to Apple pay
How to add to Google pay

The account owner (parent) will have full control over how the Young Explorer money is managed. Other members in the account (the child, and another adult if they’re added), will have limited access.

title: Is there a limit to how much my child can spend using their Young Explorer card?
content:
Parents who set up a Young Explorer Account can choose how much their child can spend in a day and over a month. If you set up the account, your child’s spending will count towards your overall account limits.
Although you can choose your child’s spending limits, they can’t exceed certain amounts each month. How much your child can spend in total each month depends where you live.
Due to local regulations, if your registered address is in Australia, New Zealand or Singapore, there are limits on the amounts your child can hold in their Young Explorer Account at any one time, too.
Where you live
How much your child can spend in total each month
How much money your child can hold in total
Australia
999 AUD
999 AUD
Brazil
10,000 BRL
No limit
New Zealand
690 NZD
999 NZD
Singapore
1,600 SGD
999 SGD
Switzerland
1,000 CHF
No limit
UK
1,000 GBP
No limit
These monthly limits include both card spending and ATM usage. 

If you’re a customer based in Singapore, ATM usage isn’t allowed locally or abroad.

title: How do I receive money to my Wise account details?
content:
With Wise, you can get account details that you can share with others to receive payments.
Once you get account details, you can share them with the person or business that wants to pay you and they can send you money directly from their bank.
How your account details work
How do I open account details?
If you live in
a country where you’re eligible to hold money in your account
, you can get account details by selecting one of the
eligible currencies
from the home screen and selecting
Get account details.
We’ll guide you through the setup process — there may be requirements if it’s your first time.
Learn more about getting account details
What currencies and types of payments can I receive?
You can get account details in over 10 different currencies including
USD
,
EUR
, and
GBP
.
Learn what currencies and payment types you can receive
With GBP
account details
, you can receive international Swift payments from outside the UK in 23 different currencies.
Learn more about receiving to your GBP account details
With EUR
account details
, you can receive international Swift payments from outside the SEPA region in 5 different currencies.
Learn more about receiving with your EUR account details
Wise doesn’t accept cash or cheque payments. We can receive from some Cryptocurrency platforms, but not from all.
Learn more about receiving from Cryptocurrency platforms
What happens after I receive the money?
Your payment will be added directly into the relevant currency in your Wise account — you'll see it under Transactions.
Once the money is in your account, you can
convert it to any available currency
, hold it on your Wise account, or
send it to an external bank account
.
Fees and limits
Most currencies have no receiving fees for domestic payments, with the exception of USD domestic wires. There is a fee to receive international (Swift) payments in most currencies.
And most currencies have no receiving limits with the exception of USD and SGD.
Read more on fees and limits here
Checking and sharing your account details
How do I check my account details?
Once you've opened your account details, on app or web:
From Home, select one of your currencies
Select the account details button just under the currency
You can view and copy your account details to share with others.
What details should I provide for domestic payments?
Domestic account details are available for many currencies. These allow you to get paid like a local, wherever you are. For example, someone can pay you USD as if you’re located in the US — even if you’re actually somewhere else.
Domestic payments are usually the cheapest and fastest way to receive money with your account details.
When receiving a domestic payment, you'll typically need to provide the sender with these details:
Account Number
: Your account’s unique identifier within the bank.
IBAN
(International Bank Account Number)
: Used in many countries, it includes your country code, bank code, and account number.
Routing Number - can be for ACH and Wire payments
(for U.S.): A 9-digit code identifying your bank.
Sort Code
(for the UK): A 6-digit code identifying your bank and branch.
BSB Code
(for Australia): A 6-digit number used to identify the individual branch of an Australian financial institution.
What details should I provide for international payments?
For international payments, provide the sender with:
Swift/BIC
: A unique code to identify your bank globally.
IBAN (International Bank Account Number)
: Used in many countries, it includes your country code, bank code, and account number.
Name
: Name as it appears on your account.
Learn more about how account details work
Are there other ways to receive money to my Wise account?
You can also receive payments in other ways such as through your email address or phone number, your Wise card details, or by sending a request.
Learn more about these other ways to receive here

title: How do I open account details to receive money?
content:
Here’s how you can get account details to receive money from people or businesses:
From
Home
, select the plus icon to add
a currency that has account details
, such as
USD
,
EUR
, or
GBP
.
Select
Get account details.
If it’s your first time, you’ll need to complete some requirements first.
Check below or in the app or web for the requirements for your account
Once you’ve completed all of the requirements, you’ll have your own account details that can be shared with whoever wants to send you money such as friends, companies, or customers.
You can also view your account details from the
Payments
tab.
Eligibility
To get account details, you need to add a currency in one of the supported currencies.
You can add a currency if your registered address is in a country where the currency is
available
.
USD account details are not available in some regions.
Learn more about getting USD account details
.
RON account details are not available in some regions.
Learn more about getting RON account details
.
What happens if I move to another country?
If you relocate to a country or region where currencies you have in your account aren't available, your account details will automatically become inactive.
You’ll need to have one of the supported currencies to get account details — and that depends on whether your registered address is in a country where Wise offers currencies.
For instance, if you move to a region
where USD account details aren't available
, then we'll have to deactivate your USD account details.
To keep your account details active, you'll need to reside in a region where Wise supports the desired currencies.
Personal account requirements
To get your first set of account details, we'll ask you to:
add 20 GBP to your account
(or equivalent in your currency)
verify your identity
with
a photo of your ID and a separate selfie of your face
Business account requirements
For businesses in the EEA (except Germany, Netherlands, and Italy) and Switzerland you'll need to pay a registration fee when signing up.
This is a one-time onboarding fee — you won't be charged extra to open account details to receive money.
For
businesses in India
you don't need to pay additional charges to get account details.
For
businesses in Hong Kong
you don't need to pay additional charges to get account details.
For businesses in Brazil, you'll need to pay a registration fee when signing up.
For all other regions, signing up for a Wise account is free, but there's a fee to get account details.
You only have to pay to get your first set of account details. After that, you can freely get account details for any of the other available currencies.
How much does it cost to get account details?
The fee to register an account or get account details varies based on where your business is registered.
You can check the fee when signing up for account details or you can check the fee by:
Going to
our pricing page
.
Scroll down to the Receiving money section to see how much it costs to get account details.
If you want to check the pricing for other countries, click the circular flag icon in the top right to change your country.
Note
: there are fees to receive most currencies by Swift.
Learn more here
Why does the fee vary?
Verification processes cost more in some countries, so we might charge you a higher fee in order to cover these costs.
After paying the fee, we’ll get started on
verifying your business
. Once verified, you’ll get your account details. If we can’t verify your business, we’ll refund the fee back to you.
How can I pay to get account details?
For businesses in EEA, Switzerland and the UK: You can only
pay the fee using your debit/credit card
.
For Brazilian businesses you can pay with Pix via your business bank account.
For all other regions you can
pay the fee using your debit/credit card
, or with money in your account.

title: Can I receive money without sharing my Wise account details?
content:
In this article, we’ll cover the different ways that you can receive money to your Wise account without using your account details. If you want to receive to your Wise account details
learn more here
.
Once you add a currency you can receive money to your Wise account through your:
email address or phone number –
learn more
Wise card details –
receive money by using your 16-digit card number
or by requesting a payment, such as with a
payment link
or your
Wisetag
. Learn more about the different methods below.
Receiving to your email address or phone number
If you make your account
discoverable
, others can send you money through their Wise account by just entering your phone number or email address.* They may need to
sync their contacts
so they can find you.
You can find out if your account is discoverable in
your privacy settings
.
From there, you can also enable or disable the option to be discoverable by email, phone or your
Wisetag
.
If you haven’t enabled this, then
we’ll send you an email
and you can manually add the details that you want to receive money to.
Requesting payments
With payment requests, you can send a request to other people or businesses on Wise so they can pay you directly.
How do I request money?
To request money, just select
Request
from Home, or select  one of your currencies and then
Request
.
You can send a request to your
synced contacts
or to anyone with a payment link.
You’ll need to have an active currency for the currency you're requesting.
Learn more about how to request money
What are the different ways I can request money?
Here's a quick overview of the different ways you can request payments. Select any of the links below to learn more.
Personal and business accounts can get a
payment link
that you can share with others to get paid.
Personal and business accounts can set up a
Wisetag
to set up a unique link with your name that you can share with others, such as with a QR code.
Business accounts can request money by
invoice
where you can set up all the details for the payment
Businesses can also receive using
Quick Pay
— a fast and easy way to receive money.
For Wise Business accounts, you can also choose how to be paid — straight from your customer’s Wise account, by bank transfer, or by card.
Learn more about getting paid by card
Receiving to your Wise card details
If you have a Wise card issued anywhere besides the US or Japan, you can get paid directly to your 16-digit Wise card number.
The receiving limits depend on what currencies you’re receiving.
Read more about receiving money to your card
*What do we do with the information from your phone’s contacts?
We never send or store your full phone’s contacts on our servers. We also never get in touch with your contacts. We only use this information to make it easier to send money between you and your friends.
When you switch the feature on, any Wise customer with your phone number or email address will see your account name and photo on their app but rest assured that they aren't able to see any of your account numbers.

title: How your Wise account details work
content:
Wise offers
account details
for both domestic and international payments. Select the currency that you want to receive into, then choose the number right under the currency, and we'll show you both sets of details.
Read on to learn more about them.
What are domestic payments?
Domestic payments are transfers between the financial institutions in the same country via local payment networks.
Let's say you’ve moved from the UK to the US, and you’d like to receive money in British pounds from your family living in the UK. We’ll give you GBP account details that your family can send money to — this means no conversion or international fees.
What are international payments?
International payments are a way for someone to send money in a currency from almost any country in the world — even if that country doesn’t use that currency. When someone sends you an international payment, it comes through the Swift network.
Now let's say you live in the UK, and your friend in Japan wants to send you 10,000 JPY. They send the money through their bank using the Swift network,
a
nd the payment arrives in your Wise account shortly after, without incurring currency exchange fees.
Bear in mind that Swift fees, and receiving fees may impact the final deposited amount.
Learn where you can receive Swift payments from
To save on speed and fees, you can use domestic payment details to receive money in the sender's local currency. Otherwise, use Swift details for international transfers.
What details do I need to provide for a domestic payment?
That depends on the currency your sender is using to pay. These are the details they might need:
Account Number
(your account’s unique identifier within the bank)
IBAN
(used in many countries, it includes your country code, bank code, and account number)
Routing Number – both for ACH and Wire payments
(for the U.S.): A 9-digit code identifying your bank.
Sort Code
(for the UK): A 6-digit code identifying your bank and branch.
BSB Code
(for Australia): A 6-digit number used to identify the individual branch of an Australian financial institution
Remember that payment schemes vary by country or region, and the account details your sender will need depend on the scheme used.
UK
: common schemes include
CHAPS, BACS, FPS, and Swift
EU
: the
SEPA
scheme is popular for EUR-to-EUR cross-border payments
US
: Common schemes are include
ACH, Wire
What details do I need to provide for an international payment?
For international payments, provide:
Swift/BIC code
: identifies your bank globally.
IBAN
: your account's unique identifier.
Account name
: your name as it appears on the account.
Bank name and address
: found on your account details screen.
Use our IBAN checker
to break down IBANs into BIC and account numbers.
Can the same details be used for all currencies?
Not necessarily, as different currencies and payment types require specific details. For example, when sending EUR you need to share your IBAN, the GBP IBAN can accept 23 currencies via Swift, while sending USD requires the ACH and Swift codes, for example.
What happens if the wrong details are provided?
Incorrect payment details can cause delays or failed transactions. Always double-check:
Account numbers and codes — such as IBAN, Swift/BIC
Payment method — such as ACH vs. Swift
Recipient’s name and address
Learn more about your transfer may have been cancelled

title: I'm expecting money, but it hasn't arrived yet
content:
If you’re waiting for a payment, don't worry. Here’s a checklist to help you find your money.
1. Check the date
Most transfers are processed on
working days
only.
Weekends and holidays:
Banks usually don't process payments on weekends or public holidays. If the money was sent on a Friday, it likely won't arrive until the next working day
Time zones:
If the sender is in a different time zone, their bank might be closed even if yours is open
2. Check your Wise account
We might have paused the transfer to keep your money safe while we ask for more information. We'll always email you if we do, but it's worth checking your account too.
Check your Activity list:
Look for any pending tasks or notifications on your home screen
Check your limits:
Some currencies have limits on how much you can receive. If a payment takes you over the limit, we may have to refund it.
Learn more about receiving limits
Check your email:
Search your inbox and spam folder for emails from Wise. We might be asking to verify your identity or for more information about the transfer
3. Check with the sender
If your account looks fine, the issue might be with the sender's transfer. Ask them to check:
The details:
Did they spell your name correctly? Did they use the correct account number? A typo can cause the bank to reject the payment
The delivery estimate:
Ask them for the estimated delivery time they received from their bank when they set up the transfer
The tracking link:
If they sent money using Wise, ask them for the
tracking link
. This will show you exactly where the money is
Why might my money be delayed?
Scenario
Details
The money was sent from another Wise account
In this case, it should be instant. If there's a delay, the sender may need to take action on their end. For privacy reasons, we can only discuss transfer details with the sender.
The money was sent from an outside bank
The delivery time depends on how it was sent. For example, a
Swift
or wire transfer can take 2–6 working days to reach us.
Learn how long your currency usually takes
.
The transfer was cancelled
Sometimes, we have to cancel a transfer for regulatory reasons. If this happens, the money is always sent back to the sender. You should advise them to contact their bank for more information.
What if the estimated time has passed?
If the delivery estimate has passed and you’ve checked the steps above, we can help you find the money.
Please ask the sender to send you a
proof of payment
. This is a document from their bank confirming the money was sent. It must show:
The sender's name and account details
Your name and account details
The date and amount
The currency
Once you have this document, get in touch with us and
upload it here
so we can track the payment for you.

title: How long does it take to receive money into my Wise account?
content:
If you're expecting a payment into your Wise account details, you'll want to know when it'll arrive. The time it takes depends on a few things:
The currency that was sent
The payment method the sender used
When the payment was sent, as payments sent on weekends and holidays can cause delays
When will my payment arrive?
Payments are usually processed during working days, Monday–Friday. If a payment was sent over a weekend or a public holiday in the sending country, it should arrive on the next working day.
Here's an overview of how long each currency typically takes.
Currency
Domestic payment
International  payment (Swift)
AUD
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 1 working day
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
CAD
Usually a few hours, sometimes up to 1 working day
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
EUR*
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 1 working day
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
GBP*
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 1 working day
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
HUF
Instant for payments under 10 million HUF, a few hours for payments over 10 million HUF
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
MYR
Usually a few hours, up to 2 working days
You can only receive MYR from Malaysia
NZD
Usually a few hours, up to 2 working days
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
PHP
Usually instant, or up to 2 working days
You can only receive PHP from the Philippines
PLN*
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 2 working days
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
RON
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 2 working days
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
SGD*
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 1 working day
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 5 working days
TRY
Usually a few minutes, sometimes up to 1 working day
You can only receive TRY from Türkiye
USD*
Usually within hours, and up to 4 working days for ACH transfers. Usually within minutes, and up to 1 working day for wire transfers
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 6 working days
*EUR: This also applies when receiving
non-EUR currencies
by Swift to your EUR details
*GBP: This also applies when receiving
non-GBP currencies
by Swift to your GBP details
*PLN: If you already have PLN account details, you can continue to use them. We don't offer new PLN details for customers right now
*SGD: Receiving a payment from Singapore in SGD is typically very fast, but if the payment is sent in SGD from a country outside of Singapore, it becomes an international payment and will take longer
*USD: Make sure your sender knows the difference in speed between ACH and wire transfers
Why do some international payments take longer?
When a payment is sent internationally, it might be processed by one or more intermediary banks before it reaches Wise. We have no control over this process, and these banks may have their own timelines and fees.
Usually,
International Swift payments
take up to 5 working days to reach your Wise account, but if it's delayed, you can contact your sender to ask them to request a payment trace. We'll also notify you if we receive an international payment with an unexpected amount.
What should you do if your money hasn't arrived?
If the estimated time for your payment has passed, follow these steps before contacting us:
Check with the sender. Ask the person or business who sent the money to confirm they sent it to the correct Wise account details. If they sent it to the wrong details, they'll need to initiate a trace or recall with their bank.
Ask the sender to provide you with a
proof of payment
. This document helps our team locate your money if it's been delayed.
Once you have the proof of payment, go to our
Document Uploader
, select the option
Waiting for money to arrive in my account
and follow the instructions. Our team will review the document and get back to you by email.

title: What are the fees and limits for receiving money?
content:
When getting paid to your Wise account, most payment types are free to receive,  and you can receive almost any amount. Read on to see exceptions.
Learn how to get account details and what currencies you can receive
Fees
To check any of our current fees for receiving money, go to our
pricing page
— just set it to your region at the top.
Sometimes, the sender’s bank may charge a fee before the payment reaches us, so reach out to the sender to check with their bank.
Domestic fees
Receiving domestic payments for the majority of currencies is free.
The only currency with any domestic receiving fees is USD for domestic wire transfers. However, ACH payments, (direct deposits) in USD are free to receive.
Learn more about receiving using your USD account details
Swift fees
There are fees to receive international (Swift) payments for all currencies. Additionally, when someone pays you by Swift, some
correspondent banks might apply their own fees
on top of the payment.
Check
the pricing page
for the most up-to-date Swift receiving fees for your region.
Limits
For most currencies, there's no limit on how much you can receive to your account. But check the relevant currency article under
Using your account details
to be sure.
Receiving large amounts
After receiving a large amount of money, we may need to ask you about your relationship with the sender, or the purpose of the payment. This is a regulatory requirement for us, and it helps us to make sure that our services are safe for everyone.
You don't need to do anything before receiving large amounts, such as sending documents to us. We review transfers on a case by case basis, so there’s no way to know what information we’ll need until we’ve received the money from the sender.
Currencies and regions
USD
These limits apply if you have USD account details with our partner, CFSB. Your routing number would start with 026.
Personal limit (USD)
Business limit   (USD)
USD limit per transaction and per day
20 million
50 million
USD limit per year*
35 million
150 million
*These limits apply for a rolling 365-day period, and not per calendar year. So say you receive a payment on 10 April — this will count towards your limit until 11 April the following year.
Any money you receive into your USD using your USD account details, either by someone else or yourself, will count towards the limit.
There are no limits if you choose
Add USD
, or convert money from one of your other currencies into your USD.
SGD
Payment method
Personal limit  (SGD)
Business limit (SGD)
FAST
200,000 per transfer
200,000 per transfer
GIRO
Not available for personal customers
90 million
MEPS
Not available for personal customers
No limits
There are no receiving limits per day, but for personal customers there's a daily holding limit for your Wise account.
Learn more about holding limits if you live in Singapore
HKD
There are no receiving limits
India
There are limits to how much you can receive and you can't hold money on your Wise account.
Learn more here
Philippines
There’s no limit on how much you can hold in your Wise account, but there’s a monthly receiving limit.
Learn more here
Holding limits
For most regions, there are no limits on the amount of money you can hold in your Wise currencies.
If your registered address is in one of the following countries, select the link below to learn more info about holding limits in your country.
Japan
Malaysia
Singapore

title: How do I request money?
content:
When you need to be paid, for example from your friends or family, you can send the payer your account details and ask for a payment. But with your Wise account, you can also create a direct
payment request
to your payer. Once they pay you, it’ll get deposited directly to your account.
For businesses wishing to get paid with a payment link, learn more here
Before you request money
You'll need to have at least one set of
account details
open before requesting any money. Check our individual pages for each
currency account details
to see what your account can receive.
If you’re requesting money from someone with a Wise account, you can request money in any currency — as long as you’ve opened account details for at least one currency. If they don’t have a Wise account, you can only request money from them in a currency you have account details for.
Learn more about receiving money to you account
If you request money from other Wise users, we'll notify them about your request via email or a push notification — you'll just need to
sync your contacts
first.
How to request money
You can create a payment request from a few different places in your Wise account — from your currency, from the Payments tab, or from your existing Wise contacts by going on the Recipients tab.
You can select Request from the home screen, or go to the currency and select Receive. If the person is a Wise user, select or enter them from the list. If they're not a Wise user, just select Anyone to create a payment link that you can share with your payer. It will include a QR code with your
Wisetag
.
Checking your payment requests
You can see all of your active and inactive payment requests.
Go to
Payments
Go on
Payment requests
Requests are only valid for 30 calendar days
Fees and limits
There are no fees for requesting money, but we do have fees to receive USD wire and Swift payments.
Check our pricing page
to see the latest fees for receiving money — just set it to your region.
What if I get a payment request from someone?
If you're getting a request from a Wise user to pay them, you'll get a push notification or email, depending on
your notification settings
. If you haven't paid or declined it, the request will remain in your account activity for 30 days as a
Task
on the top of the Home page. You can always turn your profile off for visibility if you don't want others to be able to send you requests.
If you don't have Wise, and someone shared a link with you, you'll see the amount, currency and bank details you'll need to send money to. Never pay a request from a contact you don’t recognise.
Wise will
never
request a payment from you through this feature.
Learn more about how to control this feature on your account

title: Can I get USD account details?
content:
You can get USD account details for your personal or Wise Business account if your address is in a
country where you can open a currency
.
What are some restrictions?
Unavailable countries
You can't get USD account details if your address is in:
Afghanistan
Bangladesh, Belarus, Burundi
Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Congo (Republic of), Congo (Democratic Republic of the), Cuba
Eritrea
Guinea-Bissau
Iran, Iraq
Libya
Myanmar
Nevada, North Korea
Pakistan
Russia
Serbia, Somalia, South Sudan, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Syria
Türkiye
United Arab Emirates, Ukraine
Venezuela
Yemen
*
Learn what currencies Indian business accounts can hold
Why am I being asked for more verification details?
We know you provide information to us when you join Wise, but sometimes we need to ask for up-to-date info about your identity.
We may ask for this to comply with our regulatory requirements. If you receive an email from us about this, just follow the instructions to continue using your Wise account as normal.

title: Getting paid to your Wise Business by card, Apple Pay or Google Pay
content:
With a Wise Business account in the EEA, the UK, Australia, Hong Kong, New Zealand, Singapore, Canada or the US, you can request a payment from anyone and send them a link to pay you for your goods or services by credit, debit card, Apple Pay or Google Pay. This feature isn't available in any other regions.
Find out more about
accepting card payments
in your business.
How to apply for this feature
Any business with a registered address in Australia, the EEA, Hong Kong, New Zealand, Singapore, the UK, Canada, or the US can
apply to receive card payments, Apple Pay or Google Pay payments
. When you apply, you’ll need to:
provide the address of your business website
tell us what your business does
agree to how we charge card fees
In some cases, we may not be able to provide this feature for your business, even in eligible countries. We're continuously working on expanding our product to all customers and hope that this feature can be made available to you in the future.
How to get paid by card, Apple Pay or Google Pay
Once you have this feature, from the App or Web:
Request a payment
, create an invoice in Wise, or share your Quick Pay link with your customer
Select
Card
,
Apple Pay
or
Google Pay
as a payment method
Share the link with your customer
We’ll tell you when your customer has paid, and we’ll email them proof of their payment
We’ll notify you again when your money arrives
Learn more about requesting payments
What card types and currencies work
We currently support credit and debit Visa and Mastercard payments in AUD, CAD, CHF, CZK, DKK, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, JPY, NOK, NZD, PLN, RON, SEK, SGD and USD.
What it costs you
The fee we charge depends on the card your customer pays with and it's the same for cards, Apple Pay and Google Pay.
