Our fees already cover Visa and Mastercard card scheme and interchange fees, so you won’t be charged anything extra.
Once your payment arrives, you can check the fee we charged in your
Activity
.
What it costs your customer
We don't charge your customer anything, but their bank or card issuer might charge them a fee if:
they're paying you in a foreign currency
they’re paying with a credit card
their bank treats the payment to Wise as a
cash advance
If your customer isn’t sure what they’ll be charged, ask them to check with their card issuer.
How long it takes to receive the payment
It can take up to 14 days to pay out your money after you receive a payment from your customer, depending on the type of business you are running.
For some currencies, payouts sometimes can take longer. But you can always see the estimated payout time in the
Transactions details
section by selecting the transaction in your activity feed.
In cases where we decide to close your account, we may retain your funds for a period of up to two months. This retention period allows us to address any incoming chargeback requests for which you remain liable.
This section corresponds to Schedule 1 settlement timeframes in the
EEA Agreement for Acquiring Payment Services
.
How to issue refunds
You can now issue refunds to anyone who paid you by card through the
Request payment
option. The refund will go back to the original paying card.
You can go to your
Transactions
on the home screen, or:
Go to the
Payments
tab
Select either
Payment links
or
Invoices
Select the
Request
, and then the specific payment
Select
Refund payment
Choose how much you want to refund — it can be any amount up to the full amount paid. You can choose to issue a few partial refunds, but the total shouldn't be more than the full amount
If you refund your customer, you won't get a refund on the fees you paid for using our service when charging the payer.
Refunds to the card can take 2-10 working days to arrive.

title: Getting paid to your Wise Business by invoice
content:
If you have a Wise Business account, you can create and send invoices directly from your account to help you get paid faster.
Sign up for a Wise business account
You'll need to have
account details
in at least one currency to start using invoices.
Setting up invoices
Here’s how you can set up and manage your invoices.
You create the invoice
On web or app, you can either select
Request
and then
Create an invoice
from the top of the home screen, or:
Go on the
Payments
tab
Select
Invoices
Select
Create an invoice
Enter the details of your invoice:
the currency, invoice number, and issue date
who you're invoicing — you can add their email address and we’ll send your invoice directly to them
the products or services you’ve sold
how you want to be paid and the due date
Preview the PDF draft
Select
Create invoice
or
Create and send invoice
After you've created your invoice, you can share the payment link and download a copy for your records. Both options have details on how the recipient can pay.
Add a logo to your invoice
You can add a logo to your invoice. This will be the same image as your Wise Business profile picture. It will appear on the invoice PDF, the payment link, and the email we send to your customer.
To upload or change the logo:
Go to your business profile page by selecting your account name in the top right corner, or
Select the camera icon on the invoice preview screen.
Note:
Only users with
manage business details
permissions can upload a logo.
Your customer pays the invoice
If your customer pays by bank transfer, ask them to include the invoice number as a reference. This helps us link the incoming payment to your invoice.
They should use
either
the invoice number or the payment reference number, but not both. For example, if the invoice number is INV-2024-01, they should enter that in the reference field. If the payment reference number is 123456, they should enter that in the reference field. If they enter both, the payment may not match in our system.
Your customer paying the invoice can learn more here
Managing your invoices
Here's how you can view, cancel or mark invoices as paid.
Select
Invoices
from the
Payments
tab
Filter by
paid
,
due
,
overdue
or
expired
Select the invoice you want to view or manage
You can:
view invoice details and see if it’s paid, overdue, or expired
cancel or mark the invoice as
paid
download the invoice and get the payment link
You can’t have recurring invoices, but you can duplicate an existing one.
You can check information about our
latest fees
, our
receiving limits
, and
fees for being paid by card
in your region.

title: How to pay for an invoice sent from a Wise Business
content:
This article covers what you can do if you received an invoice from a Wise Business account. You don't need to have a Wise account to pay for an invoice sent from Wise.
Why did I get an invoice?
You may have received an invoice by email if you recently bought a product or service from a business that has a Wise Business account.
The email shows who the invoice is for, the products or services purchased, and the total amount due. It'll also have a link to download the full invoice.
If you have any questions or concerns about the invoice, contact the business directly.
Never pay an invoice that you don’t recognise.
How can I pay the invoice?
Review the invoice to make sure the details are correct
If everything looks correct, select
Pay now
You can pay for your invoice by:
card
bank transfer
— include the reference number when you pay, so the business can identify your payment
your Wise account
if you have one
The available payment methods may vary — you’ll see what options are available on your invoice.
Including a reference
When paying the business, you should use
either
the invoice number or the payment reference number, but not both. For example, if the invoice number is INV-2024-01 —
enter that invoice number in the reference field. If the payment reference number is 123456, enter that payment number in the reference field. If you enter both, the payment may not match in our system and may not go through.
Approvals
If you receive this request on a Business account, depending on your permissions, you may need approval from an Admin, or another team member with an approval status, before you can pay for the invoice.
Learn more about Approvals
What if I received an unexpected invoice?
If you believe you received an invoice in error, here’s what you can do:
Review your order history
— check your recent transactions and emails for any recent purchase confirmations
Contact the business
— the email you received will show contact details for the business who's invoicing you. Reach out to them with your invoice number and question, and they can look into it for you

title: How to receive money by Easy bank transfer for businesses
content:
When receiving GBP from UK bank accounts, you can receive by an Easy bank transfer if you have a Wise Business account.
What are Easy bank transfers?
Easy bank transfers are powered through Open Banking technology for UK accounts. It’s a fast, easy, and secure way to pay for your transfers.
You can share a payment link, which the sender can use to pay you with an Easy bank transfer.
How does it work for the payer?
The payer clicks on your payment link and chooses to pay with Easy bank transfer
They’ll log in directly to their online banking where they can complete the payment without having to enter any of your account details.
How do I accept Easy bank transfers with my payment requests?
Easy bank transfers are turned on by default for all payment links you create with Wise.
This is currently only available to select UK and EU Wise Business customers.
If you’d like to turn it off, simply toggle off
Easy bank Transfer
as a payment method when you create a new payment link.
Which banks are supported with Easy bank transfer?
The majority of the major UK banks are supported.

title: How our account details use partner banks
content:
We work with partner banks to provide account details for some of our supported currencies.
Working with partner banks means that you can receive a wider range of currencies, using both domestic and
international Swift
payment methods.
When you’ll see partner bank details
When you want to receive money with your account details, you might need to provide the partner bank’s details to the person sending the money. Or your sender might see the partner bank’s name shown when they make the payment instead of Wise's name.
Don’t worry — as long as you use the account details shown in your Wise account, the money will arrive safely to your account. And we always display the partner bank details clearly alongside your other account details, so you can share them all with people sending you money.
Getting help with partner banks
Although some of our account details are provided by partner banks, your account is with Wise — not the partner bank. So if you need any help, make sure you contact us first.
Learn more about receiving money into your account details
Learn more about the Wise account
Learn how Wise keeps your money safe

title: How is EUR in my main account managed by Wise?
content:
This article covers how Wise manages EUR you hold in your main account and your EUR account details, including which Wise entity is responsible for overseeing and safeguarding them.
Learn more about how to receive money with your EUR account details
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Your EUR account details work exactly the same for you, regardless of where you live — you share them with your sender, and they can send you EUR using these details. But the way we manage your money depends on whether your address is inside or outside the European Economic Area (EEA).
We use our own network to make it easy for you to receive money domestically. To ensure a smooth process, the
different entities within the Wise Group
manage and protect your money through their respective accounts.
Your Wise Account will always be held with the
Wise entity based on your residence
. This means that you have one account with Wise, and it’s managed by the local Wise entity which operates in your country of residence.
The entity with which you hold your Wise Account retains the responsibility and liability for any issues related to your EUR Account Details or money received through them
. This is because the money is held by the entity that manages your Wise Account.
If you live in the European Economic Area
If you're a resident within the EEA, your account and account details are held with
Wise Europe
.
When you receive money to your EUR Account Details, it is received in an account held by Wise Europe.
Wise Europe retains the responsibility and liability for any issues related to your EUR Account Details or funds received through them.
If you live outside the European Economic Area
If you live outside of the EEA, your contract is with the local Wise Group entity in your country of residence.
This local Wise Group entity has an arrangement with Wise Europe to provide you with EUR Account Details and handle the flow of funds associated with these details. For this purpose the local Wise Group entity has opened a payment Account with Wise Europe to which these EUR Account Details are issued.
If you are a resident outside of the European Economic Area (EEA) and you have EUR Account Details, this doesn’t mean you have a separate account with Wise Europe or you are directly a customer of Wise Europe. Your one Wise account will still be held by the local Wise Group entity in your country of residence.
When you use your EUR Account Details, the funds are first received in an account held by your Wise Group entity with Wise Europe. As soon as the funds arrive in this account, they are transferred to the local Wise Entity that manages your Wise Account and credited as EUR in your main account.
For example:
If you live in the UK, your contract is with Wise Payments Limited.
When someone sends you EUR to your EUR IBAN details, the money will be received by Wise Europe first.
Wise Europe will allocate the money to the payment account Wise Payments Limited holds with Wise Europe, who then makes it available in your Wise Account.
This all happens in the background — you’ll only see the money that you hold in EUR in your main account.
How it works
Here's some answers to questions you may have about your EUR in your main account, or your EUR account details.
If I receive EUR via my EUR Account Details, where are these funds collected?
Wise uses its own network to make it easy for you to receive funds domestically. To ensure a smooth process, the different entities within the Wise Group have made agreements to manage and protect your funds through their respective accounts.
For example, if you're located outside the European Economic Area (EEA), your contract is with the local Wise Group entity.
Your EUR Account Details are made available by Wise Europe to the local Wise Group entity that has an arrangement with Wise Europe to handle the flow of funds associated with your EUR Account Details and make these available to you.
When you use your EUR Account Details, the funds are first received in an account held by your Wise entity with Wise Europe. As soon as the funds arrive in this account, they are transferred to the local Wise Entity that manages your Wise Account and credited as EUR in your main account.
If I have EUR Account Details does that mean I hold a Payment Account (Wise Account) with Wise Europe?
No, having EUR Account Details does not necessarily mean you have a separate or additional account with Wise Europe. EUR Account Details are simply a way to receive EUR funds into your existing Wise Account through the local payment network.
Your Wise Account will always be held with the Wise entity based on your residence. This means that you have one account with Wise, managed by the local Wise entity operating in the country where you reside.
If you're a resident outside of the EEA and you have EUR Account Details, this doesn't result in you having a separate account with Wise Europe or you becoming a customer of Wise Europe. Your one Wise account will still be held by the local Wise entity in your country of residence.
Learn more
If you're a resident within the EEA, your account is held with Wise Europe.
If I have a problem with my EUR Account Details or funds that I received via these details as EUR, which entity is liable for these funds?
The entity with which you hold your Wise Account retains the responsibility and liability for any issues related to your EUR Account Details or funds received through them. This is because the funds are held by the entity that manages your Wise Account.
For instance, if you're a resident of the United Kingdom and you receive EUR funds via your EUR Account Details,
Wise Payments Limited
will be responsible and liable for safeguarding your funds.
Learn more

title: Fees for receiving money by Swift
content:
This page shows the fees for receiving money by Swift.
We regularly review our fees to reflect the real cost of moving your money. We recently reviewed fees for receiving international Swift payments, and the fees now more accurately reflect our costs.
You can see all of our fees on our
pricing page
— just set it to your region from the flag icon at the top.
Learn how to open account details to receive payments

What are correspondent bank fees?
Correspondent banks that help send money can sometimes deduct their own fees from the sender's amount — Wise can't change this. You can ask your sender to opt for the OUR charges — this option allows the sender to cover all the correspondent charges.
Learn more about correspondent bank fees here
What if the fees change?
We’ll always let you know if and when we change our fees. The date for these changes depends on where you live, and whether the fee is decreasing or increasing.
If you signed up for a Wise account before these changes went live, these fees will apply to you after your region's notice period.
Learn more about when fees change and when it applies to you

title: Can I return money paid into my account details?
content:
Yes, you can return a payment received into your AUD, EUR, GBP, HUF, NZD, PLN, SGD (FAST), or TRY account details.
For GBP payments, there’s a 90-day deadline to return the money. After that, the
Return Money
option won’t be available.
How to return a payment
Go to
Activity
in your Wise account.
Select the payment you want to return.
Click
Return Money
.
Review and confirm.
The money will be sent back to the account it came from.
You can learn more about how long a transfer usually takes here.
Swift payments
We can return Swift payments. All currencies you can receive by Swift can also be returned.
Learn which currencies you can receive via Swift

title: How do I receive CAD with Interac Autodeposit
content:
For personal customers, to receive CAD with Interac Autodeposit, all you have to do is set up your
CAD account details
and register your email with Interac. Here’s how:
Get
CAD account details
if you don’t have them already
From your CAD, select
Interac e-Transfer
.
Select
Manage Interac e-Transfer details
, confirm your email and
Register
.
You’ll receive an email from Interac — follow the instructions in the email to complete your registration
You’ll then receive a confirmation email that your Interac account is registered with Peoples Trust, our CAD banking partner.
You can now share your email address with others so they can send CAD directly to your Wise account.
This feature isn't available for business accounts at this time.
The availability of money upload methods, such as Interac, will depend on a number of factors including your verification status with us. Upload methods are not part of our Services, they are services provided by third parties. We cannot guarantee the use of any particular upload method and may change or stop offering a particular upload method at any time without notice to you.
Learn more about our Terms of Use
.
How does it work?
Interac is an interbank network for receiving e-transfers (payments) without sharing your bank details.
When others send money to your registered Interac email address, you'll receive it into your Wise account.
There are no fees to receive Interac payments
You can receive payments 24/7 — usually they arrive within 20 minutes
You can receive 25,000 CAD per day. However, the sender’s bank may have limits on how much they can send.
You can only have one Autodeposit account per email address, so if you connect your email with Wise, it’ll remove Interac Autodeposit with your other bank.
Do I need to claim payments?
With Interac Autodeposit, your money will be sent directly to your Wise account so there’s no need to claim it.
You can only receive with Interac using Autodeposit — we don’t currently support Interac e-Transfers which require you to manually claim the payment.
How can I unregister Interac Autodeposit with my Wise account?
Navigate to your CAD and select your account number.
Choose
Manage Interac e-Transfer
under your details.
Select your currently registered email alias.
Select
Delete
to unregister your address.
If you accidentally disconnected your Autodeposit from Wise just follow the initial steps above to re-register your account.

PayNow
.
If your Wise account address is in Singapore, you can't hold more than the equivalent of 20,000 SGD in your personal Wise account. This is because we’re regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) under the

Payments to your business account details
You have 2 sets of account details for different purposes, along with a PayNow QR code.
1. Receiving through FAST
You can get instant payments 24/7 with FAST. It can sometimes take up to 1 working day.
When your sender enters your account details in their banks’ system, they may see “Wise Asia-Pacific Pte. Ltd. (Formerly TransferWise)” as the bank. This is because Wise is a Non-Bank Financial Institution directly connected to the FAST network.

You can get money from platforms like Google or Stripe through GIRO and MEPS, which is common for receiving salaries. This is only available to businesses.

title: How do I receive money with my USD account details?
content:
Account details are a set of details for a currency to receive money.
Once you have your USD account details, you’ll have your own account and routing number where you can receive United States dollars.
This isn’t a bank account, but you can receive money in the same way by sharing the details with friends, family, clients or companies, so that they can send you money.
They can pay you domestically from their bank in the US, or internationally by Swift. Once you receive the money, it’ll be credited to your USD.
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Opening new account details
You’ll first need to
add USD to your main account
. Then you select
Get your account details
and follow the steps. 

We’ll need to verify you before we can provide details to you.
USD account details are not available in some regions.
Learn more about getting USD account details
Checking your account details
To see your details once you’ve got them, on the app or web:
Go to USD
Select the account details button just under the currency
View and copy your account details to share with others
You can also
request a payment
from your sender or download an
account details document
.
How your USD account details work
Here are some general details about your account details.
Domestic payments
International payments
You can receive
ACH and wire transfers from the US
Swift in USD from outside the US
You can't receive
Currencies other than USD
Currencies other than USD and payments from
these countries
Receiving
fees
No fees for ACH or 6.11 USD for wires
6.11 USD for Swift
Receiving
limits
Starts from 20 million USD
Starts from 20 million USD
Receiving
speed
ACH: Usually within hours, and up to 4 working days. Wire: Usually within minutes, and up to 1 working day.
Usually 1 working day, sometimes up to 6 working days
Keep in mind that you can only receive USD to your USD account details. Other currencies will be rejected back to the sender.
Payments from inside the US
If your sender’s USD bank account is in the US, then share your domestic USD details for receiving ACH or domestic wire payments. This includes your:
full name
routing number
account number
There’s
a small fee
to receive domestic USD wires into your Wise account. ACH payments are generally free to receive, except for businesses based in Canada or the Netherlands, where there's
a small fee
.
The sender’s bank will give them an estimate when they set up the payment — for any details about how long it takes, reach out to your sender so they can ask their bank.
Payments from outside the US
If your payer isn't able to make a domestic transfer, you can use your USD details to receive Swift payments. They can send Swift payments to your account from
most places
in the world.
If your sender’s USD bank account is outside the US, share your international account details for receiving Swift  payments. This includes your:
full name
Swift/BIC code
account number
bank name and address
You can see the bank name and address when viewing your account details. Make sure they include your address for the payment — we have to reject Swift payments that don’t have addresses.
There’s
a small fee
to receive international USD payments into your Wise account and
correspondent banks might apply their own fees
.
If you don’t have access to USD account details or you want to receive another way, you can also receive USD to your GBP account details.
Read more about that here
Frequently asked questions
How should my sender enter my account details?
Your sender will need to enter your account details in their bank’s online form or let a banker fill it out in person or over the phone. Here’s more info about your details:
Account holder
– your name as it appears on your Wise Account
ACH or Wire routing number
- your routing number ending in 3150, 9519, or 9628
Account number
– this is your unique account number
Account type
–
Checking
Bank name and address
– our partner institution’s details that processes your incoming payments.
Swift/BIC
– only used for international payments sent from outside the US.
When setting up the transfer, your sender should add your account details manually instead of selecting the bank name.
Your sender's bank may ask for an e-teller ID, but your account doesn’t have this.
What’s the difference between ACH and wires?
ACH (ABA) payments
, also known as electronic fund transfers, are the most common payment method in the US. They’re usually free, or cost a lower fee for your sender and they typically take 1–3 working days to reach Wise.
ACH is commonly used for salary, direct deposits, platform earnings and any non-urgent payment.
Wire payments,
also known as domestic wires, are faster, but are usually more expensive than ACH. Wires typically cost 20-30 USD for the sender, and there’s
a small fee
to receive wires to your USD Wise account details. Wires typically arrive within 1 working day.
Wires are commonly used for large or urgent payments such as for real estate, car purchases or large investments.
What if my sender used the wrong account details?
If money was sent to the wrong account details, we may have to reject the money back to the sender, or in some cases it won’t even reach us.
To get the money back to the sender it usually takes:
3-5 working days for domestic payments
5-10 working days for international Swift payments
If the money hasn’t gotten back to them in that time, they should get in touch with their bank to set up a trace on the money. We can’t track money that was sent to the wrong details.
Learn more on how we keep the money in your account safe
and
about earning interest on your Wise account if you live in the US
.

title: How to use EUR details to receive other currencies via Swift
content:
Having EUR account details allow you to not only receive EUR internationally via Swift, but also 5 other currencies: AUD*, CAD, GBP, JPY, and USD.
Receiving payments
internationally via Swift
means that the sender's bank uses the Swift system instead of domestic payment methods. There's a fee to receive Swift payments, which you can see on
our pricing page
.
These payments usually go through several correspondent banks
which may charge fees
.
Because it goes through multiple banks before reaching Wise, it might take up to 6 working days to get Swift payments credited to your account depending when and from where it was sent.
*You can't receive AUD by Swift if your address is in Australia.
What details do you need to share to receive a payment?
To receive a payment via Swift to your EUR IBAN, you should share:
The currency you want to receive
Your IBAN
Your BIC*
Your account address**
*A BIC code identifies banks and financial institutions. It says who and where in the world they are — a sort of international bank code or ID.
**If a bank asks for a bank name and address, use the address shown in your EUR account details. Although Wise isn’t a bank, these details will work for processing your transfer.
We provide a BE (Belgian) IBAN, which any bank can use to send you all the currencies we list. If a bank mentions using an IBAN from a different country, you can read about
IBAN discrimination
.
You can also use the
Share
button to send details via
Share
,
Copy
, or
Download PDF
.
What happens when you receive a payment?
We'll add the money to your account in the currency sent. For example, if someone sends you JPY, it will be added to your JPY, or we'll add the currency for you if you don't have one.
If you try to receive a payment to your EUR details in a different currency from the ones we listed, we'll automatically reject it. It will be sent back to your sender.

title: How to use GBP details to receive other currencies via Swift
content:
Your account details may include an IBAN that starts with “GB”. This is because Wise is based in the United Kingdom.
Although this IBAN starts with “GB”, you can use it to receive many currencies: GBP, AED, EUR, USD, AUD*, CAD, CHF, CZK, CNY, DKK, HKD, HUF, ILS, JPY, NOK, NZD**, PLN, SEK, RON, SGD, UGX and ZAR.
*You can't receive AUD by Swift if your address is in Australia.
**You can't receive NZD by Swift if your address is in New Zealand.
Receiving payments
internationally via Swift
means that the sender's bank uses the Swift system instead of domestic payment methods. These payments usually go through several intermediary banks
which may charge fees
.
Because it goes through multiple banks before reaching Wise, it might take up to 6 working days to get Swift payments credited to your account. This can depend on when it was sent and
where it was sent from
.
What details do you need to share to receive a payment?
To receive a payment via Swift, you should share:
The currency you want to receive
Your IBAN*
Your account address**
*Your IBAN begins with GB because Wise is based in the UK. For Swift transfers, some banks may require both a Swift/BIC code and an account number. Use
an IBAN calculator
if needed.
**If a bank asks for a bank name and address, use the address shown in your GBP account details. Although Wise isn’t a bank, these details will work for processing your transfer.
You can also use the
Share
button to send details via
Share
,
Copy
, or
Download PDF
.
What happens when you receive a payment?
We'll credit your account in the currency sent. For example, if you receive JPY, it will go into your JPY account - or we'll open one for you if needed.
Some banks may convert the currency because of the UK-based IBAN (starting with GB). Ask your sender to ensure their bank doesn’t convert it unnecessarily.

title: How to receive USD with Swift
content:
If you already have USD account details, you can
receive USD
locally and via Swift.
USD account details are not available in some regions
check where you can get USD details.
Getting USD via Swift with your GBP details
If you can't open USD account details, you can still receive international Swift payments in USD by using your GBP account details.
Learn more about receiving Swift payments to your GBP details
1. Add a GBP currency
From Home, add a GBP currency if you don’t already have one, and select
Get account details
.
Learn more about getting account details
2. Share your Swift details
In your GBP account details use the international account details, and share these details with your sender. These details include your Swift (BIC) code, account number, and GBP IBAN
Let the sender know that you want to receive in USD so you can avoid conversion fees. Some banks might recognise the GBP IBAN and want to convert to GBP, but this isn't necessary.
If their bank asks them the country of the recipient’s (your) bank, tell them to put the United Kingdom.
Some banks or services, such as Stripe, may not accept the GBP IBAN because we're connected to SEPA so you might not be able to send to your GBP details if that's the case.
3. Get paid
You usually receive your payment in under 1 working day, but it can take up to 6 working days. Once we receive your money, we'll add it directly to your USD account.
There's a fee to receive Swift payments, which you can see on
our pricing page
and some banks charge
correspondent fees
for Swift transfers.

title: How do I receive money from Amazon with Wise?
content:
If you sell on multiple Amazon marketplaces, you can withdraw your earnings in different currencies directly into your Wise account. This way, you can avoid high currency conversion fees.
Your Wise account details can be used with Amazon worldwide, but only for local payouts — this means you can link your USD account details to receive payments from Amazon US, or your EUR IBAN to receive payments from Amazon EU. However, you won't be able to connect your account to withdraw other currencies using your GBP or EUR IBAN as Amazon doesn’t make international payouts.
You can receive local currency payments from each marketplace and convert the funds with Wise to get a better exchange rate. No matter where you’re selling, you can manage your payments easily with Wise.
Setting up your account details on Amazon
Go on
Settings
>
Account
Info
>
Payment
Info
>
Bank
Account
Info
Add or replace your bank details
If you’re replacing existing bank account details, select
Replace
If you’re adding new bank account details, select
Assign
Select
Add new deposit method
Select the bank location. This will be the country under the address of the account — for example, the GBP account is in the UK, and the USD is in the US
Add your bank account details — these should be your Wise account details
Verify your bank account details. You can use your
proof of account ownership document
or
bank statement
to verify your account details
Wait for your documents to get verified. Verification can take up to 4 working days
You're all set! Now you can withdraw your earnings into your Wise account.
Non-US residents using Amazon US marketplace
If you’re not a US resident, Amazon will ask for tax verification. Sign the document stating that you're a non-US citizen and not a taxpayer there. Your payments and new product postings will stay pending until you sign it and send it back to Amazon.

title: How do I receive money from PayPal with Wise?
content:
PayPal is an online payment system and digital wallet that allows you to send and receive money. You need to have your own account details on Wise to link them to PayPal.
Learn how to open account details
Before you start: Personal vs. Business accounts
It's important to use the correct account for your payments. PayPal payments for goods, services, or freelancing are considered business payments.
You must use a
Wise Business
account to receive business payments. For personal payments, like from friends and family, you should use your
Wise Personal
account.
What currencies can I send between PayPal and Wise?
You can manage a number of currencies on your PayPal account, but the available transfer options depend on where you live. In general, you can send money from PayPal to Wise, if your local currency is
supported by PayPal
and
Wise offers local account details
for that currency.
In some countries you can select USD as an alternative currency and link your Wise USD details to PayPal. For transfers made in USD, PayPal may charge extra fees.
How do I link my Wise account to PayPal?
The exact steps to link your bank account will depend on where you live. For the most up-to-date guide, we recommend visiting the
PayPal Help Centre
.
On your PayPal, you need to select the option to link your bank account. This usually leads to a list of local banks you can choose from. For now, Wise won’t be appearing on this list, but it
doesn’t mean you can’t link your Wise account
. In most currencies, you can manually add bank details.
When you type in your account details, you’ll see either 'Wise', or our banking partner’s name appear. For some currencies, PayPal asks you to verify your account with microdeposits or via Direct Debit.
What if I have trouble linking my USD account?
Some customers report having trouble linking their Wise USD account details. This can happen if a platform doesn't easily recognise multi-currency account details.
If you see an error, the simplest alternative is to use your Wise card.
Can I use my Wise card instead?
Yes, in most countries, a simpler option is to link your
Wise card
to your PayPal account. You can use it to top-up your account or to
receive money
.
These transactions are usually instant, but keep in mind that PayPal may charge you additional fees for sending money to your Wise card details.
What details should I use for other currencies?
PayPal requires you to link account details that are in the same country as your PayPal account. For example, if you have a UK PayPal account, you can only link your GBP account details.
Below are the general rules for each currency we support. Please note that your PayPal account may work differently:
Your local currency on PayPal
How to link with Wise
AUD, HUF
You can manually type in your Wise account details.
CAD, GBP, MYR, NZD, USD
Wise won't be shown on the list of available banks, but you can select
Manually add your bank account
and use your Wise details.
EUR
In some Eurozone countries, you can link your Wise details manually. In others you may need to reach out to PayPal support to link them for you.
PHP
Use the
01828-001-6
bank code to manually link your Wise PHP account.
SGD
Linking isn't possible at the moment.
What if I need help?
In some cases, PayPal will tell you that you can only link an account if you contact their
customer service
. You can also reach out to them for further assistance if you’re having trouble linking your accounts.
We won’t be able to directly help with your PayPal account, but if your Wise account details aren’t accepted by PayPal or any other platform, let us know. Simply go to your account details on Wise, click on
Tell us where
at the bottom of the page and fill the form. The information you provide will help us improve.

title: How do I receive money from Stripe with Wise?
content:
You can link your Wise account to Stripe to receive money. It only works with some account details.
Learn more about supported accounts on Stripe's website
What currencies can I receive?
Your bank account should be located in the country where the currency you're receiving is the official currency — for example, CAD bank accounts must be based in Canada.
You
can
receive payments in CAD, CHF, DKK, HKD, HUF, JPY, NOK, NZD, PLN, RON, SEK, SGD, USD, and ZAR. You can also receive other currencies to your
EUR account details
.
Learn more about receiving payouts on Stripe's website
What currencies can I
not
receive?
Payments in AED, AUD, CNY, CZK, ILS, and UGX
won’t work
, even if you can connect the details in the Stripe platform.
How do I link Stripe to Wise?
Log into your
Stripe Dashboard
Open
Settings
in the left-hand menu
Select
Bank
accounts
and
scheduling
Select
Add
a
bank
account
Enter your Wise account details from
Home
and choosing the
currency
you’d like the account details for
What do I do if my Wise account is blocked?
A common reason for blocked accounts on Stripe is making typos or mistakes in the account details they link. When entering your Wise account details, always double-check these are correct. If you get these wrong, get in touch with Stripe and have the correct details ready so they can help fix the problem.
Another reason is if you go into a negative balance in Stripe because of refunds or disputes. If this happens, Stripe will automatically try to make a direct debit from the linked account (in this case — Wise) to make the refund. This won’t work for NZD.
Get in touch with Stripe if this happens, and they’ll help you arrange another way of clearing the negative balance.
Talk to our business team
if you're a fast-growing or medium-large e-commerce business.

title: How do I receive money from Upwork with Wise?
content:
Upwork is a freelancing platform that helps businesses and individuals conduct business, and for freelancers to get paid.
Upwork operates in USD. If you decide to get paid in a different currency, the platform will convert the money at an exchange rate decided by Upwork before paying out to your Wise account.
You can receive money from Upwork in a number of currencies, but it’s best to get paid in USD and convert the money within Wise. Regardless of what country you live in, you can opt to receive payments in USD to your Wise account.
Set up your USD account details on Upwork
Make sure you already h
ave
USD account details
with Wise before you start.
On Upwork:
G
o to
Settings
Select
Get paid
Select
Payment details
and
Add method
Select
Direct to US Bank (USD)
and
Set up
Add your USD account details from Wise
Check the box to validate the information provided — you’ll have to confirm this by entering your Upwork password
Confirm the details by selecting
Add account
Upwork might send you 1 or 2 microdeposits to your Wise USD account to verify your ownership of the account. This can take 1–3 days to arrive, and you’ll need to go to Upwork and confirm the amounts received
You're a
ll set! Now you can get paid in USD from Upwork.
For getting paid in other currencies, you can select
Direct to local bank
and enter your account details. Upwork might say they can’t find the branch of the bank — in that case, you’ll need to add the bank address for your account details by selecting
Enter branch address manually.
Read our blog about getting paid on Upwork
Learn more on Upwork support

title: How do I receive money from Etsy with Wise?
content:
Etsy is a marketplace platform where businesses and individuals can sell products from around the world.
When creating your Etsy account, you’ll need to enter your Shop country — this is where you or your business is located) and your Shop currency (this is the currency you want to withdraw from Etsy.
Make sure you choose the currency that works best for your business — this will help you save on conversion fees.
Depending on your location, Etsy may send you money in different currencies. Make sure you check
supported currencies
in your region when setting up your account.
Learn more about Etsy’s conversion fee
Setting up your Wise account details on Etsy
Make sure you already have
account details
with Wise for the currency you want to get paid in.
On the Etsy website, or Sell on Etsy app:
Select
Shop Manager
Select
Finances
and
Payment
settings
Next to bank details, select
Update
You might need to confirm your bank information if you haven’t before
Add your account details from Wise
If you're using USD, Etsy might send you 1 or 2 microdeposits to your Wise account to verify your ownership of the account. This can take 1-3 days to arrive, and you’ll need to go to Etsy and confirm the amounts you received
You're all set! Now you can get paid from Etsy
Learn more about getting paid on Etsy

title: How do I receive money from GOAT with Wise?
content:
GOAT is a platform where individuals and businesses can list and sell authentic sneakers, apparel, and accessories. Users can receive their payments in USD to their Wise account.
As a seller, you can only set and manage your seller account on the GOAT app. It operates in USD, so all fees are listed in USD. But you can transact in any of the 11 currencies listed on their app. GOAT will always first convert from USD to your preferred currency at their
defined exchange rate
and
conversion fee
.
If you reside in a country outside the US, we recommend using your Wise account to receive USD from GOAT — it’ll help you save on conversion fees. When you receive the USD, you can use Wise to
convert to your preferred currency
and
withdraw to your local bank account
.
Setting up your USD account details on GOAT
Make sure you already have
USD account details
with Wise before you start.
Make sure your seller request has been approved on GOAT by selecting
Sell
on their app
Select
Credits
— you’ll be prompted to go through 2-step verification with your phone number
Select
Cash
out
and
Cash
out
method
Add your Wise USD account details
Once your cash-out method has been approved, your payment can take 1–3 working days to be deposited into your USD account.
Learn more about getting paid on GOAT
For the most up to date information on your fees, please see the
Terms of Use
for your region.

title: Can my business use Wise?
content:
Wise Business works for all different types of businesses — from freelancers and entrepreneurs to large-scale enterprises. When you add your details, we’ll let you know if we support businesses in your country.
Wise works for:
sole traders and freelancers
limited and public companies
partnerships
charities and trusts in the European Economic Area (EEA), Canada, US, Switzerland, Australia, and New Zealand
If your business is registered in Brazil
, we only support
Empresário Individual, including MEI (Micro Empresa Individual)
Sociedade Limitada Unipessoal
Sociedade Unipessoal de Advocacia
Wise doesn’t work for:
charities and trusts in countries not listed above
unregistered charities
businesses involved in cryptocurrency, tobacco, adult content, or other prohibited activities.
See the full list of prohibited activities
Superannuation Funds or Self Managed Superannuation Funds (SMSF) in Australia
businesses who offer services or goods relating to Cuba with US relations
businesses with bearer shares — regardless of the jurisdiction they are incorporated in

title: Can my business get a Wise card?
content:
You can use your card to pay business expenses and spend online or in-store anywhere in the world. If you don’t have the currency in your account, we will automatically convert the amount for you.
The Wise Business card is currently available for Wise Business users in the following countries and regions. You can also get a Wise digital card for your business.
Europe
Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France (only Metropolitan), Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Ireland, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, Gibraltar (British Overseas Territory) and the UK Crown Dependencies: Guernsey, Isle of Man, and Jersey.
Other countries in the European area:
Andorra, Saint Barthélemy, Falkland Islands [Malvinas], French Guiana, Guadeloupe, Cayman Islands, Monaco, Saint Martin (French part), Martinique, Saint Pierre and Miquelon, Réunion, Saint Maarten (Dutch part), Mayotte
North America
Canada
US
Asia
Singapore, Japan and Philippines.
Oceania
Australia and New Zealand.
We aim to get your new card to you in 3 working days if you’re in Singapore, in 2–6 days if you’re in the UK, within 2 weeks if you’re in Europe, and up to 3 weeks if you’re in Australia and New Zealand. But how long it takes depends on the country you live in, so check the delivery estimate.
South America
Brazil
My country isn't listed
We’re working hard to make cards available everywhere. In the meantime, if your country or region is not on the list you can go on our
waitlist
, and we’ll let you know when cards are coming your way.

title: Fees for using Wise Business
content:
How much does it cost to open a Wise Business account?
For businesses in Brazil, Switzerland, UK, and the EEA (except Germany, Netherlands, and Italy) you'll need to pay a registration fee when signing up.
For all other regions besides India, signing up is free but there's a fee to get account details. The fee depends on where your business is registered.
How to find the exact fee amount
Go to
our pricing page
.
Scroll down to the Receiving money section to see how much it costs to get account details.
If you want to check the pricing for other countries, click the
circular flag icon
in the top right to change your country.
Some countries cost us more to verify you, so we might charge you a higher fee. You can only
pay the fee using your debit/credit card
or by using money in your account. If you want to
pay using money in your account
, you would first need to add money to your business account.
Note
: you can't pay with your
Wise card
— instead you can pay directly using money in your account.
After paying the fee, we’ll get started on
verifying your business
. Once verified, you’ll get your account details. If we can’t verify your business, we’ll refund the fee back to you.
You only have to do these 2 steps for the first set of account details you get. After that, you can freely get account details for any of the other currencies that have them.
All of your account details will also show in your Account tab in the Account Services section.
How much does it cost to convert money in the Wise Business account?
There’s a small fee to convert between currencies — either in your account, or if you spend on your Wise Business card in a currency you don’t have in your account.
When you convert money in your account, it’s always with the real exchange rate. And we always show you our fees upfront, so there are no surprises.
For more information, see:
our pricing page
how we determine our fees
fees for using your Wise card
spending in a currency you don’t have in your account

title: Are there any restrictions on business transfers?
content:
Wise is licensed to make personal transfers in all the countries we operate in. However in some cases, business transfers are slightly more limited. There are some currency routes which we can’t support business transfers to or from due to the way we’re regulated in those countries.
For the majority of
our supported currencies
there are no restrictions on Wise receiving money from your business.
Learn more about each currency and their limitations by searching for "Guide to [currency] transfers".
Note
, t
he name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Currencies with business limitations

Yes
Yes
See the business types we can support for Australia
here
.

No
No
Both the sender and the recipient of a transfer to BDT must be a privately owned account of an individual.
BRL
Yes
Yes
As defined by the Central Bank of Brazil, if you send money to or from BRL, you (either as a person or a business) must provide the corresponding
transfer nature code
.
CNY
Yes
Yes
Businesses in China can't receive CNY from AED and TRY
COP
Yes
No
The originating currency can arrive to us from a business or personal account, however we cannot make transfers to any businesses in Colombia

No
Yes
Individuals can send IDR to businesses, but businesses can't send IDR. Sole traders can create a account and send IDR, but the sender name would be the name on the personal account.

No
Yes
You can send a maximum of 1,500,000 INR in total per day to business recipients in India.
JPY
Yes
Yes
We can send transfers from JPY to a business bank account. We can only accept business deposits once personal and business verification are both completed.
MYR
No
Yes
Individuals can send MYR to businesses, but businesses can't send money from MYR. Businesses can send out MYR from their currencies.
NPR
Yes
No
The originating currency can arrive to us from a business or personal account, however we cannot make transfers to any businesses in Nepal.
NZD
Yes
Yes
Excluding trusts. See the business types we can support for New Zealand
here
.

Yes
No
The recipient of a transfer to PKR must be a privately owned account of an individual.

Yes

Only businesses that are registered in Turkey can make bank transfers from Turkey.

No

Both the sender and the recipient of a transfer to TZS must be a privately owned account of an individual.

Yes
No
Wise can only make transfers to personal banks in Ukraine. The originating currency can arrive to us from a business or personal account, however we can't make transfers to any businesses in Ukraine.
USD
Yes
Yes
See the
business types
we can support for the US.

Yes

title: How do I send money as a business?
content:
If you wish to make transfers on behalf of a company, you’ll need to set up a Wise Business account with us.
To open an account, provide details about your business and the person who will manage the account. And depending on the amount of money you wish to send and where, we will need to verify your account before sending any money or opening a currency.
Learn more about verification and what details we’ll need
Please note:
When you are sending money on behalf of your business, the money must come from your business bank account. This must be the same as the name of the business registered with us.
If you have sent us money for a transfer from a business bank account but the transfer has been set up as a personal transfer (or vice versa), please let us know.
If you own or wish to make payments on behalf of 2 or more businesses, please
get in touch
and we can help set this up.
If your business ever becomes incorporated, or changes its entity type, you’ll need to be re-verified and provide proof of the change.

title: Can I send from a business bank account?
content:
Yes, as long as you have a Wise Business account. Unfortunately, we can’t accept payments from business bank accounts if you only have a personal Wise account.
Learn more about sending money as a business
Note
: payments from charities and trusts are business payments and must be done on a Wise Business account.
Check here if your business can use Wise
.
What should I do if I’ve already sent money from a business bank account?
If you’ve already sent money from a business bank account and you don’t have a Wise Business account — or you’ve accidentally used your personal Wise account — here’s what you can do:
If you have a Wise Business account, but accidentally used your personal Wise account you don't need to do anything.
We can normally fix this ourselves, and send your money anyway. Sometimes we might need additional information from you, but if we do, we'll get in touch by email.
If you don't have a Wise Business account, but used your personal Wise account we can't process your payment
. The easiest way to fix this is to cancel the transfer yourself. Then,
open a Wise Business account
and restart your transfer. Verifying a new account can take 4-7 business days, so your transfer will take longer.
How can I cancel my transfer?
Whether you can cancel your transfer depends on how far along it is in our system. To see what stage it’s at,
track the transfer
in your account. From there, match the status of your transfer to the correct heading below before reading on.
If you've just set up your transfer or your money is still on it's way to us you can cancel yourself
. You may see a different message for this step, like ‘Your money may still be in your bank account’, or ‘Sorry, we didn’t get the money yet’. This is fine — you should still be able to cancel, and we’ll refund the money.
Learn more about cancelling transfers you haven't paid for yet
If we've received your money and it's being processed we’ll stop the transfer and send an email to you
. It’ll ask whether you’d like to:
tell us this if is a business transfer and set up a Wise Business account to continue.
or cancel your transfer and get a refund.
Tip:
This email is sent automatically — so it's usually faster and smoother to fix this over email, than it is to get in touch with us.

title: How do I move money between my Wise business and personal accounts?
content:
It’s easy to move money between your Wise accounts. First, you’ll need to make sure you
open bank details in the desired currency
. Then, follow these steps:
Find your bank details for receiving money
Switch to the account
you want to receive money into
Click on the currency you want to receive to see your bank details
Keep these bank details on hand — you’ll need them to set up your transfer
You can also send money to your other account using the registered email, phone number, or
Wisetag
of your other account.
Learn more about receiving money without account details
Set up your transfer
Switch to the account you want to send money from
Click on the currency you want to send
Click Send, and enter the amount
Create a recipient using the bank details for the account you’re sending to
You’ll receive the money directly into that account

title: Can I have more than one business account?
content:
If you run multiple businesses, you can have Wise accounts for each one. All of your accounts can live under one set of login details.
To set up additional business accounts:
Go to your Wise account on the web or the iOS app, and select your name icon in the top corner
Select
Open a business account
Follow on-screen instructions
You can only have one account per business or entity.
Invite team members to your business accounts

title: Getting paid to your Wise Business by card, Apple Pay or Google Pay
content:
With a Wise Business account in the EEA, the UK, Australia, Hong Kong, New Zealand, Singapore, Canada or the US, you can request a payment from anyone and send them a link to pay you for your goods or services by credit, debit card, Apple Pay or Google Pay. This feature isn't available in any other regions.
Find out more about
accepting card payments
in your business.
How to apply for this feature
Any business with a registered address in Australia, the EEA, Hong Kong, New Zealand, Singapore, the UK, Canada, or the US can
apply to receive card payments, Apple Pay or Google Pay payments
. When you apply, you’ll need to:
provide the address of your business website
tell us what your business does
agree to how we charge card fees
In some cases, we may not be able to provide this feature for your business, even in eligible countries. We're continuously working on expanding our product to all customers and hope that this feature can be made available to you in the future.
How to get paid by card, Apple Pay or Google Pay
Once you have this feature, from the App or Web:
Request a payment
, create an invoice in Wise, or share your Quick Pay link with your customer
Select
Card
,
Apple Pay
or
Google Pay
as a payment method
Share the link with your customer
We’ll tell you when your customer has paid, and we’ll email them proof of their payment
We’ll notify you again when your money arrives
Learn more about requesting payments
What card types and currencies work
We currently support credit and debit Visa and Mastercard payments in AUD, CAD, CHF, CZK, DKK, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, JPY, NOK, NZD, PLN, RON, SEK, SGD and USD.
What it costs you
The fee we charge depends on the card your customer pays with and it's the same for cards, Apple Pay and Google Pay.
If your business is based in Australia
Credit or debit card

1.6% + 0.3 AUD

3.5% + 0.3 AUD
If your business is based in the EEA or the UK

2.78% + 1.98 HKD

3.58% + 1.98 HKD
All business cards
3.58% + 1.98 HKD
If your business is based in New Zealand
Credit or debit card

If your business is based in Singapore

3.7%

3.7%
Our fees already cover Visa and Mastercard card scheme and interchange fees, so you won’t be charged anything extra.
Once your payment arrives, you can check the fee we charged in your
Activity
.
What it costs your customer
We don't charge your customer anything, but their bank or card issuer might charge them a fee if:
they're paying you in a foreign currency
they’re paying with a credit card
their bank treats the payment to Wise as a
cash advance
If your customer isn’t sure what they’ll be charged, ask them to check with their card issuer.
How long it takes to receive the payment
It can take up to 14 days to pay out your money after you receive a payment from your customer, depending on the type of business you are running.
For some currencies, payouts sometimes can take longer. But you can always see the estimated payout time in the
Transactions details
section by selecting the transaction in your activity feed.
In cases where we decide to close your account, we may retain your funds for a period of up to two months. This retention period allows us to address any incoming chargeback requests for which you remain liable.
This section corresponds to Schedule 1 settlement timeframes in the
EEA Agreement for Acquiring Payment Services
.
How to issue refunds
You can now issue refunds to anyone who paid you by card through the
Request payment
option. The refund will go back to the original paying card.
You can go to your
Transactions
on the home screen, or:
Go to the
Payments
tab
Select either
Payment links
or
Invoices
Select the
Request
, and then the specific payment
Select
Refund payment
Choose how much you want to refund — it can be any amount up to the full amount paid. You can choose to issue a few partial refunds, but the total shouldn't be more than the full amount
If you refund your customer, you won't get a refund on the fees you paid for using our service when charging the payer.
Refunds to the card can take 2-10 working days to arrive.

title: Getting paid to your Wise Business by Quick Pay
content:
Quick Pay is a fast and easy way to receive money to your Wise Business account. With Quick Pay your customers can pay through your website, in person, or through invoices.
To receive money, you can share your Quick Pay link (URL) or QR code. This allows other Wise users to send you money, or
pay with their card
if you have this feature available.
How do I set up Quick Pay?
Quick Pay is available to all business accounts that have:
A currency with account details
.
E
nabled
2-step authentication
.
C
ompleted
business account verification
.
Once your Wise account is set up, you can get your Quick Pay link or QR code by:
Going to
Payments.
Select
Quick Pay.
Select
Get started
to see your link and QR code
You can now share your link or QR code with anyone. When they click the link or scan the QR code, customers will be directed to log into Wise and pay you — even if they don't have your account details.
How can I receive money using Quick Pay?
To receive money for your business, you can use your Quick Pay link or QR code in various ways.
Receiving through your website
If you want to get paid through your website, copy your
Quick Pay link
and add it as a link or button on your website. When they select it, they'll be given directions on how to pay so that they can send you money.
You can paste your unique link into your invoice templates, emails, and any other payment requests that you send to customers.
Learn more about getting paid through your website
Receiving in person
or at your business location
The easiest way to get paid in person is to use your unique
QR code
. When your customers scan it, they’ll be directed to log into Wise and pay you.
You can add your QR code to invoices, or display it at your location so that people can scan and pay through their phone.
If you want to have a paper copy instead of on the phone, you can print it out and display it at your business location by choosing
Download.
Learn more about getting paid in person
Receiving through an invoice
Most invoicing tools, such as QuickBooks or Xero, will allow you to customise your invoices so that you can add your Quick Pay link and QR code to your invoice template.
Learn more about how to use Quick Pay with accounting or invoicing software like Xero or QuickBooks
How it works
How can my customers pay?
Currently, the only payment methods they can use are Pay with Wise or
card payments (if activated)
.
For the payer to send you money, all they need to do is click on your Quick Pay link or scan your QR code and follow the steps. If they're Paying with Wise they'll need to log into their Wise account.
Can I customise my Quick Pay link?
For security reasons, we're not able to support customisation for Quick Pay Links or QR codes, but we're working on adding this in the future.
However, if your business name has changed,
contact us
to request a new Quick Pay link.
People can't find me using my Quick Pay link, what should I do?
If this happens your Quick Pay link might be toggled off. To toggle it on:
Go to
Payments
Select
Quick Pay
.
Select
Manage
and toggle it
on
.
Can I pre-fill the amount and currency of the payment request?
Yes, you can do it in two ways.
Through our website
On Web only, you can set an amount and currency with your request. To do this:
Go to
Payments and select Quick Pay
Select
Personalise
Enter the amount, currency and description.
This will pre-fill your personal Quick Pay link with the details. You can Copy the link and share it with your payers. The amount and currency will be defined.
Editing the URL
If you want more flexibility you can also manually modify the Quick Pay link yourself. To do this:
Copy your Quick Pay link (ex.
wise.com/pay/business/
YOUR_BUSINESS
)
Append
?currency=CURRENCY&amount=AMOUNT
and set
CURRENCY
and
AMOUNT
to what you would like to request.
To pre-fill the amount and currency for anything else, simply change the values in the URL.
For an example of a 100 GBP request, the final link for your business should look something like
http://wise.com/pay/business/YOUR_BUSINESS_NAME?currency=GBP&amount=100

title: Tips for paying invoices
content:
If you need to make transfers or pay invoices from your business account, the tips below should assist you in making sure your money arrives safely and your recipient gets the exact amount they need.
Adding a reference
When creating a transfer with us, we give you the option to add a reference in Step 4 of set up. For a lot of business transfers, this reference is crucial, so please check the table below to see the limitations on references in our receiving currencies.

Sent with bank transfer

Sent with bank transfer

Sent with bank transfer

50 characters
Sent via email only

35 characters
Sent with bank transfer

35 characters
Sent with bank transfer

12 characters (25 characters with Bankgiro payment)
Sent with bank transfer or Bankgiro

Sent with bank transfer

Sent with bank transfer

25 characters
Sent with bank transfer

Sent with bank transfer

Sent with bank transfer

Sent via email only

Sent via email only

Sent via email only

Sending an exact amount
Wise offers
guaranteed rate
. As long as we receive your money within a specified amount of time, then the rate you saw when creating the transfer is the rate you’ll get.
Enter how much your recipient gets
You can enter exactly how much you’d like recipient to get. This way, we’ll calculate any fees and conversion for you. Then all you have to do is pay within the
guaranteed rate period
.
Pay within the guaranteed rate period
We’ll show you when we guarantee the exchange rate. If you’re not paying instantly, we'll show you how long we guarantee the rate for.
As long as you pay within the guaranteed rate period, we convert your money at that rate and send the exact amount.
Some payment methods like debit or credit card take seconds. Others, such as bank transfers, take longer which means you need to pay before the guaranteed rate expires to send the exact amount.
If you can open a Wise account, you can choose to pay using it. This means we can take the exact amount from your account even if we can’t guarantee the exchange rate. You just need enough money in your account to cover the transfer amount.

title: Receiving payments for Indian businesses
content:
Your international clients can send money to your Indian business using your
account details
with Wise.
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
What are account details?
These are your very own account details to receive money in EUR, GBP, USD, AUD, CAD, NZD, SGD and HUF.
This isn’t a bank account, but you can receive money in the same way by sharing the details with friends, family, clients or companies, so that they can send you money.
How to receive money
Sign up for a Wise Business account
and fill in the necessary details about your business
Choose the currencies
that you want to have account details for and finish the
verification steps
Share your account details with your clients
Clients can send you money using Wise or their bank accounts
Once you receive the money we'll
automatically convert the money into INR
and transfer it to your verified Indian bank account that you provided during verification
You'll
receive the payment
to your INR bank account within 1-2 days
and
receive the transfer e-FIRC
by email within 7 days.
Keep in mind:
Bank transfers from your clients using the local payment methods usually take 0–2 working days to arrive in your account. International Swift transfers take 2–6 working days.
We
additionally
charge you an equivalent of 2 USD in the requested currency for the transfer to your INR bank account to issue e-FIRC
automatically
. You can check our
pricing page
for more details.
Where can I find my account details?
You can find your account details on your Wise account as follows:
On the web
Go to
Payments
on the left-hand menu
Scroll down to view all your
Account Details
On the app
Go to
Payments
tab on the bottom navigation
Scroll down to view all your
Account Details
How much can I receive?
Based on regulations and our operational policies:
the maximum you can receive is 29,000 USD or equivalent per transaction
the minimum you can receive is 5 USD or equivalent per transaction
If the transferred amount isn't within these limits, it'll be returned to the sender.
Can I hold or send money using the account details?
These account details can't be used to send money or hold in Balances, due to regulatory reasons. We automatically convert the funds to INR and send them to your Indian bank account once they are deposited in the account details.
Can I submit my Importer Exporter Code (IEC)?
Yes, you can. If you don't have one and would like to get it, head to the Registration Certificate session on the
Directorate General of Foreign Trade page
.
Can I download an e-FIRC?
Yes, you can. The e-FIRC for your transfer will automatically be sent to your Wise registered email 2-3 days after your transfer is complete. Opt in to email notifications to receive this email.
If you don't get the email:
Go on the Wise website and select your transfer
Select
e-FIRC PDF
to download
Can I download a GST invoice?
Yes, you can. If you have provided a GST ID, you will be able to download the GST invoice per transfer.

title: Receiving payments for Hong Kong businesses
content:
Right now, new USD account details are only available in some regions.
Learn more about getting USD account details
If you already have USD account details, they’ll work as normal.
Learn more about other ways to receive USD to your Wise account
Businesses registered in Hong Kong can receive money from international clients by using their account details with Wise.
Once you receive the money, you'll need to use it within 90 days. For example, you can withdraw it, send it to someone, or convert it to another currency in your Wise account.
How do I set up my Wise Business account?
Sign up for a Wise Business account
and fill in details about your business
Choose the currencies you want to have account details for
Finish the
verification steps
We'll verify your Hong Kong business account within 4-7 working days
How do I receive money?
Once your account is verified, you can
share your account details
with your clients so they can send you money. They can pay from their bank account, or their Wise account if they have one.
What currencies can I receive?
You can receive both local and international payments to your Wise account.
To receive money, just share your account name and details to your clients, so they can send you the amount from their bank account or Wise account.
You may be able to receive without account details —
learn more here
Local account details
With local account details, you can receive money by domestic bank transfer in the countries that use that currency. For example, receiving GBP from the UK.
Wise provides local account details to Hong Kong businesses to receive money in AUD, CAD, EUR, GBP, HUF, NZD, SGD, TRY, and USD.
International account details
With International account details you can receive money
from most countries
via Swift international payments. For example, receiving USD sent from a bank account in the UK.
Wise provides international account details in AUD, CAD, EUR, GBP, HUF, NZD, SGD, USD, CHF, CZK, CNY, ZAR, UGX, AED, ILS, DKK, HKD, JPY, NOK, SEK, PLN.
How long does it usually take for the money to arrive?
Once Wise receives your money, it usually takes 1–3 working days to arrive in your bank account. Conversion can take up to 2 working days.
Payments received via international account details take longer, as they might go through correspondent banks, so it can take up to 5 working days.
How much are the fees?
There are no fees for domestic transfers. There are receiving fees for international transfers, USD domestic wires, and CAD international wires.
Check those fees here
For international Swift payments,
intermediary banks
that help send this money can sometimes deduct their own fees from the sender's amount — Wise can't change this. You can ask your sender to opt for the OUR charges — this option allows the sender to cover all the correspondent charges.
How much can I receive?
For most currencies, there's no limit on how much you can receive. But check the relevant currency article under
Using your account details
to be sure.
What happens if my account has no activity for 90 days?
If there's no activity in the 90 days after you've received a payment, your account might be restricted.

title: How do I download an audit report?
content:
You can now download an audit report for your Wise business account. The report will have account details for your currencies, and the amount in each account.
This is only available on the web and on the Android app:
Select your business name at the top right corner
Select
Statements and reports
Select
Audit report
and
Download report
Select the date you want the report for
Select whether you want to authorise team members with access to your account to be included in the report
Select
Download
The audit report is available for Wise Business customers.
Only team members with Admin or Admin/Owner permissions can download this report.
Learn how to change your team's roles
You can also
send the report directly to the Auditor
from your Wise business account.

title: How do I send an audit report directly to the Auditor?
content:
You can now send an audit report directly to the Auditor for your Wise Business. The report will show the account details for each of your currencies, and the total amount of those.
Learn how to download your audit report
This is only available on the web:
Select your business name at the top right corner
Select
Statements and documents
Select
Audit report
and
Send report
Select the date you want the report for
Add the email address of the Auditor
Select whether you want authorised team members with access to your account to be included in the report, and select
Continue
Review the details, select
Preview audit report (PDF)
if you’d like to see the report
Select
Confirm and send to auditor
The audit report is available for Wise business customers.

title: How to use Quick Pay with Xero, QuickBooks, and other invoicing tools
content:
Quick Pay is a Wise service that makes it easy for your business to get paid quickly by customers. When you add your unique Quick Pay link and QR code to your invoices, customers can pay you quickly through Wise — without needing your bank account details, and without having to log in to Wise first.
You can also add the invoice number into the link on your invoice that you generate through your invoice tool.
Here, we'll explain how to use Quick Pay with Xero, QuickBooks and other invoicing tools.
How can I add my link and QR code to my invoices?
If you use accounting software to create invoices, you can add your link and QR code to your invoice template. Do this once, and every future invoice will include the option to pay this way.
Most invoicing and accounting software include the option to create and save a template, which can be automatically used for every invoice. Find the option to create or customise your invoice, and depending on the tool you use, you can then input your link and QR code into the invoice template.
Accessing your link and QR code
On the app or web, choose
Payments
Choose
Quick Pay
Copy
your link and then paste or input it into your invoice template
You can also choose
Download
to get your QR code as a file you can import to your invoice template
Can I add a description to the Quick Pay link?
Yes, you can do this through our website or you can manually adjust the URL. Here's how:
Through the website
Go to
Payments and select Quick Pay
Select
Personalise
Enter the amount, currency and description.
This will pre-fill your personal Quick Pay link with the details. You can Copy the link and share it with your payers. The amount and currency will be defined.
Manually editing the URL
Get your Quick Pay URL
At the end of the URL, add:
description=
After "=" add your text description. i.e.
description=your-text-here
For example, https://wise.com/pay/business/account?amount=123&currency=USD&
description=invoice1234
How to add your link and QR code to invoices in Xero
After you get your Quick Pay link or QR code file from the steps above, sign in to Xero and follow these steps.
Setting up the invoice template in Xero
Chose your
company name
(top left) and then
Settings
Choose
Invoice settings
Choose
Payment services
Scroll to the text that says “If you're wanting a payment service that isn't listed,
add another online payment option
” and choose this link
Choose
Custom Payment URL
Enter an
Account name
. Use something you’ll recognise, like
Wise account
In the
Your custom URL
field, paste your Quick Pay link from Wise. If you want your customer to enter the currency and amount to pay, leave the URL as is. If you want your payment link to include the amount due on the invoice, add this to the end of the link:
?currency=[CURRENCY]&amount=[AMOUNTDUE]&description=[INVOICENUMBER]
In the
Pay now
button text
field, enter
Pay with Wise
, or your own button text
This text will appear on your invoice as a button, which customers can click to pay
Select
Save
After you set up the invoice
Choose the option to
add
online payments and select the Wise payment method you just created
To see how it works, choose
Approve and get link
(top right).
Copy and paste the link into a new browser window to see how the invoice will look for your customers
And it's done! Send your invoice via Xero as normal
How to add your link and QR code to invoices in QuickBooks
After you get your Quick Pay link or QR code file from the steps above, sign in to Quickbooks and follow these steps.
Setting up the template in QuickBooks
Choose the
Gear
icon (top right)
Choose
Custom form styles
Choose
Edit
to change your invoice template
Choose the
Content
tab
Choose the
Footer
area of the invoice preview (right of screen)
Paste your Wise Quick Pay URL into the
Add payment details and footer
field
You may want to add some extra text to explain the link, such as “Please use this link to pay”
Select
Done
to save your changes — and that's it!
Learn how to use Quick Pay to get paid in person
Learn how to use Quick Pay to get paid through your website

title: How to add receipts to your business card transactions
content:
If your business needs to have receipts for accounting or tax claim purposes, or you just want to keep track of your expenses, you can upload a receipt for any of your Wise card transactions.
Here’s how to upload a transaction receipt:
From the transaction (Web and App)
Take a picture of your receipt with your smartphone
Go to your transaction list and select the relevant transaction
Select
Add receipt
(app) or
Upload file
(web)
Select the image that you want to upload and confirm
From the Activity page (Web)
Open the transaction list
Select the
upload attachment symbol
Select the receipt that you want to upload and confirm
If your business account is integrated with Xero, receipts will automatically appear on Xero. The handling of receipts depends on the options you've enabled under
Connection settings
:
