Transactions with attachments: If this feature is enabled, receipts will appear in the
draft bills
and
draft invoices
sections in Xero. You would need to review and publish them.
Card Transactions with receipts: If this feature is also enabled, card transactions are automatically synced as
spend money
transactions in Xero and receipts attached to the payment entries.
Learn more about how to connect your business account to Xero

title: Chargebacks when you use Wise Business to accept card payments
content:
What is a chargeback?
Chargebacks can be made on credit and debit card payments that you receive.
A chargeback happens when a customer who has made a card payment asks their card issuer (their bank) to recall money that they've paid. The whole process is called a “chargeback dispute”.
The reasons for chargebacks can vary, but include:
Goods or services not being delivered
Goods or services not being as described
Transaction being made with a lost or stolen card
The wrong amount being charged
The customer being charged twice
I have received a chargeback – what happens?
When Wise receives a chargeback against a card payment that you’ve received, we’ll take the amount of the chargeback from your account. This ensures that if the chargeback is successful, we’re able to pass the funds back to your customer’s card issuer.
If this happens, we’ll contact you to give you full details of the chargeback and ask for some information from you. You have two options:
Accept the chargeback.
If the chargeback seems legitimate, you can let us know that you accept it. If you accept the chargeback, you won’t receive the money into your bank account. If we’ve already withdrawn money to cover the chargeback amount, you won’t get this back.
Dispute the chargeback.
If you disagree with the chargeback, you'll need to tell us. We'll also need you to provide evidence to support this. For example, you might be able to send us proof of delivery, proof of service, your business terms and conditions, and so on. If you dispute the chargeback, we’ll share the information you give us with your customer’s card issuer. If the dispute is successful, the money will be paid into your account.
If you don’t reply to our request for information, we'll need to accept the chargeback, as we can't defend the chargeback without your help.
Can the chargeback amount vary?
Yes. A chargeback can be made for the total payment amount, or for just part of it.
For example, a customer might make a partial chargeback if some items in their order were fine, but others arrived damaged.
The chargeback amount can also vary for cross-currency transactions, when the exchange rate has changed between the date of the payment and the date of the chargeback.
How long does it take to resolve a chargeback?
The time it takes to resolve a chargeback can vary. This is because both the payer and recipient may need to provide information and evidence about it.
In general, cardholders can raise a chargeback any time up to 120 days after the transaction date.
If you receive a chargeback, you usually have 18-45 days to reply, depending on the card scheme. And if you decide to dispute a chargeback, it can take up to 45 days to learn the outcome.
Do I have to dispute a chargeback?
No. If you agree with the chargeback, you can accept it by replying to the email we sent asking for details.
What happens if I win a chargeback dispute?
If you win a chargeback dispute, you’ll receive payment of the disputed amount into your account.
What happens if I lose a chargeback dispute?
If you lose a chargeback dispute, you won’t receive the money into your bank account. If we’ve already withdrawn money to cover the chargeback amount, you won’t get this back.
Can I lose a chargeback dispute even if I’ve given you extra information?
Yes. When you dispute a chargeback, we'll share the extra information you’ve given us with your customer’s bank so they can review it. They'll decide if the chargeback is valid.

title: How do I open a Wise account for my business?
content:
Wise Business
is an online account that lets you make payments, get paid, and spend money around the world with the mid-market exchange rate in over 40 currencies.  You can also use it to pay invoices, buy inventory, and handle payroll in over 160 countries.
Here are some tips to help you get started.
It’s quick and easy to open an account
It only takes a few minutes to set up your Wise Business account. You just need to enter some details about yourself and your business. We might need to verify your account before completing any transfers for you.
Learn how to verify your business
If you already have a personal account, you can link your new business account to it, so that you can access both with the same login. You can open a business account from the web or app by selecting
Open a business account
from your name icon in the corner.
In some regions there's a fee to open a business account or get account details for receiving money.
Learn more
We’re not a bank, but we are regulated like one
Since 2011, we’ve helped millions of people and businesses send money to over 70 countries. We’re registered with
numerous financial authorities
, so you can do business around the world.
We use HTTPS encryption, and two-factor authentication to protect all your transactions and make sure everything is secure. We’ll never misuse or sell your data.
Read customer reviews on Trustpilot
We use the mid-market exchange rate
Banks and services like PayPal often set their own, unfriendly exchange rates. This means you pay more than you need to when you send, spend, or receive money internationally. And they pocket the difference.
At Wise, we never mark up the exchange rate. You'll always get the
'real' mid-market exchange rate
– like you’d find on independent sources like Google, XE, or Yahoo Finance. Our customers are already saving over £3 million every day.
Compare us to others
You can automate your finances
Our open API lets you connect your account with your business tools. This means you can automate payments and workflows. So you can also build whatever you need to do business better. Get creative!
Wise also works with Xero and your favourite accounting tools. You can connect your accounts to automatically sync your activity, and make accounting a cinch.
Learn more about our API
You can add other users to your account
Our multi-user access supports different permissions and access levels to suit different needs.
Learn more about multi-user access
Get an account and card

title: Why do I need to verify my business?
content:
Verification is one of the ways we keep your business’s money safe, and
comply with the financial regulations
in the countries that we operate in.
That’s why, when you open a business account, we may ask you for a few documents or to confirm your details on a business registry. This helps us verify who you are, that your business is currently trading, and it meets our
acceptable use policy
. Think of it like opening a bank account — except that with Wise you can do the whole thing online, quickly and easily.
Ready to open your business account? Read our
guide to getting verified
.
Why am I being asked to provide additional verification information when I've already been verified?
We know that you provide information to us when you join Wise, but sometimes we need to request up-to-date info about your identity.
We may ask for this to comply with regulatory requirements. If you receive an email from us about this please follow the instructions and provide the necessary information and documents to continue using your Wise account.

title: How can I verify my business?
content:
When you open a Wise Business account, we need to verify some details about your business and the people associated with it. This helps us keep your money safe and comply with financial regulations. We aim to make this process quick and fully online.
What are the verification steps?
Here’s how the verification process generally works:
1. Sign up and tell us about your business.
Start by
registering for your Wise Business account
.
You'll need to provide details like your business registration number, registered and trading addresses, industry, online presence (if any), and information about key people (like directors and owners). We also need personal details for the person opening the account.
If you're not a director, you'll need to provide proof you're authorised to open the account (like a signed letter of authorisation, power of attorney, or board resolution).
2. Pay the one-time fee or set up your first transfer.
For businesses in certain regions (like the EEA, UK, Switzerland, Brazil), there's a mandatory one-time setup fee.
In other regions, you might choose between paying the fee to unlock all features or setting up a transfer.
3. The fee or transfer triggers our main verification review.
Check the fees for your region on our
pricing page
.
4. We review your information.
First, we perform an initial review using the information you provided during sign-up.
5. Provide documents if we ask for them.
Sometimes, we need more information. This might happen if we can't confirm all your details during our initial review, or if your business operates in certain industries, or requests specific features.
If we need more details, our specialist verification team will review your account.
They will email you directly if they need specific documents or information. Please check your inbox (and spam folder).
What documents might I need to provide?
We capture most information during sign-up. However, if our team needs to review your account further, they will email you to ask for specific documents.
These commonly include:
Proof of Business Registration:
Like your certificate of incorporation, articles of association, or an official registry excerpt.
Proof of Address:
For your trading address (e.g. a recent utility bill, lease agreement, or bank statement addressed to the business at that location).
Ownership Documents:
Documents showing who ultimately owns and controls the company (e.g. a recent shareholder register, operating agreement, or partnership deed).
Personal ID:
For the account administrator and potentially other directors or owners.
Are there country-specific requirements?
Yes. Verification requirements can vary depending on your country of registration. We have specific guides for some regions.
If your business is registered in any of the following countries, there may be additional information or documentation we need to collect:
EEA
(European Economic Area)
UK
US
Singapore
Japan
Hong Kong
Philippines
New Zealand
We only support charities registered in Canada, EEA, Switzerland, UK, US, Australia, and New Zealand.
We only support trusts in Canada, EEA, Switzerland, Australia, New Zealand, US, and the UK.
If your business is registered in Brazil, we only support: Empresário Individual (including MEI, Micro Empresa Individual), Sociedade Limitada Unipessoal, and Sociedade Unipessoal de Advocacia.
If your business registered address and trading address are in different countries, we can only offer you services that are available in both countries.
How do I provide my documents?
If our verification team needs documents:
Check your email.
Our team will send an email from a Wise.com address detailing exactly what's needed.
Follow the instructions in the email.
Reply with the requested information and upload any documents through the secure upload link (in the email or in Wise app).
Important:
Don't send documents unless specifically requested by our team via email or through an official prompt in your Wise account.
How long does verification take?
We'll carry out an initial review after you sign up.
If additional verification is needed after you pay the fee or fund a transfer, our team will review your information or documents. This review usually takes up to 10 working days.
We'll email you as soon as verification is complete, or if we need anything else. If your transfer was paused for verification, it will resume once verification is done.

title: How do I get verified using an authorisation letter?
content:
Depending on your position, we may ask you to prove you're authorised to use and communicate with Wise on behalf of the business. Authorisation can be granted with a letter signed by a director, board member, managing partner, or significant controller of the company. You can't authorise it for yourself.
You can download a template below, according to where the business is registered. Make sure to have it ready when we ask for it.
Australia
Europe: Authorisation on behalf of a company (legal entity)
Europe: Authorisation on behalf of a self-employed person (sole trader)
Hong Kong
India: Authorisation on behalf of a Company
India: Authorisation on behalf of a LLP (Partnership)
New Zealand
Philippines: Authorisation on behalf of a self-employed person (sole proprietor)
Philippines: Notarised authorisation on behalf of a company (legal entity)
Philippines: Authorisation on behalf of a partnership
Singapore
Brazil businesses
This option is currently not available for businesses registered in Brazil. The Owner of the business has to register the business account with us and
add an Admin team member
.

title: How does Wise verify my business address?
content:
As a financial institution, Wise needs to verify where your business operates to comply with regulations and keep everyone's money safe.
For all businesses, we collect two addresses:
Registered address
— where your business is officially registered and the one listed on your official business registration document (for example, on your Companies House registration).
Trading address
— where you actually run your business day-to-day (your office, home, shop, etc.)
These may be the same address, but it's also perfectly acceptable for them to be different. This can also be a co-working space.
What can't my trading address be?
Your trading address must be a physical location. It can't be:
A PO Box
A mail forwarding or receiving service
A virtual office
A solicitor's or attorney's office
A business registration agency
How do I add or update my business address?
You can usually update your business address in your Wise account. Make sure you’re currently viewing your business account.
On the website:
Select your business name in the top right corner.
Select
Business details
.
Under
Trading addresses
, select
Edit
.
Enter your physical trading address and select
Save
.
On the app:
Tap on your profile picture in the top-left corner.
Select
Business details
.
You can edit your addresses there.
Good to know:
If your business is already verified, some details (like your registered address) may be locked for security. If you can't edit your address, please
contact us
with your new document ready, and we can help.
What documents do you accept for my trading address?
We need a high-quality photo or PDF of a document that clearly shows your business or business owner's name, full trading address, an issue date, and the sender's name or logo (like your bank or utility provider).
Document Requirements:
Utility bills and bank statements must be
less than 3 months old
.
Other documents (like tax bills, insurance policies, or licences) must be
less than 12 months old
.
The document must be in PDF, JPEG, or PNG format.
Acceptable Documents:
Document Type
Specific Rules
Utility bill
We accept bills for gas, electricity, broadband/internet, or a landline phone. We can't accept mobile phone bills.
Lease or rental agreement
Must be current and signed by both you and the landlord.
Bank or credit card statement
We accept statements from most traditional banks and neobanks (like Monzo, Revolut, or Starling). We can't accept statements from Mercury.
Business licence or registration document
Must be an official government-issued document.
Tax document
A tax return, tax filing, or official correspondence from a tax authority (like HMRC or the IRS).
Business insurance policy
We accept policies including (but not limited to): commercial, liability, business owner's, umbrella, or professional insurance.
Council tax bill
Must be an official council bill or notification.
What if I work from home and don't have bills in my business name?
This is common, and we can accept personal documents in your name.
If you're a
sole trader
, a
freelancer
, or a
significant
owner/director
of a registered company (like an LTD or LLC), we can accept one of the following documents in your
personal name
. The address on the document must match the trading address on your Wise account.
Utility bill (gas, water, electric, landline, broadband)
Council tax bill
Bank statement
Tenancy agreement
An ID with your address
HMRC notifications
Common Questions
Can my registered and trading address be in different countries?
Yes, this is fine. We'll just need to verify your trading address with one of the documents listed above.
Can I change the country of my registered address?
No, it's not possible to change the country your business is registered in. You would need to open a new Wise Business account for the business in the new country.
Are there special rules for the US or Canada?
Yes, there are specific regional restrictions:
Canada:
Your personal residential address cannot be the same as your registered business address.
United States:
Your personal residential address can be the same as your registered business address if you are a sole proprietor (sole trader).
What if I trade from a country where Wise Business isn't available?
You must have a physical trading address in a country where we support Wise Business. We can't accept trading addresses from unsupported countries.
What are some common rejection reasons?
The document is too old.
The document has an SSN on it.
Some of the information isn't visible on the document.
The details on your document don't match the details on your Wise account.
The document isn't uploaded in full (if there's information on both sides, upload both sides).
The document type isn't on the list of acceptable documents (e.g. it's a mobile bill).
The document isn't in a PDF, JPEG, or PNG format.

title: How does Wise verify Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBOs)?
content:
A Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) is the ultimate beneficiary of a business, and can be a person or an entity.
Any individual who exercises ultimate control over the board of directors and has the right to appoint or remove the majority of the board.
Any individual who ultimately owns or controls 25% or more of the shares or voting rights in the body corporate.
We need to make sure we know the identity of all of the people who have ultimate beneficiary ownership of any business we onboard. Identifying and verifying the UBOs of a business helps us prevent unlawful activities. It's also a regulatory requirement for some regions, so depending on where your business is registered, we may have to ask to verify the identity of the UBOs of that business.
Learn more about how Wise verifies businesses
and
why we need to verify businesses
Types of identification we accept
To verify the identity of a UBO, the document needs to have the person's name, photo and date of birth ─ if not, we'll ask for a new, acceptable document. Here are the documents we accept:
Passport (photo page only)
National ID card (with the exception of US military IDs)
Photo driving licence (we don't accept Electronic Driving Licences)
Exceptions
There are a few exceptions:
Companies registered in the EU, Iceland, Liechtenstein and Norway: we don't accept driving licences if the UBO resides in any of those countries.
Brazilian-issued ID card: although this card may not have an expiry date, we can only accept it if it’s been issued in the past 15 years. We’ll also need to see the front and back.
Documents issued in Nigeria: we can only accept passports, driving licences, and the most recent Nigerian national ID. We can't accept paper or digital slips, regional IDs or voter ID documents.
If they’re a refugee: depending on which country you’re in, we can accept more documents than the ones listed above.
Learn about verification for refugees
What other documents do you accept?
Passport from another country
Driving licence from another country
Permanent residency card/Work visa
Copy of naturalisation or citizenship certificate
Student or other visa valid for more than 6 months
If you can't provide any of the documents we listed, reach out to our support team and let us know what documents you have. Our team will be happy to support you.
What do you do with this information?
We only ask for information we need, so that we can run security checks and verify our customers. So we use the information we ask to stay compliant.
Protecting your information is very important to us. All data, financial details, or documents you give us are stored securely, encrypted, and kept private as per our
privacy policy
.
When will Wise get back to me?
We'll aim to get back to you in 5 working days, whether to verify you or to ask for more information. We’ll email you once we’ve verified you. So be sure to check the email you use to log in to Wise first, or go into your notifications on the app or web.
But if you haven't heard from us in that time, you can reach out to our Support team and ask for the status. And if you have a transfer already in progress, we’ll automatically resume it.
What if my document didn't get accepted?
We hope you don’t run into any issues while we verify you, but in case you do, here are some common reasons we might not accept a document:
Unclear image
: Some important information might be covered or blurred. We need to see the full document photo page and the image needs to be clear.
Expired
: Please check that the document is still valid before you upload it, as we can't accept expired documents.
Details don't match
: Please check that you've entered the UBO's name and date of birth correctly on the website. If the details don't match, we can't accept the document.
Missing file
: We may also need to see the back of the document. If there is information on both sides, please make sure you upload both for us.
Wrong document type
: It may be a document type that we can’t accept.
Wrong format
: It may be in a format we can’t accept, like .doc or .docx.

title: I need to edit details on a business account
content:
Here's how you can change your business details on either web or app depending on if your business has been verified or not.
If your business isn’t verified
If your business hasn’t been verified yet, follow these steps to edit your details on either web or app.
Go on your business name in the top corner — make sure you're in your business account
Select
Business details
Select
Edit
for the section you need to change and fill in the form
Select
Request change
If your business is already verified
If your business has been already verified, you may still be able to
update your name and  address
by following the steps above, as long as it's within the same country and legal entity. You need the right permissions in your team to be able to edit these details.
Address change
Your trading address should be a permanent physical address. We can’t accept PO boxes or virtual or temporary addresses (hotels, Airbnbs) for a trading address.
If your business is changing countries, it's going to change its legal entity, so you would need to open a new business account with Wise.
Name change
Note that once name change is complete, all statements will display the new name. If you need statements to show the old name, you need to download them before submitting the request.
Your business name can be either the full legal business name or a trading name.
Your business name has a 50 character limit. If your business name is longer, you will need to use an abbreviation
Owner (Ultimate Beneficial Owners) or controller change
You need to
contact us
if you want to update the director or shareholder information. We will need a document confirming these changes (such as a registry document).
If you need to update any other details, please
get in touch
with us.

title: How do I separate my business and personal account from the same login details?
content:
To separate your business account login details from your personal account, you need to transfer the ownership of your business account to a different email address.
You can only do this on the web. Follow these steps:
Change the email address on your account
from your business email to your personal email, if it isn’t already.
Go to your business account.
Click on your business name in the top-right corner.
Select
Team members and payment approvals.
Choose to
Add team member.
Invite your business email
as a team member.
Accept the invite sent to your business email, and follow the steps to join the account as a new team member.
Log back into your business account with your personal email.
Go to
Team
, and select the team member with your personal email (the account owner) to go to your team member page
Choose
Transfer ownership,
then select the new team member with your business email to be the new owner.
Select
Continue
. The team member with your personal email will be removed from the business account. Your cards will be permanently deactivated.
You can now log into your business account using your business email address and the password you used when you accepted the invite. You should be able to log into your personal account with your personal email as usual.
Follow the same process if you have more than one business account on the same login. Just invite a different email address as a team member for your business account that you'd like to separate, and transfer the ownership to this team member.

title: How do I move my Wise accounts to the same login details?
content:
If you have multiple Wise accounts with different logins, you can consolidate them under a single login by transferring ownership of your business account(s).
You move a business account to your personal login but not the other way around. This process is only for business accounts.
You can only do this on the web. Follow these steps:
Log in to the business account you want to change login details for.
Go to
Team
in the left-hand navigation menu.
Select
Add team member
.
Invite the email address you want to use for your new login. The permissions you select don't matter.
Accept the invite sent to the new email address and follow the steps to join the account as a team member.
Log back into the business account you're moving.
Go to
Team
and select the team member with the old email address (marked as 'Account owner').
Choose
Transfer ownership
.
Select the team member with the email address you want to use as your new login.
Select
Confirm and transfer ownership
. The old email address will be removed from the business account, and any cards associated with it will be permanently deactivated.
You can now access the business account with your new login details and
easily switch between them
.
Repeat these steps for any other business accounts you want to consolidate.

title: Opening a personal account alongside your Wise Business account
content:
If you only had a business account open with Wise, but would also like to open a personal one — you can do so from either our website or your app.
Web
Go on your business name in the top corner
In the dropdown, choose
Get a personal account
App
Go on the icon or business name in the top corner
Select
Open a personal account
under Other accounts, and follow the steps
New personal account
You can also register a personal account under a
different email address, creating an account from scratch.
Log out from your business account
Go on our website
Select
Register as a Personal account

title: Can I switch between business accounts?
content:
If you’ve got a business account set up — or you have access to several business accounts — it’s easy to switch between them. Please always make sure to check which account you’re using before creating a transfer.
Website
On the website, click on your name / your business name in the top right corner
Select the account you'd like to switch to — business or personal
App
You can switch accounts on the mobile app via the
Your account
tab.
Click on the circle with your profile photo/initials in the top left corner.
Select the account you'd like to switch to — other business or personal
What if I can’t see the account I’m looking for?
If you can’t see the account you’re looking for when you go to switch, it might be because:
You signed up with a different email address for that account. Try logging out, and then logging in again with the right email address.
Your access may have been revoked.
Contact the account owner or a team member with permission to add or remove team members to get access again.

title: Can I transfer my business account to someone else?
content:
For most business accounts you can leave your Wise Business account, and transfer it to another team member at any time.
If your business is registered in
Japan
,
Hong Kong
, or
Singapore
, please check the exceptions below for the procedure.
How to change ownership
You can only change ownership
on the web
:
Click on your business name in the top-right corner
Select
Team members and payment approvals
Click your name to go to your team member page
Select
Transfer ownership
— if you don’t have any other team members, you’ll need to
Add a team member
first
From your list of team members, choose the new owner
Select
Continue
and leave the account
Learn more about managing your team’s access
What happens once I transfer ownership?
Once you transfer ownership of an account, your access is fully cancelled. This means, you can’t log in anymore, and your business Wise cards will be deleted.
You can be invited again at any time.
If the current account owner isn't available to transfer ownership
If the current owner is not available to transfer ownership of the business account, a business director needs to
contact us
and request the change.
We’ll need the following information from a director:
an official document showing that you’re a director e.g. a registration document
ID for the director and a
selfie with ID
an email address for the new owner
The new owner will need to have a personal Wise account, with a verified ID and
2-step verification
enabled.
If the future owner does not have a Wise account, they'll need to open one and complete verification before we can transfer the business to them.
Regional exceptions
Singapore
If the new account owner is the director of business you can transfer ownership yourself by following the usual steps above.
If the new account owner is not the director of business:
Fill out the Singapore
authorisation letter
.
Contact us
to request a change in ownership. Make sure to upload the authorisation letter.
Japan
Only the representative director of the company (or the sole trader) can be the account owner. To transfer your business account ownership to the new representative director:
Scan/copy your
certificate of registered matters issued
within the last 6 months.
Contact us
to request a change in ownership. Make sure to upload your certificate of registered matters with your request.
Hong Kong
1. Get the required documents ready.
For all business types if the new account owner is not the beneficial owner or the director of the business fill out the Hong Kong
authorisation letter
.
For Partnerships or Sole traders
: Business Registration (BR) certificate or Partnership registration document
For Limited companies
: No additional documents required
2.
Contact us
to request a change in ownership. Make sure to upload any required documents.

title: How to verify your Indian business to receive payments
content:
We currently onboard the following business types in India:
Sole trader / freelancer
Sole proprietorship
Private limited company
Limited liability partnership
General Partnership
If you have a different business type, we're working hard on bringing this back —
start registering your business
to join the waitlist.
We need to verify your identity and your business before you can start receiving money from your payers. It’s how we keep your money safe and comply with regulations.
Once you're verified,
you can receive money
from your international clients. They can pay to your account details on Wise and we'll automatically convert the money to INR at mid-market rate for you.
What's my company type?
You can choose from 5 company types based on the following criteria:
Sole trader or Freelancer
You're making money as an individual
Your
PAN is under your personal name
Your bank account for receiving INR is under your
personal name
Sole proprietorship
You're the sole owner of your business
You must have a government-issued document (
GSTIN
and/or
IEC)
showing your trade or business name, proving your ownership
Your
PAN is under your personal name
Your bank account for receiving INR is under your
trade
or
business name
Private limited company
You have a privately held, legally-recognised business entity that is owned by private stakeholders
You have a government certified registration document to prove your stake in company
You have a
business PAN
that is under the company’s name
Your bank account for receiving INR is under your
company’s name
Your business is registered with a
Corporate identification number (CIN)
Limited Liability Partnership
You're running a partnership-based firm where partners share profits according to an LLP agreement while having limited liability protection
You have a government certified registration document to prove your stake in the partnership
You have a
business PAN
that is under the
company’s name
Your bank account for receiving INR is under your
company’s name
Your business is registered with a
Limited Liability Partnership Identification Number (LLPIN)
General Partnership
You're running a partnership-based firm where partners share profits and losses according to a partnership agreement
You're running a partnership-based firm where partners share profits and losses according to a partnership agreement
You have a business PAN that is under the partnership based firm name
Your bank account for receiving INR is under the partnership firm name
How do I get verified?
We'll need to verify your:
Aadhaar number and PAN via video verification
Bank account details
Business ownership
We will verify your Aadhaar number and PAN via video verification as required by the Reserve Bank of India for international remittances. You can complete this process from home via a short video call — no need to visit an office or wait in line.
We'll then reach out within 2 working days to let you know that your business account is ready. If there’s any further information required, we will notify you in an email.
1. Video KYC verification
General information about the process:
The video call can be done in English or Hindi and it takes about 2-3 minutes
You need a smartphone or a computer with a webcam, microphone, and stable internet
You must be physically in India and not using VPN during the video call
You must be the account holder of the Wise account to make the video call
No one else can assist you during the video call
Have your Aadhaar number (with access to its linked mobile/email for OTPs) ready
Have your Digilocker access ready (with your 6-digit security PIN)
Have your personal physical PAN card ready (we do not accept e-PAN or screenshots of digital PAN card)
Make sure you're in a well-lit, quiet space
Video verification is available Monday to Friday, from 9:15 AM to 11:00 PM IST, excluding public holidays
Learn more about video verification
here
How to start the video verification process:
Complete account registration on Wise
Proceed to verify your identity through IDfy, our KYC verification partner
Capture your PAN details by taking a photo of your PAN card
Capture your Aadhar details by selecting
Verify with Digilocker
and logging in with your 6-digit Digilocker PIN 
(If you forgot your PIN,
reset it here
)
Once you verified your Aadhar details, select
Start Video KYC
How to complete the video verification process:
Allow camera and microphone access — this is secure and only used for the call
You’ll meet a Wise agent who’ll guide you through:
Reading a 4-digit code displayed on your screen
Showing your physical PAN card to the camera
Taking a live selfie
The agent will verify your Aadhaar and PAN details
2. Bank account details
Details of the bank account where you want the INR to be deposited. We ask for your account number and IFSC code to verify your bank account.
Freelancers
– the account holder name on your bank account must match with your name registered on your personal Wise account.
Sole proprietorships
– the account holder name on your bank account must match with your registered business name on your Wise business account.
Private limited, General Partnerships & Limited Liability Partnerships
– the account holder name on your bank account must match with your business profile name.
3. Business ownership
Sole proprietorships
— you'll need to provide a
Business Registration Number (GSTIN or IEC) and a different supporting document. If you use GST or IEC for your registration number — provide a different proof document from the list below
:
Udyam Registration Certificate
Shop and Establishment Document
Income Tax Return (ITR) & Acknowledgement letter
GST Certificate — only if not used as your registration number
IEC Certificate — only if not used as your registration number
The document must match the registered business name on your Wise account.
Private limited companies —
you'll need to provide the following information:
Corporate identification number (CIN)
Business entity PAN number and a copy of the card
Memorandum of Association and Articles of Association (MoA and AoA)
Importer Exporter Code (IEC)
GST identification number, if you’d like to receive tax invoices
Directors’ details
Beneficial owners’ details and IDs
List of shareholders and their respective ownership percentages
Authorisation letter
if the person registering is not one of the directors mentioned earlier
Limited Liability Partnerships
— you'll need to provide the following information:
Limited liability partnership identification number (LLPIN)
Business entity PAN number and a copy of the card
Importer Exporter Code (IEC)
GST identification number, if you’d like to receive tax invoices
Partnership deed
Partners’ details
Beneficial owners’ details and IDs
List of shareholders and their respective ownership percentages
Authorization letter
if the person registering is not one of the directors mentioned earlier
General
Partnerships
— you'll need to provide the following information:
Business entity PAN number and a copy of the card
Importer Exporter Code (IEC)
GST identification number, if you’d like to receive tax invoices
Partnership deed
Partners’ details
Beneficial owners’ details and IDs
List of shareholders and their respective ownership percentages
Authorization letter
if the person registering isn't one of the directors mentioned earlier
What to do if my bank account verification has a mismatch error?
Please make sure that you check the company type you registered with and if the documents and information you sent us match the requirements.
If you’ve registered as a freelancer, your provided bank account name must match with your personal profile name.
Otherwise, your provided bank account name must match with your business profile name i.e. bank account name must be under your business name
How can I check my profile details?
You can check your personal or business profile details by going to
https://wise.com/profile/view/
where you will be able to modify editable information.

Some fields can only be modified if verification is still in progress.

title: How do I close my Wise Business account?
content:
You can close your Wise Business account at any time. You must be the
Owner
of the account to close it. If you need to change the account owner, learn how to
change team member roles
.
When you close your business account, you can choose to either close
only
the business account or close
both
your business and personal accounts.
Before you close your account
To make sure you can close your account smoothly, you'll need to:
Withdraw all money from your account
Go to
Home
and select a currency that has money in it
Select
Send
Make sure you send the full amount and choose a bank account outside of Wise
Repeat for all currencies you have money in
If you have a very small amount left (like 0.50 USD or equivalent) that you can't send out, please
contact us
, and we can help clear it for you.
Tip:
If you're keeping your personal account, you can
send the money from your business account to your personal account
.
Download any statements or transfer receipts you need for your records:
Once your account is closed, you'll
permanently lose access
to it. You won't be able to see your transfer history or
download statements
for your accountant. Please download everything you need
before
you close the account.
Complete or cancel all pending transactions:
This includes recent card payments, which can be 'pending' for up to 7 days after you made the purchase. If you get an error when trying to close your account, this is the most common reason.
Disconnect any connected accounting software,
like
Xero
or
QuickBooks
.
Wait for any pending fees
(like from your last card use) to be taken.
How to close your account on the web or app
Click on your business initials/photo in the top corner
Go to
Settings
Under
Actions and Agreements
, select
Close your account
Follow the on-screen instructions
What happens to my data after I close my account?
Wise is required by data protection regulations to keep your data for a certain period. After this regulatory retention period has passed, your data will be automatically deleted.
Learn more about data retention
What if my business is in liquidation or insolvency?
If your business is entering liquidation or insolvency, share our email
legalrequests@wise.com
with your liquidator or the insolvency administrator.
Your appointed liquidator or insolvency administrator will need to give us instructions on where to send any remaining funds. Our team will then act on their instructions to close the account.

title: Opening a Wise account for your Brazilian business
content:
If you have a business registered in Brazil, and want to create a Wise Business account, here's some things you'll need to keep in mind.
Getting a Wise Business account
To get a Wise Business account, you need to:
be an active business
provide a valid CNPJ
and be one of the following legal types:
Sole Trader
– Empresário Individual, including MEI (Micro Empresa Individual). Legal nature code
213-5
Single Person or Multi-owner Limited Liability Company
– Sociedade Limitada Unipessoal/Sociedade Empresaria Limitada. Legal nature code
206-2
Single Person Lawyer Company
– Sociedade Unipessoal de Advocacia. Legal nature code
232-1
All business accounts must be in line with our
Acceptable Use Policy
.
Currently, we can only onboard accounts where the owner of the company is the same as the personal account holder. There can only be 1 owner that owns 100% of the company. This means that the owner of the business must create the business account with us.
Read more about getting verified for your Brazilian business
If you have managers at your business, you can
add them as an Admin
on the account.
What information will I need to provide?
We'll need to verify the owner's CPF and we'll ask for an ID. We'll also need to collect info about your business, such as name, address, CNPJ, activities, income, etc.
For Sole Trader (Empresário Individual), we also need a
live ID + Selfie
Additional information/documents about your business might also be requested if needed.
Read more about how Wise verifies your identity
What are business sending limits?
There are limits per transfer and also a business yearly limit:
Limits per transfer
Sending from BRL
– 5,000 USD (or equivalent in another currency) both for sending to business or to personal accounts.
Sending to BRL
– 3,000 USD (or equivalent in another currency) for sending to personal accounts and 10,000 USD (or equivalent in another currency) for sending to business accounts.
Any fees you pay are included in the limit. This means that you may not be able to send it if the total amount with fees exceeds the limit.
Financial capacity (yearly limit)
During registration, you might need to provide income information or additional documents about your business's financial capacity.
Businesses are able to increase this financial capacity by providing additional source of funds documents, upon Wise's request.
Wise reserves the right to request further information at any time if needed, to comply with our regulations.
Acceptable Brazilian source of funds documents
Here are the
source of funds
documents we accept.
Acceptable documents during onboarding
IRPJ (
Imposto sobre a Renda das Pessoas Jurídicas
) + Receipt
DASN-SIMEI + Receipt (for those MEIs exempt from Tax Declaration)
Sales/Services Contract
Acceptable documents after reaching financial capacity
Sales/Services Contract
Owner’s Tax Declaration (for Sole Traders)
Loans Contract
Personal Tax Declaration (to assess ownership of the company and financial capacity) + Declaration of Investment in the Firm
Is there anything else I need to know?
With a Brazilian business account, you can:
Send money
Have a
Wise Account
where you can add money to the account, hold money in 40+ currencies, convert between the currencies and send from these accounts.
Get account details
to receive money
in different currencies
for a
setup fee
. The fee can only be paid via Pix.
the 250
BRL
feature charge
can be received via PIX (QR code),
no matter who the payer is.
It can be personal account, another person (not connected directly to the business), another business etc.
Can I receive money without sharing my account details?
Add or remove team members
to the business and
manage their permissions
.
You currently won't be able to:
Receive BRL
Send
Batch payments

title: How do I verify my business in Hong Kong?
content:
To open a Wise Business account in Hong Kong, we need to verify your identity and your business details.
We also need to
verify your personal information
, so we’ll ask you to send us a photo of your ID document and in some cases a selfie.
Currently, we’re unable to support trusts, charities, and non-profit organisations in Hong Kong.
What do I need to provide to verify my business?
You'll need to submit the following information and documents based on your business type:
Sole trader
Business name and address. If you’re operating at a different address, you need to submit it as well
Business category and details of business activities
Business owner’s valid photo ID and information (name, date of birth, country of residence, nationality)
ID with Liveness
Business registration certificate (BR)
Partnership
Business name and address. If you're operating at a different address, you need to submit that as well
Business category and details of business activities
Partners’ valid photo ID and information (name, date of birth, country of residence, nationality)
ID with Liveness
Business registration certificate (BR), partnership registration document, or partnership deed
Limited company
Business name and address. If you're operating at a different address, you need to submit that as well. You need to make sure the address is up to date.
Business category and details of business activities
Directors’ information (name, date of birth, country of residence)
Information and a valid photo ID of any shareholders who own 25% or more of the company. Shareholder information includes: name, date of birth, country of residence, nationality
ID with Liveness
What if I’m not the owner or director of the business?
If you’re not the owner of the business, you’re required to submit an authorisation letter. This is to confirm that you’re authorised to use and communicate with Wise on behalf of the business.
You can
download a template of the authorisation letter
and have it ready when we ask for it.

title: How do I add or remove team members?
content:
It’s easy to add or remove people from your business account. As long as you are the account owner or have permission to add or remove team members and change their permissions, you’ll be able to do this by following these steps.
Add someone to your account
Web
Click on your business name in the top-right corner
Select
Team members and payment approvals
Select
Add team member
Enter the email address of the person you’d like to add
Select the permissions you’d like them to have
If you gave them permission to order their business debit card, you’ll get another step to set their
spending limits and other card permissions
If you gave them permission to set up or make payments via transfer, you’ll get another step to
set up payment approvals
Review their permissions, card permissions and payment approvals. If everything looks good, select
Confirm
We’ll email your new team member an invite to join the account. This invite will be valid for 2 weeks – after that, you’ll need to re-invite them.
App
Tap your initials or profile picture in the top-left corner
Tap on
Team members and payment approvals
Select
Add
Enter the email address of the person you’d like to add
Select the permissions you’d like them to have
If you gave them permission to order their business debit card, you’ll get another step to set their
spending limits and other card permissions
If you gave them permission to set up or make payments via transfer, you’ll get another step to
set up payment approvals
Review their permissions, card permissions and payment approvals. If everything looks good, select
Confirm
We’ll email your new team member an invite to join the account. This invite will be valid for 2 weeks – after that, you’ll need to re-invite them.
Remove someone from your account
On either web or app:
Go on your business name at the top
Select
Team Members and payment approvals
Select the name of the team member you want to remove
Select
Remove
Confirm
the removal
Remove yourself from a business account
Click on your business name in the top-right corner
Select
Leave this business account
If you're the owner of the Wise business account you'll have to
transfer your business account to someone else
.
Keep in mind that if you need to update the business details, like users, you need to inform us to avoid problems regarding ID verification.
How to accept the invitation someone has sent you
When someone adds you as a team member to a business account, you’ll be sent an email with an invite to join the account.
If you don’t have a Wise account with the email address the invite was sent to
Go to the invitation email you've received from Wise.
Select
Accept the invite
.
Sign up using the email address the invite was sent to — this will already be filled in for you.
Verify your phone number.
Create a new password.
Fill in your personal details.
That’s it! You’ve joined the business account.
If you’re already logged into another Wise account, you’ll be asked if you want to log out of this account before you accept your invite.
If have a registered Wise account with the email address the invite was sent to
Go to the invitation email you've received from Wise.
Select
Accept the invite
.
After accepting the invitation, you'll be redirected to
a login page.
Log in with the email address the invite was sent to, it's already be filled in for you, and use your password.
That’s it! You’ve joined the business account.
If you’re already logged into Wise with this email address, accepting the invite will add you to the business account without needing to log in again.

title: Who can contact Wise about my business account?
content:
For privacy and security reasons, Wise can only give specific details about your account to people you authorise to have access.
You can manage who has access to your business account in
Team
.
If you don't speak any of our supported languages when contacting us, and have a team member who does, you can invite them as an Admin to your account to help verify business details on your behalf.
Learn how to add people to your account

title: What are groups and how do I use them?
content:
For business accounts, groups allow you to separate and organise your money, and control which team members have access to view, send and spend that money.
You can create as many groups as you need, such as for:
a company trip –  you can give a team member a specific amount of money to spend with, and restrict them from spending from your main account
a specific department in your business – you can create a group where only team members in that department can access the money
separating money that you don’t want everyone to see
Groups can only be created by team members with permission to create and manage groups and jars.
Learn more about creating, managing and closing groups
How is a group different from the main account?
Your main account is what you see on the Home tab. From there you’ll see all of the currencies that live in your main account.
If you create a group, any currencies in that group will be entirely separate from your main account.
You can also control which team members have access to groups you create.
What can I do with groups?
In each group, it’s possible to:
Add and hold money in different currencies
get a card to spend directly from the group*
send money
add team members who can view, send or spend money in a group
Some features that are available in the main account aren't available in groups you create.
Group
Main account
Jar
Send money
✔️
✔️
✔️
Receive money with account details
❌
✔️
❌
Hold money as Assets
❌
✔️
✔️
Spend with the Wise digital card
**
✔️
✔️
❌
Spend with the Wise physical card
**
✔️
✔️
❌
*Group cards are not available for businesses in some countries.
**You can also restrict team members to only spend from groups they're added to, and not from your main account.
See where Wise business cards are available
What can team members do in groups?
Your team members can do the same things in a group they’re added to, as they can in your main account. And what they can do depends on their permissions.
For example, if they have permission to set up or make payments via transfer, they'll be able to set up payments from a group they’re added to, just as they can from your main account.
And if a team member’s payments require approval in your main account, they’ll also require approval in a group.
If a team member has permission to order a card and spend from your main account, they’ll be able to do the same in a group. But you can also restrict them to only spend from groups they’re added to.
Learn more about managing team members in groups
Availability
Groups is currently available for businesses in Australia, Brazil, Canada, The European Economic Area (EEA), Japan, New Zealand, Singapore, and the UK.

title: Managing groups
content:
On a business account, you can use groups to separate and organise your money, and control which team members can view, send and spend that money.
Learn more about groups
You need permission to be able to create and manage groups.
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
How can I create a new group?
To open a group:
Select
Main Group
.
From the
Groups
menu, select
Create group
.
Name the group and choose the default currency.
Choose to add money, add team members, and get a digital card.
Anyone with permission to create and manage groups will have access to this group by default.
The group’s total balance is shown in the default currency, but you can change this.
How can I edit or close an existing group?
Select the group you want to edit and click
...
.
Select
Edit Group
to change the group name or default currency, or select
Close group
to close the group. Closing a group moves all funds to the Main Group, removes all team members and deletes all the Wise cards associated with that group.
How can I add more currencies to an existing group?
You can do so by adding new currencies to your existing group:
Choose the group you’d like to add a new currency to.
Select
Open 50+ currencies
.
Choose the currency you’d like to add.
How can I move money between groups?
Team members with permission to convert money between currencies can move money between different groups – including currencies in the Main Group.
To move money:
Select the currency within the group that you want to move money from.
Select
Convert
or
Move
.
Enter the amount and currency.
Keep in mind:
It's free to do if it’s in the same currency — for example, it’s free to move money from your GBP Main Group currency, to a GBP group currency.
If you move money from a currency to a group of a different currency, the usual conversion fee will apply.

title: Managing team members in groups
content:
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Once you've created a group, you can add and manage team members. Anyone with permission to create and manage groups will have access to this group by default, so you don't need to add them.
Learn more about what groups are
Learn more about managing your team's accesses
How do I add team members to groups?
To add a team member:
Go to the group you want to add them to. You can find all your groups by selecting the down arrow next to the
main account
on the home page
After selecting the group from the dropdown, select the
Members
tab
Select
Add team members
Select the team members you want to add to the group, then select
Confirm
How can I restrict team members to only spend from groups instead of the main account?
Restricting team members to only spend from groups means they can only spend from groups they’re added to, and not from the main account.
Go to the
Team
page
Select the team member you’d like to only spend from groups
Go to the
Cards
section and select the
Card permissions
drop-down
Turn on
Only spend from groups
Any of their existing digital or physical cards linked to the main account will be permanently deactivated.
What happens if a team member’s permissions are changed?
If a team member’s permission to order their business debit card is removed, we’ll permanently deactivate their cards.
What happens if a team member leaves the company or their account is suspended?
When a team member leaves the company, any team member with permission to add or remove team members can remove them from the business account. This will remove them from all groups they were added to. We’ll permanently deactivate all of their cards — this can't be undone.
If a team member’s Wise profile is suspended by Wise for any reason, their cards will be permanently deactivated and they won’t be able to spend from your business account. If this happens, please
get in touch
.
How many cards can team members order for each group?
Team members with permission to order their business debit card can only order one physical card across the whole business. This means if they order a physical card for one group, they won’t be able to order another physical card for the main account or any other groups.
If they have permission to order digital cards, they can also:
order up to 3 digital cards per day
hold up to 3 active digital cards at any point in time
How do spending limits apply in a group?
You can’t set spending limits for each group, but team members can set spending limits for each of their cards.*
Each team member also has a cardholder spending limit, which is the amount they can spend and withdraw across all their cards, in all groups.
All spending also contributes towards the account spending limits, which is the amount that everyone in the account can spend and withdraw overall.
Find out more about spending limits on a business account
How can I see all the cards linked to a group?
Team members in the group with permission to view team members can see all cards linked to the group from the group’s
Cards
tab.
Team members without this permission can only see their own cards within a group they’re in – they can’t see other team members’ cards.

title: Managing your team’s access with permissions
content:
Multi-user Access (MUA) lets you invite other users to have access to your Wise Business account. These invited users are called
team members
.
Team members can only access the business account(s) you invite them to. They can’t access your personal account.
You can control your team members’ access with permissions. Each team member has a custom set of permissions that control what they can see and do on your Wise Business account.
How do I give my team member permissions?
You’ll be asked to choose a team member’s permissions when you invite them to your account.
Team members may be subject to verification checks once they're added to the account.
Learn how to add people to your Wise business account
On the permission selection screen, permissions are grouped by the jobs your team members might need to do on the account: View account, Move money, Pay with card, Manage team, and Manage account. You can expand these groups to add or remove specific permissions, so you can choose exactly what you want your team member to do.
Some features on the business account need multiple permissions to work, one permission might be dependent on another one. When you select a team member’s permissions, those other dependent permissions will be selected automatically, and will be shown to you in grey. So you’ll know if any dependent permissions have been selected, and you can make sure you’re happy with them.
You can also use our Viewer or Admin presets to quickly select a set of permissions. The Viewer preset fills in permissions that will give the team member view-only access, and the Admin preset fills in all available permissions. You can then add or remove any permissions you need.
How do I change a team member’s permissions?
Here's how you can do this from either your app or the web.
Web
Select your business name in the top-right corner
Select
Team members and payment approvals
Select the the team member you want change the permissions for
Select
Edit
in the
Permissions
section
Choose which permissions you’d like to add or remove
Review their new permissions — if everything looks good, select
Confirm
App
Select your profile at the top
Select
Team members and payment approvals
Select the team member you want change the permissions for
Select
Edit
in the
Permissions
section
Choose which permissions you’d like to add or remove
Review their new permissions — if everything looks good, select
Confirm
You can change a team member’s permissions any time. You can’t change your own permissions, or the permissions of the account owner.
How do card permissions work?
You can give team members permission to order their business debit card so they can spend directly from the business account. This is only available in
supported countries
.
Find out more about how you can control your team’s spending
How can I manage my team member’s payments?
You can set up payment approvals for any team members with permission to set up or make payments via transfer. Team members whose payments need approval can still set up payments, but the payment needs to be approved by another team member before it's funded or completed.
Find out more about setting up payment approvals
Account owner permissions
The account owner has all permissions. There are also some actions on the account that only account owners can do, including:
Closing
the account
Appealing
account closure
Transferring ownership
of the account
Account owners can transfer ownership of their Wise Business account to another team member at any time.
Adding a member who speaks a supported language
If you need to talk to us, but you don't speak any of our supported languages, you can add invite a member of your team,
who speaks a supported language, to your account.
By granting them permissions such as Admin, they can contact our support team on your behalf, verify necessary details, and complete any required account changes.

title: How do I check the details of an approved/rejected transfer?
content:
Transfers that have been set up and are awaiting approval will appear  as a
Task
on the top of the home page for team members who can approve payments. After a transfer is approved or rejected, it will appear with your other transfers in the Activity feed.
How do I know who approved/ rejected a transfer?
You can check who approved or rejected a transfer by selecting the transfer and viewing its
transfer details
on the website.
If you set up the transfer, you’ll also be notified via email when it’s been approved or rejected. Please ensure you have
Transfer and balances
email notifications turned on to receive these emails.
Manage your notifications
(under Notifications > Transfers and balances)
Why was the transfer rejected?
If the team member that rejected a payment left a
Rejection reason,
you can find it under the
Transaction details
section. If there's no rejection reason, try checking with the team member that rejected the payment for more information.

title: How do I approve/reject a payment requiring approval?
content:
Payments can be approved by team members with permission to approve payments created by other team members.
If there's a payment set up that needs approval
On the website or mobile app, payments that need approval will appear under
Tasks
on the home page.
To approve a payment:
Select
Review
on the approval task
If you’ve got more than one payment waiting for approval, select the payment you want to approve
On the Transaction details screen, select
Approve
A confirmation screen will pop up. You can select
Review details
to see the transaction details before approving
Select
Approve payment
If approval is needed from a second team member, a
Task
will appear for any team members with permission to approve payments created by other team members
To reject a payment:
Select
Review
on the approval task
If you’ve got more than one payment waiting for approval, select the payment you want to reject
On the Transaction details screen, select
Reject
A confirmation screen will pop up, with a
Rejection reason
box where you can leave a comment. This reason will be sent to the team member who set the payment up

title: How do Payment Approvals work?
content:
Payment approvals help businesses control their payments on Wise. Payment approvals are only available in regions where you can add currencies and hold money.
Learn more about where you can hold money with Wise
How does it work?
Team members whose payments need approval will be able to set up payments, but the payment won’t be funded or completed until it’s approved by another team member.
You can set up a payment approval for any team member with permission to set up or make payments via transfer.
Learn more about setting up payment approvals
Setting permissions for your team
For each team member, you can choose whether all their payments should require approval, or just those above a certain amount.
You can also choose for payments to require approval by 1 or 2 people. The team member approving a payment can be anyone with permission to approve payments created by other team members.
Team members can’t approve their own payments.
How to approve or reject payments
Once a team member sets up a payment that needs approval, a different team member that has permission to approve payments (approver), will be automatically notified to review it.
They can manually view the payment under
Tasks
from Home.
Here's an example:
A team member sets up a payment that requires approval.
The approver will be automatically notified.
They'll review the payment and approve or reject it.
If approved, the payment can be funded and completed.
After a payment is approved or rejected, it'll appear with your other payments in the Activity feed.
What happens if I don't review the payment?
If you don't approve or reject the payment within 7-10 days the payment will automatically be cancelled. The exact timeframe for auto-cancellation depends on the source currency.
I'm not receiving the automatic notifications to approve payments
Make sure that you have Transfers and currencies notifications turned on to receive approval-related notifications.
Manage your notifications here
(under
Notifications
>
Transfers and currencies
)

title: How do I set up a payment approval?
content:
Payment approvals help businesses control their payments on Wise. Payment approvals are only available in regions where you can open accounts and hold money.
Learn more about how payment approvals work
You can set up a payment approval for any team member with permission to set up or make payments via transfer. You must have permission to set up payment approvals for other team members.
You can set up a payment approval for a team members in three ways:
When you add them to the account
You need permission to add or remove team members and change their permissions.
When adding a team member to the account, if you give them permission to set up or make payments via transfer, you’ll get another step where you can set up payment approvals for this team member.
Here, you can choose whether 1 or 2 people need to approve their payments. You can also choose whether all their payments should require approval, or just those over a certain amount.
From the Team page
Go to the
Team
page
Select
Payment approvals.
You’ll see any team members with permission to set up or make payments via transfer
Find the team member you want to set up a payment approval for and select
Set up approval
Choose whether 1 or 2 people should approve their transfers, and if they only need to be approved over a certain amount
Select
Confirm
From Team Member pages
Go to the
Team
page
Select the team member you want to set up a payment approval for to go to their Team Member page
Select
Edit
in the
Payment Approvals
section
Choose whether 1 or 2 people should approve their transfers, and if they only need to be approved over a certain amount
Select
Confirm
How do I cancel a payment approval?
Go to the
Team
page
Select
Payment approvals
Find the team member you want to remove the payment approval from and select
Edit
Select
No approval
Select
Confirm no approval
You can also remove a payment approval for a team member on their team member page by selecting
Edit
in the
Payment approvals
section.

title: How does my connection with Xero work?
content:
To get started,
connect your Xero account to your Wise account
. Once connected, you can manage the following features from your
Connection settings
:
Import bills to Wise
Mark bills as paid in Xero
Bank feeds
Transactions with Attachments
Card Transactions with Receipts
Importing Bills to Wise
This is only available for business in the UK at the moment. You can see your unpaid bills in real time and pay them from
Bills
.
We won't import any bills you mark as paid in Xero. It means if you paid a bill through another provider and haven’t marked it as paid in Xero, it's still going to be visible in your Bills page on Wise.
To see how this feature works in detail, check
How do I import and pay bills from Xero with Wise?
Marking Bills as Paid in Xero
When you pay a bill through your Bills page on Wise, we’ll automatically mark it as paid for you in Xero. This requires a Xero plan that supports multi-currency.
How to turn on multicurrency in Xero
.
Bank Feeds
Bank feeds let you sync your Wise activity directly with Xero. To see your Wise transactions (bank statement lines) in Xero, you need to connect each Wise currency you want to sync.
If you didn't connect the currencies during the initial setup, you can add them at any time by following these steps:
Open your
connection settings
and go to
Connected accounts
Find the Wise currency (for example: USD, GBP, EUR) you’d like to connect and click
Select
Choose an existing Xero bank account to connect with, or create a new one.
If you create a new account, Xero will automatically add it for that Wise currency within your Xero organisation.
Learn more about adding a bank account or credit card account to Xero
You can only connect one Wise currency with one Xero account within a Xero organisation. If you try to sync two Wise currency with Xero accounts with identical names (for example both accounts named "Wise Bank" or “USD”), you'll encounter an error as Xero requires each account to have a unique name within the organisation.
If you previously used a name for an account in a Xero organisation and then archived or deleted it, you can't reuse that name.
We automatically sync accounts every 4 hours, but you can manually sync anytime — just go to
Sync options and status
from your Xero
Connection settings
and click on
Sync now
.
Transactions with Attachments
This feature automatically sends invoices and bills attached to your Wise transactions to Xero. These will be stored as Draft Bills or Draft Invoices in Xero, awaiting your review and publication.
Once you turn this feature on, any transaction attachments from connected currencies and jars will be synced to Xero where it will create draft bills and invoices. You just need to review and publish them.
Card Transactions
When you attach a receipt to a Wise card payment, we send it to Xero as an expense with the receipt attached  - so it’s ready to review and categorise.
Card payments are sent to Xero and categorised as ‘Wise expenses’, with all receipts included as files.
To use this,
Card transactions
must be turned on.
Advanced Settings
Advanced settings aren't available for new customers. If you don’t see this feature under the
connection settings
, it means we can’t provide it.
These settings provide additional customisation options, allowing you to:
Sync Bill and Invoice Payments:
Automatically marks bills and invoices as paid in Xero when paid through Wise. We'll match bills and invoices by the recipient name, amount, and reference.
Record Other Transactions as Expenses:
Record Wise payments without a bill or invoice as an Expense in Xero.

title: Managing your Xero connection with Wise
content:
By integrating Xero with Wise, you can seamlessly import bills and automatically mark them as paid. You can sync bank feeds and transfers or card payment attachments to Xero.
More about the features here:
How does my connection with Xero work?
How do I connect my Wise account with Xero?
You don’t need to leave your Wise account to create a new Xero bank account, but you also can start the integration from Xero if you prefer.
Log in to your Wise Business account
Select your business name in the top corner
Under the Settings, go to
Connect and Manage apps
Choose
Xero
and follow instructions
Choose the currency for accounts and jars you want to sync with Xero.
Make sure that your Xero account is correctly connected with the relevant Wise currencies.
Confirm your setup, and your Wise transactions will automatically sync with Xero moving forward.
Note that you can only connect one Wise account with one Xero account within a Xero organisation. Each Xero bank account within needs to have a unique name.
You’ll see some of your Xero currency accounts already appear once your accounts are connected. These are the accounts we think you’d want to sync your Wise accounts with.
If any of those don’t look right, go to
Edit,
and choose another account or remove it. You also can create new currency accounts in Xero without leaving Wise.
If you want to sync previous transactions with Xero, check out:
How do I sync my Wise transactions to Xero?
How do I disconnect my Wise account from Xero?
To disconnect your Wise account from Xero:
Log in to your Wise Business account on the web.
Go to
Connect and Manage apps
under
Settings
from your profile (top corner).
Click
Connected
.
Click
Manage
next to
Xero
.
Click
Disconnect
from the bottom of the page.
You can also directly go to
connection settings
to disconnect.

title: How do I sync my Wise transactions to Xero?
content:
First you need to
connect your Wise account with Xero
— it allows you to sync your transactions and manage your finances in one place. Once the accounts are connected, head to your
Connection settings
and make sure the
Bank feeds
feature is 'up and running'.
From the same page, choose which currencies you want to sync automatically. You can only sync a Wise account currency to a Xero bank account in the same currency.
Wise transactions from the selected curencies will appear in your corresponding Xero bank accounts, but only if the currency matches. Make sure that your Xero account is correctly mapped with the relevant currencies in your Wise account.
Once you’re connected, Wise will automatically sync all new transactions starting from the day of the connection, every 4 hours.
How do I import my previous Wise transactions to Xero
To start the sync sooner, go to
Sync options and status
from your
Connection settings
and select
Sync now
. On the same page you can choose an earlier start date, up to 1 year before you were connected.
If you want to sync transactions that date further back than one year, you can manually upload your older Wise statements.
Download a statement
in PDF or CSV format for every currency you have activated or hold in your Wise account, then follow these instructions to
import statements to Xero
and
reconcile your accounts
.
How do I sync more than one currency
If you want to sync more than one currency, you need to have
a multicurrency plan with Xero
.

title: How do I import and pay bills from Xero with Wise?
content:
Once you connect your Wise account to Xero and enable the bills feature, you can import your bills directly to Wise. You can also view your unpaid bills in real-time on your Wise account and pay them directly from your
Bills page
.
Learn how to connect your Wise account with Xero
How to import bills to Wise
Log into your Wise account
Select your business name in the top corner
Go to
Connect and Manage apps
under
Settings
Choose
Connected
, then choose
Manage
next to the Xero logo.
Scroll down to
Bills
and toggle it right to turn it on.
Please note that Wise won't import any bills you mark as paid in Xero. If you paid a bill through another provider and marked it as paid in Xero, it won't appear on your
Bills
page in Wise.
Marking bills as paid in Xero
On the
Connection settings
page you’ll also see an option to
mark bills as paid in Xero
. If you enable this, when you pay a bill through your Bills page on Wise, we’ll automatically mark it as paid in Xero. Fees will be recorded as Bank Charges.
Paying unpaid bills
Navigate to the
Bills tab
in your Wise account.
If your bills are already synced, you'll see a list of unpaid bills from Xero. These will be synced automatically.
From the Bills tab, you can pay the bill as you would normally send money through Wise.
Examples for bill payment
Let's say you used the
Bill pay
feature on Wise to pay your bill ABC123 in the amount of £500. It's been paid to ‘Supplier 1’ and you funded the bill from your Wise GBP currency.
Because you connected your Wise GBP currency to your Xero bank account named ‘Wise - GBP’, the bill payment transaction will be recorded in that account, and we'll mark your ‘ABC123’ bill as paid.
Why is there a new clearing account added?
We’ll automatically create these clearing accounts in your
Chart of Accounts
