for the following reasons:
Expenses (Wise):
If you choose an external payment method, we’re not always able to retrieve information about the bank account that is funding a payment, for example a non-Wise card payment or bank payment. If this occurs, the unidentified transaction will be recorded in ‘Expenses (Wise)’.
Cross currency clearing account (Wise):
To help you keep your books tidy, all transactions that include a currency exchange will be revalued to your base currency. Note that this only applies where neither of the currencies being exchanged are your base currency. We’ll record these transactions in ‘Cross currency clearing account (Wise)’ in two parts. First, a transfer from the source currency to your Xero base currency. Second, a transfer from your Xero base currency to your target currency.
Example scenario
Your Xero base currency is GBP, and you choose to fund a USD (target) payment with your EUR (source) Wise currency account.
First we’ll record a EUR to GBP transfer to ‘Wise-service’ with the following reference:
“
This transfer was reported using a clearing account to allow you to reconcile multiple currencies through your base currency.
”
We’ll then record a GBP to USD Transfer to ‘Payment: [Supplier name]’ with a reference.
How are fees recorded?
Wise fees — including a reference — are shown as separate expense transactions to ‘Wise-service’ in the bank account that we’ll create for you, called
‘Bank charges (Wise)’
. You can find them under the
Chart of Accounts.

title: Why isn't my Wise account syncing with Xero?
content:
If you're having trouble syncing your Wise account with Xero, don't worry—there are a few simple steps you can take to sort things out.
Here’s what to check in your Wise account's
Connection settings
:
Make sure your Xero account is still connected with Wise. If it got disconnected, reconnect by following the steps in
Managing your Xero connection with Wise.
Make sure the currency you want to sync is connected under
Connected accounts.
We only sync transactions from currencies that are connected. If your currency isn't connected, choose
Select
and link it to a Xero bank account.
You can only connect one Wise currency with one Xero account within a Xero organisation. If you try to sync two Wise currencies with Xero accounts with identical names (e.g. both named "Wise Bank" or “USD”), you'll encounter an error as Xero requires each account to have a unique name within the organisation.
If you previously used a name for an account in a Xero organisation and then archived or deleted it, you can't reuse that name. Choose a unique name for the new account to avoid mapping errors.
If you've removed a Xero bank account, you’ll need to select another account or create a new one. Navigate to
Connected accounts
and select
Edit
next to the currency to choose a Xero bank account or set up a new one.
If everything seems connected correctly, check
Bank feed activity
in your
Connection settings
and select
Sync now
. You can also choose an earlier start date for syncing up to one year back.
Reasons why sync may not be working
There are a few reasons why your sync to Xero may not be working:
You can only sync your Wise currency to a Xero bank account of the same currency. For example, you can sync your GBP Wise account to a GBP Xero bank account. Double check the currency of the Xero bank account you’re looking for.
You need to have a plan with Xero that allows multi-currency to sync your Wise currencies. Please
check with Xero
to learn more.
If your Xero bank account of the same currency has been removed in Xero, you need to choose another or create a new one.
If the transfer is funded by an external bank, it won’t be reported because we only report transfers sent or received through Wise account currencies.
You or someone else may have disconnected Wise from within your Xero account. Reconnect to start syncing your Wise activity again. Select
Reconnect
on your
Setting page
or
try again
now.
If these don’t seem to be the issue,
get in touch with us
.

title: How do I upload my Wise statements to Xero?
content:
When you
connect your Wise business account to your Xero account
, you no longer need to manually upload your Wise statements to Xero. Just make sure that
Bank feeds sync
is turned on in your
Connection settings
.
All activity will automatically sync every 4 hours, starting from
the day you connected.
If you want to sync transactions from before the day that you connected, go to
Bank feed activity
from your
Connection settings
. You’ll see the option to choose an earlier start date — up to one year.
If you want to sync transactions that date further back than one year, you can manually upload your older Wise statements:
Download a statement
in PDF or CSV format for every currency you have activated on your Wise account.
Follow these instructions on how to
import statements to Xero
and how to
reconcile your accounts
.

title: How does my connection with QuickBooks work?
content:
If you haven't already,
Connect your QuickBooks Online account to your Wise account
. Once you’ve connected, you can manage the following from your
connection settings
:
Import bills to Wise
Mark bills as paid in QuickBooks
Advanced settings
QuickBooks also allows you to sync your
Wise Bank feeds
, but that part of the connection is managed by QuickBooks. Follow
these steps
to see a list of transactions for each of your Wise currency accounts within your QuickBooks Banking Transactions.
Import bills to Wise
Turn this on to see your unpaid bills in real time and pay them seamlessly from your
Bills page
.
We won't import any bills you mark as paid in QuickBooks. This means if you've paid a bill through another provider and haven’t marked it as paid in QuickBooks, it will still be visible in your Bills page on Wise.
Mark bills as paid in QuickBooks
Turn this on to automatically mark a bill paid in Quickbooks when you pay through your Bills page on Wise. Fees will be recorded as Bank Charges.
To mark bills as paid in QuickBooks, your QuickBooks plan must have the multicurrency feature turned on.
How to turn on multicurrency in QuickBooks
Advanced settings
Advanced settings aren't available for new customers. If you don’t see this feature under
connection settings
, it means we can’t provide it.
These settings provide additional customisation options, allowing you to:
Match bills and invoices:
Match bills marked as paid in Quickbooks using the recipient name, amount and reference.
Create expense entries for card transactions:
Create expense entries in Quickbooks for card transactions without attachments. If no match is found, we'll create a new expense entry.

title: Connect your Wise account with QuickBooks
content:
Note
, the name for your 'main account' in Wise can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
How to connect your Wise account with QuickBooks
There are two ways to connect your Wise account with Quickbooks — both of them provide different features.
1. To sync your bank feed, you need to initiate a connection from
Quickbooks
.
2. For bill reconciliation, you need to initiate the connection from
Wise
.
If you want to use both features simultaneously, you'll have to establish a separate connections for each.
Here's more details on what each connection does, and how to set them up:
Connecting from Quickbooks
Bank feeds syncing:
QuickBooks syncs and pulls transactions from your Wise currency accounts into QuickBooks Banking Transactions. You can view all connected Wise accounts directly in QuickBooks for easy tracking of transactions.
Follow these steps to connect:
Log into your QuickBooks account
Go to
Transactions
and select
Bank transactions
Select
Link Account
Search for Wise. You'll see multiple options for different regions.
Choose the one based on your registered business address. 

Choosing the wrong option may cause an error.  If that happens, reconnect and select the correct region based on your business address:
If your Wise account is registered in the EU, select
Wise (European Union)
If your Wise account is registered in the UK, select
Wise (UK)
For all other regions, select
Wise (UK).
You'll be redirected to log in to your Wise account
Choose the
business account
you want to connect to QuickBooks
Select the
balances
you want to sync
Choose the
date
you want QuickBooks to pull transactions from
Select
Connect
Connecting from Wise
Automatic reconciliation of your bills:
When you pay a bill through Wise, it will automatically be marked as paid in QuickBooks, and any fees will be recorded as Bank Charges. You can view all unpaid bills in real time from the Bills page in Wise and pay them directly. This helps to keep the records accurate, and reduces manual reconciliation.
Follow these steps to connect:
Log in to your Wise account
Click on your business name in the top corner
Under the Settings, go to
Connect and manage apps
Choose Quickbooks and follow the steps to connect
Once you pay a bill on Wise, we'll mark that bill in QuickBooks as paid, and it won't appear as an outstanding bill to pay any more. Fees will be added as an expense in your
Bank Charges
category.
To
automatically mark bills as paid in QuickBooks,
your QuickBooks plan must have the multi-currency feature turned on.
The
authorisation to share your data
with QuickBooks will expire after
90 days
. You’ll need to authorise sharing your data again at this point.
You won't be able to connect your Wise account if your subscription level with QuickBooks doesn't allow this connection.
To connect more than one balance,  you’ll need to enable
multi-currency
in QuickBooks.
How to turn on multicurrency in QuickBooks
.

title: Disconnect your Wise account from QuickBooks
content:
How to disconnect from QuickBooks
The process may vary depending on the features you use, and the connection type you have with QuickBooks.
If you want to disconnect from the bank feed sync, follow these steps:
Select your business name in the top corner
Under
Settings
, go to
Connect and manage apps
Choose
Disconnect
next to QuickBooks under data sharing
If you want to disconnect from the reconciliation of your bills:
Select your business name in the top corner
Under
Settings
, go to
Connect and manage apps
Choose
Manage
next to QuickBooks (Under Connected)
Choose
Disconnect
Important: if you use both features and want to disconnect from both, you need to follow the instructions for both of them.

title: How do I import and pay unpaid bills from QuickBooks with Wise
content:
You can connect your Wise account to QuickBooks to have the bills feature, giving you the option to import your bills directly to Wise so you have better visibility.
Learn how to connect your Wise account with QuickBooks
Import bills to Wise
You can switch on the bills feature to view your unpaid bills real-time on your Wise account. You'll then be able to pay them directly from your
Bills page
.
Switch on bills feature
From your Wise account, go on your business name in the top corner
In
Settings
, go on
Connect and manage apps
Select
Manage
next to the QuickBooks logo
Switch on
Import bills to Wise
We won't import any bills you mark as
paid
in QuickBooks.
If you have paid a bill through another provider, and have marked it as
paid
in QuickBooks, it won't appear on your
Bills
page in Wise.
Mark bills as 'paid' in QuickBooks
On the same page, you’ll also see an option to Automatically mark bills as paid in QuickBooks. If you switch this on, then when you pay a bill through your Bills page on Wise, we’ll automatically mark it as paid in QuickBooks. The fees will be recorded as "Bank Charges".
To pay unpaid bills:
Navigate to the
Bills tab
on your Wise account
If your bills are already synced, you'll see a list of unpaid bills from QuickBooks.  These will be synced automatically.
From the
Bills
tab, you can pay the bill as you would normally send money through Wise
Example bill payment
In this example, your bill ‘ABC456’ in the amount of €500 has been paid to ‘Vendor 1’ using the Bill pay feature on Wise, and you funded the bill with your Wise EUR.
Because you connected your Wise EUR to your QBO bank register named ‘Wise - EUR’, the bill payment transaction gets recorded in that register.
You’ll see a ‘Bill Payment (Check)’ in your Expenses with the amount.
And your ‘ABC456’ Bill will be marked as paid.
What if there’s a fee associated with my payment?
Any Wise fee will be entered automatically as a separate Expense transaction with the category ‘Wise bank charges’ and a Memo ‘Wise fee’.
Why am I seeing a clearing account? What is this?
A clearing account will be automatically created for one of these reasons:
If you choose an external payment method, we can't always retrieve information about the bank account funding the payment, for example a non-Wise card or bank payment. This applies for both incoming and outgoing payments.
QuickBooks requires all transactions that include a currency exchange to be converted to your Home currency to help you keep your books tidy.
For example, if your home currency is USD and you choose to fund a GBP bill payment with your EUR Wise account, QuickBooks still requires us to record the transaction in USD. This means we’ll record a EUR to USD Transfer in addition to a USD to GBP Expense in your ‘Wise clearing from USD’ Bank Register including a Memo describing this.
Learn more about QuickBooks home currency adjustments
.
In each of these instances, we’ll record the transaction in a ‘Wise clearing from [currency]’ or ‘Wise clearing to [currency]’ Bank Register depending on whether it was an incoming or outgoing conversion.

title: Why isn't my Xero bank account ready to connect?
content:
If you see the error message “
This bank account isn’t ready to connect with Wise
,” it means the Xero bank account you’ve chosen to connect a currency with hasn’t been configured correctly in Xero. Often, it’s because it’s already been set up to support another bank or platform.
You have three options:
Edit the chosen bank account
in Xero to make sure it’s configured correctly. Then try again. You can follow the instructions below.
Create a new Xero bank account
to connect to this currency. You can do this without leaving Wise. Go to
Currency settings
from your
Connection settings
then go to the currency you’d like to connect. Select an option from the dropdown or enter a new name. By entering a new name, you’ll create a new bank account in Xero that will be connected to your chosen currency in Wise. Click
Update
to save.
Choose another Xero bank account of the same currency.
Go to
Currency settings
from your
Connection settings
then go to the currency you'd like to connect. Select an option from the dropdown or enter a new name. By entering a new name, you'll create a new bank account in Xero that will be connected to your chosen currency in Wise. Click
Update
to save.
Editing your chosen Xero bank account
It’s easy to edit your Xero bank account so it can connect with Wise. You just need to make sure Wise is listed as the bank you’d like to use.
First, log into Xero and follow the below instructions to
edit your chosen bank account
In the
Accounting
menu, select
Bank Accounts.
Next to the bank account you want to connect to Wise, select
Manage Account
, then
Edit Account Details.
You will see a field that says
Your bank
. Delete the bank that is currently showing and search for ‘Wise’. Once it comes up as a suggested option, select this, and select
Save
.
Now go back to your Wise Business account
Home.
Select one of the currencies and select the overflow menu (
...
).
Select
Manage Accounting.
Try connecting the currency again to the edited Xero bank account.
If it doesn’t work,
get in touch with Xero
.

title: Will all my activity be synced when I connect my accounts?
content:
When you connect your Wise business account with an accounting integration, such as Xero or QuickBooks, you have the option to set a starting date for the transaction sync. This date can be up to one year (12 months) in the past for all connected currencies.
For example, if you connected your Wise account on the 1st of January, all transactions that appear on your activity starting from the 1st January will be synced.
Can I sync older transactions?
If you want to sync transactions that are older than a year, you can manually upload your older Wise statements:
Download a statement
in PDF or CSV format for every currency you've activated in your Wise account
Follow the instructions provided by your connected accounting software — like Xero or QuickBooks — to import the statements
Each platform has its own way of importing files, so make sure to follow their specific instructions.
For Xero users, there's an additional option to sync previous transactions. You can go to
Bank feed activity
from your
Connection settings
. On this page, you’ll see the option to choose an earlier start date — up to one year.

Connect your Wise account with QuickBooks

Connect and manage apps

The authorisation to share your data with accounting integrations will expire after 90-180 days. You’ll need to reauthorise sharing your data after the expiration due to regulations. For more information about open banking regulations:

Consumer Data Right (CDR)
Other Regions: Some countries have their own open banking regulations. Please refer to the relevant guidelines for your region.

title: Reconciling your Interest and Stocks with your account integration
content:
This guide is for Wise Business customers, explaining how to manage discrepancies in your accounting integration system — such as Xero and QuickBooks — caused by the returns from your use of Interest or Stocks.
How Accounting Integration works with Wise Assets
When you use Interest or Stocks, you purchase units in a fund. These units change in value over time. You don't receive returns as payments — instead, your
currency
or
Jar
is updated every trading day, based on the latest price of the units.
These changes in value don’t automatically appear in your accounting statements. This is because the accounting integration tool primarily tracks movements of money in and out of your account, such as deposits and withdrawals.
When the price of your units are updated, no money moves in or out of your account. This can create a mismatch between the figures in your Wise Account, and those in your accounting software.
How to reconcile the mismatch in the amount
The accounting integration doesn't automatically capture the changes in the price of your units in the fund. So at the end of each reporting period, you'll need to manually adjust your accounting records to reflect the change in the price of your units in the fund.
You'll need to create a manual ledger, which will reconcile the updated value of these units. This is similar to how you would report a revaluation of any other asset. Always consult with your accountant software provider if you're unsure how to do this.
Downloading a holdings statement
To find the information you need, you should download a
holdings statement
. This will tell you the number of units, and the value of those units, on the day you specify.
You can do this on the website:
Go on your profile at the top
Select
Statements and reports
Select
Holdings statement

title: What information will Wise access when I connect with an accounting software?
content:
When you connect your Wise account with
accounting softwares
, Wise will have access to:
Your organisation name
Your accounting integration bank account details
Wise
will not
have access to:
Your transactions outside of Wise
Any other financial data
Access based on connection type
Bank Feeds Sync:
If you enable only the Bank Feeds Sync, Wise won't access any sensitive information from the accounting software about your transactions or other financial data. Wise will only need your account information and details for each currency account you have activated.
Import Bills to Wise:
If you enable the Import Bills feature, Wise will access all bills you've created in Xero to facilitate payments through Wise. This means Wise will have access to that specific data.
Keep in mind that the
bills
feature is only available with
Xero
and
Quickbooks.

title: Connecting your accounting software via Open Banking
content:
Note
, the name for your 'main account' can vary based on your location. To find the exact terminology used in your region, please check the product directly.
Open Banking is a secure way to connect your Wise account with authorised apps, like accounting software. It allows you to share your financial data and initiate payments directly from these apps. It works using secure connections called application programming interfaces (APIs).
This gives you more control over your information. Instead of being locked by your bank, you choose which services can access your data.
Is it safe?
Yes. Open Banking operates under strict regulations, like PSD2 in Europe. This ensures your data is shared safely and only with your permission.
How does this work with my accounting software?
When you connect your Wise Business account to your accounting software, you’re giving it permission to view your transaction history, account balances, and account details.
This creates a secure link that automatically updates your books. You won't need to manually upload statements or CSV files anymore. Your transactions will appear in your accounting system shortly after they happen in Wise.
Why do I need to re-approve the connection every few months?
By law, your permission to share data expires after 90–180 days. You’ll need to re-authorise the connection to keep it active. This is a security feature to make sure you’re still in control of who sees your financial data.
For more information about open banking regulations:
UK customers:
The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)
European customers:
Payment Services Directive (PSD2)
Australian customers:
Consumer Data Right (CDR)
Other regions: Some countries have their own open banking regulations. Please refer to the relevant guidelines for your region.
What are Third-Party Providers (TPPs)?
Sometimes, connections are made using a specialised service called a Third-Party Provider (TPP). These companies, like Plaid or Yodlee, act as a secure go-between for Wise and your accounting app.
Because of this, you might see the TPP’s name (like Plaid) listed in your Wise account's connected apps, instead of the name of your accounting software.
How do I disconnect?
You can disconnect an app in two ways:
From your Wise account
Go to
Connect and manage apps
under
Settings
Find the app you want to remove and select
Disconnect
From the connected app
Go to the settings within your accounting app.
Follow their instructions to disconnect the bank feed from Wise.
Important:
If you've connected Wise with multiple apps through the same TPP (like Plaid), disconnecting it from your Wise account will remove all of them. To remove just one app, it's best to disconnect it from within that specific app's settings.
Connections will also expire automatically if you don't re-approve them in time. This ensures old or unused connections don't stay active.

title: Connect your Wise account with Exact Online
content:
You can connect your Wise Business account with Exact Online to automatically sync your currency transactions. This saves you time on manual data entry and helps keep your multi-currency bookkeeping up to date.
A separate bank account and journal will be created in Exact for every currency you choose to connect.
You must have your Wise currencies opened and connected to Exact Online for your statements to sync automatically.
How often does my data sync?
By default, all your Wise transaction data is synced with Exact Online every
4 hours
. You can also choose to manually sync your Wise data for a specific date range at any time.
The connection is securely facilitated by a service called
Chift
, which acts as an intermediary to ensure reliable, automated data transfer.
Which countries is the Exact Online integration available in?
The integration is currently available for businesses registered in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany.
How do I connect my Wise account with Exact Online?
Before you proceed, make sure you have the permissions on Exact Online to establish the connection:
Access journals
Access ledger accounts
Access bank entries
Alternatively, you can invite your bookkeeper or accountant to your business account as a team member. You control exactly what they can see and do — their access is limited to the permissions you choose. To connect to accounting software, you need to grant them the
Manage software integrations and tools
permission.
Learn how to add team members here
.
Follow these steps on the
Wise website
to set up your connection:
Log in to your Wise Business account.
Select your business name in the top corner.
Go to
Connect and manage apps
.
Select Exact Online and click
Connect
.
Select the company or division you'd like to use and click
Submit
. If you don't find the company/division you want to connect with, you need to adapt the allowed companies/divisions through the
Exact Online interface
Choose which currencies to connect.
You can create new bank accounts for them (we'll automatically set them up in Exact Online for each currency) or select existing ones.
You must set up a separate bank account in Exact Online for each currency account you hold in Wise (e.g., one for Wise EUR, one for Wise USD).
Confirm your setup.
What happens after I connect?
Once connected, transactions from your selected currencies will automatically sync with Exact Online and appear under the associated journals as entries.
What if I open a new currency account later?
If you activate a new currency (e.g. CAD) in your Wise account and would like to sync transactions of it to Exact Online, you should go to
connection settings
, select the Wise currency and create a new bank account for it.
How are fees and currency conversions handled?
Fees:
Wise transaction or conversion fees are imported as separate statement lines.
Currency Conversions:
When you convert money between your Wise currency accounts (e.g. EUR to USD), the transactions are recorded in a source currency Journal on Exact (if you converted from GBP to EUR, it will record the entry in GBP journal as a debit and conversion fee in EUR journal as a debit).
How do I disconnect my Wise account from Exact Online?
You can disconnect your account from Exact Online at any time:
Log in to your Wise Business account on the web.
Select your business name in the top corner.
Go to
Connect and manage apps
under Settings then select
All
.
Click
Manage
next to Exact Online.
Click
Disconnect
.
Tip:
You can also disconnect directly from the
connection settings
page.
The connection with Exact Online is powered by Chift that acts as an intermediary between Wise and Exact Online to ensure secure data synchronization.

title: Guide to batch payments
content:
Batch payments let you create and send multiple payments in one go. You need to upload one of our 3 batch templates and make a single pay-in to Wise to cover all payments in the batch.
Batch is the perfect solution for businesses that need to make a large number of payouts to their customers, freelancers, employees, investors or suppliers around the world. You can create up to 1,000 payments with a single .CSV or .XLSX file.
You can only set up a batch payment on the website. We suggest using Google Chrome.
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian business.
How do I make a batch payment?
You’ll need to set up and verify a Wise Business account first.
Learn how to get your business verified
Which batch templates are available?
We have 3 different file templates. You can send money to saved or new recipients if you know their bank account details. If not, you can send money using
only their email address.
Read more about batch templates
and
how to fill in our batch templates.
Once your Wise Business account has been verified, you can set up a batch by following these steps:
From
Payments
, select
Batch
Select and download a batch payment template file
Fill in your template file.
Learn how to fill your batch template
Save your file as a .CSV or .XLSX and upload it to the
batch payments page
Review the transfers and pay for your batch
Can I use third party platforms to create batch payments?
Yes — you can
integrate your Wise business account with our open API
. Our API helps businesses streamline their workflows. You can also connect your business tools to Wise and create other ways to manage your finances.
Learn about the Wise open API
Can other team members make batch payments on my Wise Business account?
Yes. Any team member with permission to set up or make payments via transfer can set up batch payments.
If their payments need approval, the batch payment will need to be approved by a team member with permission to approve payments before it can go through.
Read about assigning roles to your team
Is there anything else I need to know?
You can’t send batch payments to your Wise Contacts (other Wise accounts without account details to receive money). You can only send these payments to recipients added to your
Recipient list
.

title: What's a batch payment template?
content:
A batch template is a file you need to fill in with your recipients’ and transfer details. We’ve created
different templates
which you can download in .CSV or .XLSX file formats. You can then open and edit them using a spreadsheet app, such as Microsoft Excel or Numbers.
We have 3 types of templates:
Send by email
Send to saved recipients
Send to bank accounts
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian businesses.
Send by email
Use this template if you don’t know your recipients’ bank details. You need to add their email addresses to the template, along with the transfer amount and currency. 

Once you upload a template and pay for your batch, we’ll send each recipient a secure link to provide their bank details. They’ll have 1 week to enter their details before the link expires. If they don’t enter the details within a week, we’ll cancel and refund the payment.
Send to saved recipients
Use this template to send money to your saved recipients on Wise. We'll add their details automatically. The details will be filled in when you download the file. You can see all your recipients in the Recipients section
here
. 

Once you download the template, you can delete anyone from the file that you don’t want to include in the batch payment. You can’t send money to new recipients with this template.
Tip
: If you get a “Missing recipient address” error after uploading your batch file, make sure to fill in the address information manually. You’ll only need to do this once. After the batch is created, we’ll add this information to your recipient records.
Send to bank accounts
This template lets you manually add recipients’ bank and transfer details. You can send money to one or multiple currencies. All recipients you pay to will automatically be saved in your
Recipients list
.
Learn how to fill in your template.

title: How do I fill in my batch template?
content:
First, select and
download a template
. You’ll also find a template builder guide for each one to help you fill it in.
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian businesses.
Filling in the template fields
Each template has its own default columns. They’ll vary depending on the type and currencies you choose. The most important columns are:
Name
— the name of your recipient, as shown on their bank account
sourceCurrency
— the currency you use to pay for your batch
targetCurrency
— the currency your recipients will get
Amount
— the value amount of your transfer (numerical characters only)
amountCurrency
— defines your payment type:
Fixed Source Payment
— choose this to send an exact amount in the source currency. The fees will be deducted from your recipients
Fixed Target Payment
— chose this
to send an exact amount to your recipients in the target currency. The fees will be deducted from you, and your recipients will get the exact amount
For a
saved recipient template
, you'll also see these mandatory fields:
recipientId
— a unique reference to a pre-existing recipient in your Wise account. You can get this by downloading the
saved recipients template
recipientDetail
— a reference for customers to help identify the recipient. You don't need to edit this
recipientType:
Whether the recipient is a person or an institution (for example business or charity). This will often be pre-filled, but if it's blank — you should update it
recipientEmail
— any email you add for this recipient will be shown here, and you don't need to edit it
various address fields, such as address country code, address city, address firstline, address state, and address postcode. You only need to fill the address fields in certain scenarios, like when sending global Swift payments
Read more about sending exact amounts using batches
Send by email and Send to saved recipients
Log in to your Wise account and download the files from
here
. Then open the .CSV file with a spreadsheet app such as Microsoft Excel or Numbers and fill it out.
Send to bank accounts
You can pre-fill some information about recipients (for example, their currency, country and amount) in our
template builder
.
Each currency combination requires different information. Using the
template builder
, choose the target currencies and click “add row" for each combination. Then all relevant columns will be added to the batch file before you download it and fill in any missing fields manually.
Make sure you only fill in the fields starting with “e.g.”. “Email” and “Payment reference” are optional, so you can leave these blank. If you follow our template builder, you shouldn't make any mistakes.
Tips for every template
Don’t edit the names of the column headers — this will make your file unreadable. You can reorder columns if needed
Each row in the file spreadsheet shows one transfer — you can add up to 1,000 transfers per batch file
You can make batch payments to different currencies, but can only pay in one
Once you’ve filled in your template, just save it as a .CSV or .XLSX and
upload to your account
Team members that need approval to send transfers, can only send up to 100 transfers per batch

title: Why do I get an error when I upload my batch file?
content:
You may receive an error message after you uploaded your batch file. We'll show you a list of payments with the exact error message for each at the
Review Transfers
step.
You can download a copy of these errors to fix payments within the file later and continue with the rest of transfers without errors. Or, you can exit the batch upload page, fix the errors within the file and then upload it again.
Make sure to check every file you upload to see if:
the file contains more than one source currency — make sure you select only one. You’ll still be able to send multiple target currencies
some compulsory fields are left blank — you’ll need to fill in all of them. Follow the instructions for each template
here
If you get a “Missing recipient address” error after uploading your batch file, make sure to fill in the address information manually. You’ll only need to do this once. You’ll only need to do this once. After the batch is created , we’ll add this information to your recipient records
Here are solutions to the most common problems you might have uploading your batch file.
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian businesses.
Incorrect file format or size
Make sure you upload your batch file in either .CSV or .XLSX format. The file size can’t be larger than 5MB. You also can’t add more than 1,000 transfers or recipients in your batch file.
Wrong formatting or language
Make sure you haven’t changed headers filling in your template
Don’t use any special characters or punctuation — for example, commas in the
Amount
column
Check if you don’t exceed the maximum length of your payment reference
Cyrillic or Katakana characters in the file
Depending on the currency, you’ll need to write the recipient’s name in Katakana or Cyrillic. The letters may change to unknown characters because of format settings. To fix this, you’ll need to reopen your file and change the encoding setting to 65001: Unicode (UTF -8).
Your account isn’t verified
You need to verify your account, before you can use batch payments.
Read about business verification.
If you still get error messages on your new file, make sure you’re using our
template builder
. This is especially important when you're using the Send to bank accounts template.
Read more about filling in your batch template.

title: How do I pay for my batch?
content:
You can pay for your batch by
local bank transfer
or from
your Wise account
.
You won't be able to pay with other payment methods such as a connected bank account, card or
international wire (Swift)
.
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian businesses.
Paying from your account
You can pay for your batch from one of your Wise Business account. Just choose
Your account
as the payment method on the final step.
Learn how to send money from your account.
Paying by bank transfer
At the final payment step, we’ll give you:
the full amount you need to send to us
our bank details
the payment reference you should use
Once you’ve sent your bank transfer, mark your batch as paid by clicking
I have paid
. When we receive the money, we’ll pay out the correct amounts to your recipients.
Make sure to use your unique batch payment reference (starts with a letter B) when you send money by bank transfer. It’ll help us complete your transfer faster.
Batch payments set up through Xero
Make sure to send the exact amount and use the correct payment reference (B-ref) if you created a batch payment through
Xero
and you're paying via bank transfer. Otherwise, your money will be refunded automatically.
What are the fees?
There's a fee for each transfer — we’ll sum them up for all the payments in your batch and show you the total fee.
Check our pricing page
.
If we’ve offered a Guaranteed Rate (GR) on any of the payments in the batch, make sure you send us the money before it expires. If we don’t get your funds on time, your payments will be sent out automatically at the live exchange rate. We'll deduct the additional fees from the amount we send out to the recipient.
Read more about a guaranteed rate
and
sending exact amounts using batch payments
.
How to find our bank details to make a payment
If you marked your batch as
Paid
and need our bank details again:
On
Home
, click on your batch payment
Click on
Review batch
Select any transfer from your batch
On the right hand side, click on the 3 dots
Tap
View transfer details
Click on
Save as PDF
— you can use these bank details to send the money to us
If you haven’t marked your batch as
Paid
:
On the home page, view your batch in
Tasks
Select
Review
Select
Pay for this batch
Follow the instructions to choose the payment method.

title: How do I send exact amounts with batch payments?
content:
To make sure your recipient gets the full amount in their currency, follow the steps below.
Batch payments are currently not available for Brazilian businesses.
Send to bank accounts template builder
On the template builder screen, click on the
Amount currency
field and select
Target currency
. You’ll see the field
Transfer amount
is then updated to the recipient’s currency
Enter the amount you’d like for the recipient to get — we’ll show how much you’d need to send us in your currency
Keep in mind:
if you select “source” as the “amountCurrency”, we’ll deduct the fees from your payment and send the rest to your recipient in their currency.
Send by email and Send to saved recipients templates
Type the “target” under the “amountCurrency” column.
How do you calculate the fees?
When you make a batch payment, we charge fees for each transfer. These fees are the same as for individual transfers you make.
You can choose between two options:
Fixed Source Payment
— choose this to send an exact amount in the source currency. The fees will be deducted from your recipients
Fixed Target Payment
— chose this
to send an exact amount to your recipients in the target currency. The fees will be deducted from you, and your recipients will get the exact amount
If we’ve offered a Guaranteed Rate (GR) on any of the payments within the batch, make sure you send us the money before it expires. If we don’t get your funds on time, your payments will be sent out automatically at the live exchange rate. We'll deduct the additional fees from the amount we send out to the recipient.
Read more about how to pay for your batch.

title: What's the Wise API?
content:
Here’s some information about our Wise API.
API helps you connect your business with Wise to automate your interaction with us. With API you won’t even need to log in to Wise to set up transfers - instead, all this and more can be automated. This allows you to significantly improve your (and your recipients') experience. Also, our API is free to use.
Banks like N26, Monzo, LHV, Aspire, Shinhan (along with 20+ other banks in Americas, Europe and APAC) and businesses such as Xero, GoCardless, Deel, Remote, Emburse, Preply, Gameflip, Bolt and many more are using our API to streamline their cross-border payments.
Personal customers are also using Wise API for their day-to-day transfers.
What can Wise API do?
With Wise API you can:
Power your cross-border / domestic payouts as well as manage multiple business entities with a single integration
Pay out directly to bank accounts or
email recipients
Monitor the status of your transfers live
Get statements for reconciliation and accounting purposes
Always get the
mid-market exchange
and
our low cost transparent fees
Monitor payments received to your
Wise local account details
Upload Batch Payments over API —
learn how to automate batch payments
What Wise API cannot do:
Collect debit/credit card payments (check-out flow)
Create reusable plug-ins for websites
How can I get started with the Wise API?
We have information-rich documentation on our API
here
. It includes guidelines for different use cases as well as
Getting started
guide that will help your development team with implementation.
Before you involve your development team in the integration, make sure to register for the account on Wise and get verified first. There are many use cases that we support but make sure to check our Acceptable Use Policy page under our
Legal
section to make sure we can work together.
Get in touch
with our Sales team if you're a medium-large business.
